title: Improve agent container management
description: A lot of agent containers are being left running and not cleaned stopped even after an agent has been stopped. Show a list of the running agent workers in a new tab on the projects page so that individual agents can be killed. In this table of agents, show which issue and PR the agent was started on, as well as the last time that a prompt was sent to the agent. Group these by issue such it is clear which agent containers have been superseded and can be killed immediately. Also ensure that when the 'stop agent' button is clicked in a session that **all** related agent worker containers are killed. This should be for both Azure Container Apps and Docker container execution modes.

title: Fix version numbering
description: The top bar of the web app always shows the same version (0.1.0) regardless of the release version of the container that is currently being used. Ensure that in the container build step in the GitHub actions that the version of the container is passed in to the application such that the correct version of the container is displayed in the top bar of the application.

title: Project links on left hand side don't work
description:
The project links in the left side bar don't work - clicking them changes the address in the address bar but the page does not navigate to the new page. Test this on a mock instance of the app started with ./scripts/mock.sh and use the Playwright tools to investigate. The indentation is also not correct, it should be pushed further to the right so it is past the other links (which have some padding due to the presence of icons.

Add an icon for "Sessions", something like a chat bubble would work.

title: Fix colours for tool use and settings page
description:
The outline colour for tool use in the chat sessions doesn't show up in dark mode because it is the same colour as the background. Make it lighter so it provides a distinct border around the tool use chat windows. The same applies for most text input and combo boxes, and the borders around tool windows. Ensure Tailwind and reusable components are being used throughout to ensure UI consistency and DRY principles.

Some backgrounds on the settings page are also white in dark mode, please fix this. A previous similar fix was required on the branches page - this is now being shown correctly.

title: Show plan ready status in status indicators at top of page
description: Currently the agent status indicators in the top bar of the page don't show agents that are currently in "Plan Ready" status - the agent is not included in the number either. They should be shown as agents with an orange indicator, similar to agents in a "question" status. Update the status display handler to fail gracefully. If the status is unknown, show a question mark and log this as an error.

title: Show limited tool usage
description: When a tool execution group has more than 2 tool calls, only show the most recent 2 by default. Add a subtle +/- toggle button to the group header to expand/collapse the full list.

title: Blazor Server interactive mode not hydrating in mock environment
description:
When running the app via ./scripts/mock.sh, Blazor Server interactive rendering does not hydrate the page. All @onclick directives throughout the session page (Session.razor) are rendered as literal HTML attributes (e.g. @onclick="ToggleShowAll") instead of being processed by Blazor into event handlers (blazor:onclick). The WebSocket connection to /_blazor is established and Blazor's JS runtime loads, but zero <!--bl:--> component markers appear in the DOM, indicating components are never upgraded from static SSR to interactive mode.

This does NOT affect:
- Native HTML <details>/<summary> expand/collapse (used by ToolResultRenderer and thinking blocks), which work purely via browser functionality
- Any server-side rendering — the page renders correctly with all content visible

This DOES affect:
- The new +/- toggle button in ToolExecutionGroupDisplay (PR #193) — clicking does nothing
- All other @onclick handlers in Session.razor (StopSession, ClearContext, ExecutePlan, etc.)
- Sending messages via the chat input (relies on Blazor form submission)

Configuration appears correct:
- Routes.razor has @rendermode InteractiveServer (line 1)
- Session.razor has @rendermode InteractiveServer (line 19)  
- Program.cs calls .AddRazorComponents().AddInteractiveServerComponents() and .MapRazorComponents<App>().AddInteractiveServerRenderMode()
- App.razor renders <Routes /> without an explicit @rendermode attribute — this may be the root cause, as .NET 8 Blazor Web Apps may require the render mode to be specified at the call site in App.razor

Discovered during manual UI testing of PR #193 (Show limited tool usage). The PR's implementation is correct — this is a pre-existing environment issue.

title: Optimize Docker build times with shared base image
description:
Both Docker images (Dockerfile and src/Homespun.AgentWorker/Dockerfile) install nearly identical runtime tooling independently - Node.js, gh CLI, Claude Code, Playwright MCP + Chromium, Fleece CLI, Docker CLI. These npm global installs (especially Claude Code with node-pty native compilation, and Playwright MCP + Chromium at ~1GB) are the primary bottleneck. Every code change rebuilds all of this from scratch. The CI/CD pipeline (cd.yml) also has zero caching configured.

Approach: Shared Base Image + CI Caching

1. Create Dockerfile.base - shared tooling image with all common runtime tools (.NET 10 SDK, Node.js, gh CLI, Claude Code, Playwright MCP + Chromium, Fleece CLI, Docker CLI). Use BuildKit cache mounts on npm install steps.

2. Modify Dockerfile - change runtime stage FROM to use base image. Keep only main-app-specific items (Tailscale, user setup, published app copy).

3. Modify src/Homespun.AgentWorker/Dockerfile - same pattern, keep only worker-specific items.

4. Update scripts/run.sh - build base image first before app builds. Docker layer cache makes this instant when base unchanged.

5. Update scripts/run.ps1 - same change for PowerShell.

6. Update .github/workflows/cd.yml - add BuildKit (setup-buildx-action), add build-base-image job, use registry cache (cache-from/cache-to), upgrade build-push-action to v6.

Files to modify: Dockerfile.base (new), Dockerfile, src/Homespun.AgentWorker/Dockerfile, scripts/run.sh, scripts/run.ps1, .github/workflows/cd.yml

Expected impact: Local code-only rebuild ~2-5 min (from ~15-20 min). CI builds with cache hit ~5-8 min (from ~20-30 min). Disk savings ~2.5GB shared vs ~5.5GB duplicated.

title: AskUserQuestion tool fails in local agent execution mode - CLI handles it internally via stdin
description: When Claude calls AskUserQuestion in a session using LocalAgentExecutionService, the CLI handles it as an internal stdin-based tool rather than delegating through the streaming protocol. The CLI prompts 'Answer questions?' on stdin, times out after ~19 seconds with no response, and returns an error tool result. The TCS-based interception in LocalAgentExecutionService (lines 264-277) never fires because either TryParseAskUserQuestion returns null or the CLI handles the tool before content_block_stop arrives. SendToolResultAsync cannot work because the CLI doesn't give the caller a chance to provide the tool result. Need to investigate how to configure the CLI to delegate AskUserQuestion to the caller in streaming mode, or use a different mechanism to intercept and answer questions.

title: Side panel and tool expansion doesn't work
description: When loading the session page, an unhandled exception occurs in the side panel (Issue/PR/To-do's/Files area): 'InvalidCharacterError: Failed to execute setAttribute on Element: @onclick is not a valid attribute name.' This crashes the Blazor Server circuit, breaking all interactive functionality on the page. The error occurs consistently on every session page load. The @onclick directive is being rendered as a literal HTML attribute instead of being processed by Blazor's render tree, likely in a component that renders raw HTML or uses MarkupString incorrectly.

title: Update Claude CLI flags
description: Currently the default CLI flags for Claude Code include --system-prompt. Change this to --append-system-prompt to ensure that the default Claude Code system prompts are being used.

title: Sync button should reload all issues from disk
description: Occasionally the app will keep an old state of fleece issues, particularly when the issues on disk change due to an agent action or pushes from another branch. The sync button on the agents page should always fully reload the fleece issues from disk (merging if necessary) to fully reload the issues state.

title: Concurrent agent startup causes crash
description: Starting up concurrent agents causes the following exception to be thrown: {"TimeStamp": "2026-02-08T03:46:35.8144258+00:00", "Log": "F warn: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Server.Circuits.RemoteRenderer[100]"}

title: Remove worktree references
description: Remove the use of 'worktree' and just refer to branches, as the worktrees have been switch to full clones of the repository in PR #189

title: Starting another agent mid-start causes exception
Issue syncing often fails
Split into Blazor WASM and ASP.NET server
description:
There have been ongoing issues with managing the complexity of interactivity with this Blazor SSR Interactive application - a better result will be achieved using Blazor WASM for a front-end and a standard minimal API ASP.NET server application. This will also allow more careful separation of backend and frontend concerns than has currently been done.

The plan is to split the current monolithic Homespun Blazor Server application into:
1. **Homespun.Shared** - Shared DTOs, models, interfaces, and constants used by both client and server
2. **Homespun.Server** - ASP.NET minimal API server with all backend services, SignalR hubs, and data access
3. **Homespun.Client** - Blazor WASM standalone client consuming the server API via HTTP and SignalR

Key principles:
- Keep DTOs and shared contracts in Homespun.Shared (referenced by both projects)
- Server owns all business logic, data access, external integrations (GitHub, Claude SDK, Git, Fleece)
- Client is a pure UI layer using HttpClient and SignalR HubConnection to communicate with server
- Server serves the WASM client as static files
- Existing Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk (C# class library) and Homespun.Worker (TypeScript/Hono agent worker) projects remain unchanged

This parent issue contains child tasks that should be executed in series.

title: Create Homespun.Shared project with shared DTOs and contracts
description:
Create a new class library project `Homespun.Shared` that will contain all types shared between the Blazor WASM client and the ASP.NET server. This is the foundation that both projects will reference.

## What to create

### Project setup
- Create `src/Homespun.Shared/Homespun.Shared.csproj` targeting `net10.0` as a class library
- Add to `Homespun.sln` under the `src` solution folder
- Add NuGet reference to `Fleece.Core` (1.0.0) since Issue types from this library are used in DTOs

### Models/DTOs to move into Homespun.Shared

**From Features/ClaudeCode/Data/ (session models):**
- `ClaudeSession` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions.ClaudeSession`
- `ClaudeMessage` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions.ClaudeMessage`
- `ClaudeMessageContent` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions.ClaudeMessageContent`
- `ClaudeSessionStatus` enum
- `SessionMode` enum
- `ClaudeContentType` enum
- `ClaudeMessageRole` enum
- `PendingQuestion`, `SessionTodoItem`, `TodoStatus`
- `ToolResultData` and all subtypes (ReadToolData, WriteToolData, BashToolData, etc.)
- `SessionSummary` (returned by API)
- `SessionCacheSummary`, `ResumableSession`, `RunningSessionInfo`

**From Features/PullRequests/Data/Entities/ (domain entities used as DTOs):**
- `Project` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Projects.Project`
- `PullRequest` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.PullRequests.PullRequest`
- `PullRequestInfo` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.PullRequests.PullRequestInfo`
- `OpenPullRequestStatus`, `PullRequestStatus` enums
- `PullRequestWithStatus`, `PullRequestWithTime` records

**From Features/Git/ (git models):**
- `WorktreeInfo` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Git.WorktreeInfo`
- `WorktreeStatus` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Git.WorktreeStatus`
- `BranchInfo` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Git.BranchInfo`
- `FileChangeInfo`

**From Features/Gitgraph/ (graph visualization models):**
- `IGraphNode` interface and all implementations (IssueNode, PullRequestNode, SectionDividerNode)
- `Graph`, `GraphBranch`, `GraphNodeType`, `GraphNodeStatus` enums
- `TimelineLaneLayout`

**From Features/Notifications/:**
- `NotificationDto` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Notifications.NotificationDto`
- `NotificationType` enum

**From Features/GitHub/:**
- `IssuePullRequestStatus`
- `GitHubAuthStatus`, `GitHubAuthMethod` enum

**API Request/Response DTOs (from controllers):**
- `CreateSessionRequest`, `SendMessageRequest`
- `CreateProjectRequest`, `UpdateProjectRequest`, `CreateProjectResult`
- `CreateIssueRequest`, `UpdateIssueRequest`
- `CreatePullRequestRequest`, `UpdatePullRequestRequest`
- `CreateWorktreeRequest`, `CreateWorktreeResponse`, `WorktreeExistsResponse`
- `CreateNotificationRequest`
- `GenerateBranchIdRequest`, `GenerateBranchIdResponse`
- `SyncResult`

**From Features/Fleece/:**
- `FleeceIssueSyncResult`, `PullResult`, `BranchStatusResult`

### SignalR hub contracts
- Create `Homespun.Shared.Hubs.IClaudeCodeHubClient` interface defining all server-to-client SignalR messages (BroadcastSessionStarted, BroadcastMessageReceived, BroadcastStreamingContentDelta, etc.)
- Create `Homespun.Shared.Hubs.IClaudeCodeHub` interface defining all client-to-server SignalR messages (JoinSession, SendMessage, StopSession, etc.)
- Create `Homespun.Shared.Hubs.HubConstants` class with hub URL constants ("/hubs/notifications", "/hubs/claudecode")

### Constants
- API route constants (e.g., `ApiRoutes.Sessions`, `ApiRoutes.Projects`, etc.)

## Key principles
- Only move types that need to be referenced by BOTH client and server
- Types that are internal to the server (e.g., service implementations, EF Core entities if any) stay in the server project
- Keep the `Notification` type (with `Action` callback) server-side; only `NotificationDto` goes to Shared
- The `Homespun` project should still compile after this - update its references to point to the Shared project via a ProjectReference
- Update all `using` statements in the monolith to reference the new namespaces

## Verification
- Solution builds with no errors
- All existing tests pass
- No functionality changes - this is purely a code organization refactor

title: Create Homespun.Server ASP.NET minimal API project
description:
Create the new `Homespun.Server` project that will be the ASP.NET backend, hosting all server-side logic, API endpoints, and SignalR hubs. This project replaces the current `Homespun` web project's backend role.

## What to create

### Project setup
- Create `src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj` as a `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web` project targeting `net10.0`
- Add to `Homespun.sln` under the `src` solution folder
- Add ProjectReference to `Homespun.Shared` and `Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk`
- Add NuGet references: `Fleece.Core`, `Markdig`, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client`, `Microsoft.Data.Sqlite`, `Octokit`, `Swashbuckle.AspNetCore`

### What goes in this project

**Program.cs** - New minimal API startup:
- All DI service registration (from current Program.cs lines 21-260)
- Configure CORS to allow the Blazor WASM client origin
- Map all API controllers
- Map SignalR hubs (`/hubs/notifications`, `/hubs/claudecode`)
- Map health check endpoint
- Serve the Blazor WASM client as static files (via `app.UseBlazorFrameworkFiles()` and `app.MapFallbackToFile("index.html")`)
- Swagger/OpenAPI configuration
- Data protection configuration
- Mock mode support

**Controllers** - Move all 7+ API controllers as-is:
- `SessionsController`
- `ProjectsController`
- `PullRequestsController`
- `WorktreesController`
- `IssuesController`
- `NotificationsController`
- `OrchestrationController`

**SignalR Hubs** - Move both hubs:
- `ClaudeCodeHub` → Implement `IClaudeCodeHubClient` from Shared
- `NotificationHub`

**All Feature Services** - Move complete Features/ directory structure:
- ClaudeCode services (ClaudeSessionService, AgentExecutionService, etc.)
- Git services (GitWorktreeService, MergeStatusCacheService, etc.)
- GitHub services (GitHubService, GitHubClientWrapper, etc.)
- Fleece services (FleeceService, FleeceIssueTransitionService, etc.)
- Gitgraph services (GraphService, GraphCacheService, etc.)
- Projects services (ProjectService)
- PullRequests services (PullRequestWorkflowService, PullRequestDataService)
- Notifications services (NotificationService)
- AgentOrchestration services (MiniPromptService, BranchIdGeneratorService)
- Commands (CommandRunner)
- Testing/Mock services
- Shared services (MarkdownRenderingService)

**Data layer** - Move all data access:
- `JsonDataStore` and `IDataStore`
- `MessageCacheStore`, `SessionMetadataStore`, `ClaudeSessionStore`
- All data file I/O

**Configuration** - Move:
- `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Mock.json`
- `launchSettings.json`
- `start.sh` (container entrypoint)

## Agent execution and Homespun.Worker integration
- Agent execution services in the server orchestrate work by communicating with the **Homespun.Worker** (TypeScript/Hono) containers via HTTP
- The server does NOT run Claude Code sessions directly — it delegates to Homespun.Worker containers that use the official `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` TypeScript SDK
- `DockerAgentExecutionService` spawns Homespun.Worker containers and communicates via HTTP/SSE
- `AzureContainerAppsAgentExecutionService` manages Homespun.Worker instances on Azure
- The `Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk` C# library provides the SDK client interface used by the server to interact with Workers

## Key decisions
- The server project does NOT contain Blazor components or Razor pages
- No `AddRazorComponents()` call - this is a pure API server
- CORS must be configured to allow the WASM client to make requests
- The server hosts the WASM client's files via `UseBlazorFrameworkFiles()`/`MapFallbackToFile`
- Service interfaces that the client needs (for strongly-typed HTTP clients) should reference types from Homespun.Shared only
- No reference to Homespun.Worker is needed in the .csproj — communication is over HTTP at runtime

## Verification
- Project compiles independently
- All API endpoints are accessible via Swagger
- SignalR hubs accept connections
- Health check endpoint works

title: Create Homespun.Client Blazor WASM project
description:
Create the new `Homespun.Client` Blazor WebAssembly standalone project that will serve as the frontend UI. This project replaces the current Blazor Server interactive components. ## What to create ### Project setup - Create `src/Homespun.Client/Homespun.Client.csproj` using `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.BlazorWebAssembly` - Target `net10.0` - Add to `Homespun.sln` under the `src` solution folder - Add ProjectReference to `Homespun.Shared` - Add NuGet references: - `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly` - `Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client` (for SignalR connections from WASM) - `Markdig` (if markdown rendering happens client-side, otherwise call server API) ### Project structure ``` src/Homespun.Client/ ├── Program.cs # WASM entry point, DI setup ├── wwwroot/ │ ├── index.html # HTML host page (replaces App.razor) │ ├── css/ │ │ ├── tailwind.css # Tailwind input │ │ └── tailwind.min.css # Built Tailwind CSS │ ├── app.css # Custom styles │ └── favicon.png ├── Layout/ │ ├── MainLayout.razor # Main layout │ └── NavMenu.razor # Navigation ├── Pages/ # All page components │ ├── Home.razor │ ├── Projects.razor │ ├── ProjectDetail.razor │ ├── ProjectCreate.razor │ ├── ProjectEdit.razor │ ├── FeatureCreate.razor │ ├── IssueEdit.razor │ ├── SessionManagement.razor │ ├── AgentPrompts.razor │ ├── Settings.razor │ └── ... ├── Components/ # Shared components │ ├── ChatInput.razor │ ├── ChatMessage.razor │ ├── Modal.razor │ ├── LoadingSpinner.razor │ └── ... (all 50+ shared components) ├── Services/ # Client-side service implementations │ ├── HttpProjectService.cs # IProjectService via HttpClient │ ├── HttpSessionService.cs # Session operations via HttpClient │ ├── HttpIssueService.cs # Issue operations via HttpClient │ ├── SignalRSessionService.cs # Real-time session updates via SignalR │ └── ... ├── _Imports.razor # Global using statements ├── App.razor # Root router component ├── package.json # Tailwind CSS build └── tailwind.config.js ``` ### Program.cs entry point ```csharp var builder = WebAssemblyHostBuilder.CreateDefault(args); builder.RootComponents.Add("#app"); // Configure HttpClient to point to server API builder.Services.AddScoped(sp => new HttpClient { BaseAddress = new Uri(builder.HostEnvironment.BaseAddress) }); // Register client-side services (HTTP-backed implementations) builder.Services.AddScoped(); // ... more service registrations // SignalR connections builder.Services.AddScoped(); await builder.Build().RunAsync(); ``` ### Key differences from current Blazor Server 1. **No direct service access** - Components cannot inject server-side services directly. They must use HttpClient or SignalR 2. **No `@rendermode InteractiveServer`** - Remove all rendermode directives; WASM is the default 3. **HttpClient for data fetching** - Replace direct service calls with HTTP API calls 4. **SignalR client connections** - Use `HubConnectionBuilder` from the client instead of being part of the hub server 5. **No server-side state** - All UI state lives in the browser; use local storage or session storage if needed 6. **Tailwind CSS build** - Move the npm/tailwind build into this project since it's the UI project ### wwwroot/index.html Create the HTML host page (replaces App.razor server rendering): ```html Homespun
Loading...
``` ## Verification - Project compiles with `dotnet build` - `dotnet run` on the Server project serves the WASM client - Basic routing works (navigating between pages) - Initial page loads show the layout with nav menu

title: Migrate backend services from monolith to Homespun.Server
description:
Move all backend service implementations from the current `Homespun` monolith project into `Homespun.Server`. After this task, the monolith's Features/ directory and backend logic should live entirely in the server project.

## Services to migrate (by feature)

### ClaudeCode Feature
- `ClaudeSessionService` / `IClaudeSessionService`
- `ClaudeSessionStore` / `IClaudeSessionStore`
- `ClaudeSessionDiscovery` / `IClaudeSessionDiscovery`
- `SessionMetadataStore` / `ISessionMetadataStore`
- `MessageCacheStore` / `IMessageCacheStore`
- `SessionOptionsFactory`
- `ToolResultParser` / `IToolResultParser`
- `HooksService` / `IHooksService`
- `AgentStartupTracker` / `IAgentStartupTracker`
- `AgentPromptService` / `IAgentPromptService`
- `TodoParser` / `ITodoParser`
- Agent execution services: `DockerAgentExecutionService`, `AzureContainerAppsAgentExecutionService`, `LocalAgentExecutionService`, `IAgentExecutionService`

**Note:** Agent execution services communicate with **Homespun.Worker** (TypeScript/Hono) containers over HTTP/SSE. The server orchestrates agent sessions by spawning Worker containers and polling their REST API. The `Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk` C# library provides the client interface for this communication.

### Git Feature
- `GitWorktreeService` / `IGitWorktreeService`
- `MergeStatusCacheService` / `IMergeStatusCacheService`
- `RebaseAgentService` / `IRebaseAgentService`

### GitHub Feature
- `GitHubService` / `IGitHubService`
- `GitHubClientWrapper` / `IGitHubClientWrapper`
- `GitHubEnvironmentService` / `IGitHubEnvironmentService`
- `IssuePrLinkingService` / `IIssuePrLinkingService`
- `IssuePrStatusService` / `IIssuePrStatusService`
- `GitHubSyncPollingService` (hosted service)

### Projects Feature
- `ProjectService` / `IProjectService`

### PullRequests Feature
- `PullRequestWorkflowService`
- `PullRequestDataService`
- `FeatureService`

### Data layer
- `JsonDataStore` / `IDataStore`

### Fleece Feature
- `FleeceService` / `IFleeceService`
- `FleeceIssueTransitionService` / `IFleeceIssueTransitionService`
- `FleeceIssuesSyncService` / `IFleeceIssuesSyncService`
- `IssueSerializationQueueService` / `IIssueSerializationQueue`

### Gitgraph Feature
- `GraphService` / `IGraphService`
- `GraphCacheService` / `IGraphCacheService`
- `GraphBuilder`, `TimelineSvgRenderer`, `TimelineLaneCalculator`

### Notifications Feature
- `NotificationService` / `INotificationService`

### Navigation Feature
- `BreadcrumbService` / `IBreadcrumbService` (NOTE: this may become client-only)

### AgentOrchestration Feature
- `MiniPromptService` / `IMiniPromptService`
- `BranchIdGeneratorService` / `IBranchIdGeneratorService`

### Commands Feature
- `CommandRunner` / `ICommandRunner`

### Shared Feature
- `MarkdownRenderingService` / `IMarkdownRenderingService`

### SignalR Feature
- `SignalRUrlProvider` / `ISignalRUrlProvider`
- `SignalROptions`

### Testing Feature
- `MockDataStore`, `MockClaudeSessionService`, `MockDataSeederService`
- `JsonlSessionLoader`
- All mock service implementations

## Migration approach
1. Copy the entire Features/ directory to Homespun.Server/Features/
2. Update namespaces from `Homespun.Features.*` to `Homespun.Server.Features.*`
3. Update service interfaces that reference shared types to use `Homespun.Shared.*` namespaces
4. Keep service interfaces that are server-internal (not needed by client) in the Server project
5. Move `Program.cs` DI registration code to the server

## Key decisions
- Service interfaces that the client needs to know about (for HTTP client wrappers) remain in Shared as marker interfaces or are defined fresh there
- Server-only interfaces (ICommandRunner, IClaudeSessionDiscovery, etc.) stay in Server
- The `IBreadcrumbService` may need to be reimplemented client-side since it tracks navigation state
- Agent execution services remain in the server — they orchestrate Homespun.Worker containers but do not need a .csproj reference to Homespun.Worker (communication is HTTP-based at runtime)

## Verification
- Server project builds
- All API endpoints work
- SignalR connections work
- Mock mode still functions
- Existing unit tests can be pointed at the new project and pass

title: Create client-side HTTP service layer for Blazor WASM
Migrate Blazor components to WASM client and rewire to HTTP services
description:
Move all Blazor components (pages, shared components, layout) from the monolith into `Homespun.Client` and update them to use the HTTP client services instead of direct server-side service injection.

## Components to migrate

### Layout Components
- `MainLayout.razor` → `Homespun.Client/Layout/MainLayout.razor`
- `NavMenu.razor` → `Homespun.Client/Layout/NavMenu.razor`
- Remove `ReconnectModal.razor` (SignalR reconnect is not needed in WASM the same way)

### Page Components (12 pages)
Move all from `Components/Pages/` to `Homespun.Client/Pages/`:
- `Home.razor` - Dashboard
- `Projects.razor` - Project list
- `ProjectDetail.razor` - Project detail with sessions, issues, PRs
- `ProjectCreate.razor` - Create new project
- `ProjectEdit.razor` - Edit project settings
- `FeatureCreate.razor` - Create feature/PR
- `IssueEdit.razor` - Edit issue details
- `SessionManagement.razor` - View/manage Claude sessions
- `AgentPrompts.razor` - Manage agent prompts
- `Settings.razor` - Application settings
- `Error.razor` - Error page
- `NotFound.razor` - 404 page

### Shared Components (50+)
Move all from `Components/Shared/` to `Homespun.Client/Components/`:

**Chat UI components:**
- `ChatInput.razor`, `ChatMessage.razor`, `ProcessingIndicator.razor`
- `TextBlock.razor`, `ThinkingBlock.razor`, `ToolUseBlock.razor`, `ToolResultBlock.razor`
- `ContentBlock.razor`

**Panel components:**
- `AgentControlPanel.razor`, `AgentManagementPanel.razor`, `AgentStatusPanel.razor`
- `WorktreeManagementPanel.razor`, `CurrentPullRequestDetailPanel.razor`
- `IssueDetailPanel.razor`

**Utility components:**
- `Modal.razor`, `ConfirmModal.razor`, `LoadingSpinner.razor`
- `NotificationBanner.razor`, `FormMessage.razor`, `AsyncActionButton.razor`

**Session history components:**
- `SessionHistoryList.razor`, `SessionCacheHistory.razor`

### Design showcase (optional - lower priority)
- `Design/DesignShowcase.razor` and all showcase components
- Can be migrated later or dropped

## Key changes per component

### Remove render mode directives
Every component currently has `@rendermode InteractiveServer`. Remove ALL of these - WASM components don't use rendermode.

### Replace service injection
Before (Blazor Server - direct service injection):
```razor
@inject IProjectService ProjectService
@code {
    var projects = await ProjectService.GetAllAsync();
}
```

After (Blazor WASM - HTTP service):
```razor
@inject HttpProjectApiService ProjectApi
@code {
    var projects = await ProjectApi.GetAllProjectsAsync();
}
```

### Common patterns to change:
1. **`@inject IProjectService`** → `@inject HttpProjectApiService`
2. **`@inject IClaudeSessionService`** → `@inject HttpSessionApiService` (for REST calls) + `@inject SignalRSessionService` (for real-time)
3. **`@inject IGitWorktreeService`** → `@inject HttpWorktreeApiService`
4. **`@inject IFleeceService`** → `@inject HttpIssueApiService`
5. **`@inject IGitHubService`** → HTTP service or data passed via API
6. **`@inject INotificationService`** → `@inject HttpNotificationApiService` + SignalR for real-time
7. **`@inject NavigationManager`** → Keep as-is (works in WASM)
8. **`@inject IGraphService`** → `@inject HttpGraphApiService`

### Handle services that don't have API equivalents
Some services currently injected by components may not have corresponding API endpoints. For each:
- If the data is needed: Add a new API endpoint on the server and a client HTTP service
- If it's purely client-side logic: Reimplement in the client project
- Examples:
  - `IBreadcrumbService` → Reimplement client-side (it just tracks navigation)
  - `IMarkdownRenderingService` → Can run client-side in WASM (Markdig works in WASM)
  - `ISignalRUrlProvider` → Not needed; client connects directly

## Static assets
- Move `wwwroot/` contents to `Homespun.Client/wwwroot/`
- Move `package.json` and Tailwind config to client project
- Move `.razor.css` scoped style files alongside their components

## _Imports.razor
Create `Homespun.Client/_Imports.razor` with:
```razor
@using System.Net.Http
@using System.Net.Http.Json
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Forms
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Routing
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly.Http
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Projects
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.PullRequests
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Git
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Notifications
@using Homespun.Client.Layout
@using Homespun.Client.Components
@using Homespun.Client.Services
```

## Verification
- All components compile in the WASM project
- Pages render correctly when served via the Server project
- Navigation between pages works
- Data loads correctly from API endpoints
- No remaining references to server-side services

title: Implement SignalR client integration for Blazor WASM
Update Docker build and CI/CD for new multi-project architecture
description:
Update the Docker build, startup scripts, and CI/CD workflows to work with the new multi-project architecture (Homespun.Server, Homespun.Client, Homespun.Shared) and the existing Homespun.Worker (TypeScript/Hono).

## Dockerfile changes

### Current Dockerfile
The current Dockerfile builds a single `Homespun` project. It needs to be updated to:

1. **Build the WASM client** → produces static files
2. **Build the server** → produces the API server binary
3. **Package them together** → server serves the WASM client files

### New multi-stage Dockerfile
\`\`\`dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0 AS build
WORKDIR /src

# Install Node.js for Tailwind CSS build (client project)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y nodejs npm

# Copy solution and project files
COPY Homespun.sln .
COPY src/Homespun.Shared/Homespun.Shared.csproj src/Homespun.Shared/
COPY src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj src/Homespun.Server/
COPY src/Homespun.Client/Homespun.Client.csproj src/Homespun.Client/
COPY src/Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk/Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk.csproj src/Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk/

# Restore
RUN dotnet restore

# Copy everything and build
COPY . .

# Build Tailwind CSS (in client project)
WORKDIR /src/src/Homespun.Client
RUN npm install && npm run css:build

# Publish server (which includes WASM client via ProjectReference)
WORKDIR /src
RUN dotnet publish src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj -c Release -o /app/publish

# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM ${BASE_IMAGE} AS final
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/publish .
ENTRYPOINT ["dotnet", "Homespun.Server.dll"]
\`\`\`

### Key points
- The Server .csproj should have a ProjectReference to the Client project so `dotnet publish` automatically includes WASM output
- Server .csproj needs: `<ProjectReference Include="../Homespun.Client/Homespun.Client.csproj" />` (or use the `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly.Server` package approach)
- The published output should contain both the server DLL and the `wwwroot/_framework/` directory with WASM files
- **Homespun.Worker** (TypeScript/Hono) has its own separate Dockerfile (`src/Homespun.Worker/Dockerfile`) and builds independently — it is NOT part of the .NET server build

## start.sh changes
- Update the entrypoint to run `Homespun.Server.dll` instead of `Homespun.dll`
- Health check URL should remain the same (`/health`)

## CI/CD workflow changes

### ci.yml (CI pipeline)
- Update build command to build the full solution (which already happens with `dotnet build`)
- Update test commands if test project references changed
- Ensure Tailwind CSS build step targets the Client project
- Homespun.Worker TypeScript tests (`npm test` in `src/Homespun.Worker/`) should continue to run as before

### cd.yml (CD pipeline)
- Update the main image build to publish `Homespun.Server`
- The **Homespun.Worker** image builds from `src/Homespun.Worker/Dockerfile` — this remains unchanged
- Update build args if needed

## Solution file changes
- Add `Homespun.Shared`, `Homespun.Server`, `Homespun.Client` to the solution
- Remove the old `Homespun` project from the solution (or keep it temporarily during transition)
- `Homespun.Worker` (TypeScript) is referenced in the solution for IDE convenience but is not a .csproj project
- Update solution folder nesting

## launchSettings.json
- Create launch profiles for the Server project
- The development workflow: run `Homespun.Server` which serves both API and WASM client
- Mock mode should still work via environment variables

## Infrastructure (Bicep) changes
- Update `infra/main.bicep` container app configuration if the entry point changed
- Update any container image references
- Homespun.Worker container app configuration remains unchanged

## docker-compose changes
- Update the main service to build and run `Homespun.Server`
- The worker service continues to use `src/Homespun.Worker/Dockerfile`
- Ensure networking between Server and Worker containers is correct

## Verification
- `docker build` succeeds for both Server and Worker images
- Container starts and serves the WASM client at the root URL
- API endpoints accessible at `/api/*`
- Health check passes at `/health`
- CI pipeline passes
- Mock mode works in container
- Worker containers can be spawned by the Server for agent execution

title: Update test projects for new architecture
description:
Update all test projects (Homespun.Tests, Homespun.Api.Tests, Homespun.E2E.Tests) to work with the new multi-project architecture. The Homespun.Worker TypeScript tests are independent and should continue working as-is.

## Homespun.Tests (Unit Tests)

### Project reference changes
- Remove reference to old `Homespun` project
- Add reference to `Homespun.Server` (for testing server-side services)
- Add reference to `Homespun.Shared` (for shared types)
- Update `InternalsVisibleTo` in `Homespun.Server.csproj` to include `Homespun.Tests`

### Namespace updates
- Update all `using Homespun.Features.*` to `using Homespun.Server.Features.*`
- Update all `using Homespun.Components.*` references if any bUnit tests exist

### bUnit component tests
- These currently test Blazor Server components. They need to be updated to test the WASM client components instead
- Change project reference from Server to Client for component tests
- If both server and client tests are needed, consider splitting into separate test projects or using conditional compilation
- Update service mocks: instead of mocking `IProjectService`, mock `HttpProjectApiService` or use a mock `HttpClient` via `HttpMessageHandler`

### Service unit tests
- These should largely work as-is since they test service implementations that moved to `Homespun.Server`
- Update namespaces
- Verify all test fixtures still resolve correctly

## Homespun.Api.Tests (API Integration Tests)

### Project reference changes
- Change `WebApplicationFactory<Program>` to reference the new `Homespun.Server` project
- The `Program` class now lives in `Homespun.Server`
- Update `InternalsVisibleTo` in `Homespun.Server.csproj` to include `Homespun.Api.Tests`

### Test updates
- Update namespace references
- Verify all existing API tests still pass (the endpoints should be identical)
- Add new tests for CORS headers if needed
- Add tests verifying the WASM static file serving works

### WebApplicationFactory configuration
\`\`\`csharp
public class HomespunApiFactory : WebApplicationFactory<Homespun.Server.Program>
{
    protected override void ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder builder)
    {
        builder.UseEnvironment("Testing");
        // Configure test services
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Homespun.E2E.Tests (End-to-End Tests)

### Changes
- These tests use Playwright to interact with the browser - they should work largely unchanged since the URL structure stays the same
- Update the `HomespunFixture` to start `Homespun.Server` instead of the old `Homespun` project
- The server now serves the WASM client, so page navigation should work the same way
- May need to add waits for WASM initialization (the app takes longer to load initially compared to Blazor Server)
- Consider adding a loading/splash screen check

### Potential issues
- **Initial load time**: Blazor WASM has a longer initial load than Server. E2E tests may need increased timeouts or explicit waits for WASM initialization
- **WebSocket connections**: Playwright may need to wait for SignalR connections to be established before interacting with real-time features

## Homespun.Worker Tests (TypeScript - Vitest)
- The `tests/Homespun.Worker/` test suite (81 Vitest tests) is independent of the .NET project split
- These tests should continue to pass without modification
- Ensure CI continues to run `npm test` in the Worker test directory

## New test considerations

### Client-side integration tests
Consider adding a new test project `Homespun.Client.Tests` for:
- bUnit tests for WASM components
- Testing HTTP service classes with mocked HttpClient
- Testing SignalR service classes with mocked HubConnection

### Test data
- Existing test data in `tests/data/sessions/` should remain compatible
- Mock mode should still work via the Server project

## Verification
- All unit tests pass: `dotnet test tests/Homespun.Tests/`
- All API tests pass: `dotnet test tests/Homespun.Api.Tests/`
- All E2E tests pass: `dotnet test tests/Homespun.E2E.Tests/`
- All Worker tests pass: `npm test` in `tests/Homespun.Worker/`
- Test coverage is maintained or improved

title: Remove old monolith project and final cleanup
description:
Remove the old `Homespun` monolith project from the solution and perform final cleanup to ensure the new architecture is clean and complete.

## Steps

### 1. Remove old project
- Remove `src/Homespun/` directory entirely
- Remove `Homespun` project entry from `Homespun.sln`
- Verify no other projects still reference the old `Homespun.csproj`

### 2. Verify solution structure
The final solution should contain:
\`\`\`
Homespun.sln
├── src/
│   ├── Homespun.Shared/          (class library - shared DTOs/contracts)
│   ├── Homespun.Server/          (ASP.NET web API - backend)
│   ├── Homespun.Client/          (Blazor WASM - frontend)
│   ├── Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk/  (class library - Claude SDK C# wrapper)
│   └── Homespun.Worker/          (TypeScript/Hono - agent worker using @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk)
├── tests/
│   ├── Homespun.Tests/           (unit tests)
│   ├── Homespun.Api.Tests/       (API integration tests)
│   ├── Homespun.E2E.Tests/       (browser E2E tests)
│   └── Homespun.Worker/          (Vitest tests for TypeScript worker)
└── infra/                        (Azure Bicep deployment)
\`\`\`

### 3. Verify project references
\`\`\`
Homespun.Server → Homespun.Shared, Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk, Homespun.Client
Homespun.Client → Homespun.Shared
Homespun.Worker → (standalone TypeScript project, no .NET references)
Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk → (standalone .NET class library)
Homespun.Tests → Homespun.Server, Homespun.Shared (and optionally Homespun.Client)
Homespun.Api.Tests → Homespun.Server
Homespun.E2E.Tests → (none - browser tests)
\`\`\`

### 4. Clean up unused code
- Remove any backward-compatibility shims added during migration
- Remove any `// TODO: remove after migration` comments
- Remove any dead using statements
- Ensure no duplicate type definitions exist across projects

### 5. Update documentation
- Update `README.md` with new project structure
- Update `CLAUDE.md` with new development workflow
- Document: how to run the server, how to develop the client, how to build/deploy
- Document the Homespun.Worker TypeScript project's role in agent execution

### 6. Full verification
- `dotnet build Homespun.sln` succeeds with no warnings
- `dotnet test` for all .NET test projects passes
- `npm test` in Homespun.Worker test directory passes
- `docker build` succeeds for both Server and Worker images
- Application runs end-to-end:
  - Server starts and serves WASM client
  - Navigation works
  - API calls work
  - SignalR real-time updates work
  - Mock mode works
  - Session streaming works (Server → Worker → Claude SDK → SSE back to Server → SignalR to Client)
  
### 7. Performance check
- Measure initial page load time (WASM download + initialization)
- Verify SignalR reconnection works after page refresh
- Check that the WASM bundle size is reasonable
- Consider enabling ahead-of-time (AOT) compilation for better runtime performance (can be a follow-up)

title: Investigate answer endpoint of worker
description: There is an '/answer' endpoint in the Homespun worker code. This could be a legacy item that should be removed if it is handled natively in the Claude Agent SDK - investigate this directly and remove it (and all related tests) if it is not required for answering questions.

title: Clicking "back to session" when session isn't found leads to the agents page
Private repositories aren't being added
description: Private repositories that the user are authenticated to access aren't being added in. Check whether this is a PAT issue or whether Octokit is not receiving the GitHub authentication token at all.

The correct number of issues is being loaded and sent to the server, but there appears to be an issue with deserialization. See logs: Homespun.Client.Pages.ProjectDetail[0]

title: Remove watchtower
description: Remove watchtower from all build scripts and container build - it's not required anymore as we will be using Azure Container Apps for most of the production deployment.

title: Agents aren't being started with issue template variables replaced
description: When an agent is staretd on an issue, the template comes in with {{title}} text rather than the actual injected titles. Ensure that these are injected with template variables replaced when the prompt is being sent to the agent worker.

title: Fleece is not installed on the worker containers
description: Ensure the Fleece CLI tool is installed on the worker containers. This can be done with `dotnet tool install -g Fleece.Cli`. It is installed on the parent application containers, just not on the agent worker containers.

title: Dotnet is not installed on the worker containers
description: Ensure that the dotnet CLI is installed on the worker containers, including .NET SDKs 8, 9 and 10. This used to be done by having the image based on the dotnet SDK base image (similar to the main container image), but now we are working off the node image due to the Typescript SDK.

title: Permissions are not being passed to agent per command
description: Changing the permissions in the UI and sending a new message to Claude does not change the permissions of the agent. This should be updated such that new permissions are sent and the Claude Code server should use new permissions per message (if they change). The "build" permission that is used to start agents should refer to "bypass permissions".

title: Pass all messages from server to client
description: Currently messages are only persisted on the client when they are sent over SignalR, and refreshing a page can lose messages. Instead, these messages should be stored on the server (in memory or using the JSONL serialisation if that still exists) and the client should be able to perform a full refresh of all messages from the server. This should only happen if the use enters a page that is currently midway through processing. It should start by showing the messages as they stream in, with a button at the top of the conversation that prompts the user to load all previous messages in from the server.

title: "Starting session" doesn't refresh after session is started
description: When a new agent is started the "starting session" button goes into a "loading" state. Even though the timeline shows the session has started the page must be refreshed for the button to disappear and show the "open chat" page. Review this - once the agent has been started the state of this button and the issue properties panel should change.

title: Answer and plan tools no longer work correctly
description:
The answer and plan tools for the application no longer work correctly when run in docker agent execution mode. In this mode, when the agent uses the AskUserQuestion tool the agent should stop and wait for the user to respond. It is currently assuming that the first answer provided is the correct one and proceeding anyway. This is required only for the local execution mode as in the local execution mode the instance of Claude Code by default will just continue execution.

However, we are using the Typescript version of the Claude Agent SDK in Docker and Azure Container Apps execution modes, which allows this flow. Refer to https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript#ask-user-question and https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/user-input#handle-clarifying-questions for information on how the SDK handles clarifying questions.

Plans operate somewhat differently. Review the code and documentation and see if you can come up with a plan to fix this behaviour for the Docker and Agent Execution modes. The SDK has recently been changed to V1, so this may be fixed or it may require minor updates to the code.

If it will simplify things, OK to remove specific handling for questions and tools from the Local execution mode. Otherwise, if they are handled separately and don't interact in the different modes then OK to leave it as is.

title: Exception is thrown when branches page is open
description: When I open the branches page on a project, the following error is logged to the browser console. Review the root cause of this exception and fix it.

title: Session not found page flashes up when opening sessions
description: When opening a session page in the browser, a "session not found" page often flashes up briefly while the session is being loaded. Instead of this, switch straight to the session page with skeleton components and only go to the session not found page when it is confirmed by the server that the session doesn't exist.

title: Thinking messages are out of order with tool usages and displayed as normal chat
description: Thinking messages are streamed in from the chat while an agent is operating. They sometimes overwrite old messages and often appear out of order with the tool use messages (i.e. they always appear just below the last user chat while tool messages accumulate below. Put together a plan for joining and persisting thinking messages that show messages in order and stop appending to messages when interrupted by a tool use message.

title: Check that stopping sessions kills container
Add activity log
Switching projects with side bar doesn't work
description: The page address and heading changes but the graph doesn't refresh when switching projects with the project list in the side bar.

title: Branch source not mounted to agent worker containers
description: When a new agent container is created to work on an issue, it should have the branch folder of the parent container (/data/src/{project}/.clones/{branch}) mounted to the path , and the Claude Code Executable should be started in this directory. Currently the volume does not appear to be mounted, and the agent has difficulty in finding the correct source files to read and edit.

title: CurrentPullRequestDetailPanel doesn't refresh session status via SignalR
description:
The CurrentPullRequestDetailPanel component displays session status but does not subscribe to SignalR events (SessionStarted, SessionStatusChanged, SessionStopped). The session status badge will show stale data after session state changes on the server. Users must navigate away and back to see the current state.

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription following the IssueDetailPanel.razor pattern:
1. Add @implements IAsyncDisposable
2. Declare private HubConnection? _hubConnection
3. Call SetupSignalRConnectionAsync() from OnInitializedAsync()
4. Subscribe to SessionStarted, SessionStatusChanged, SessionStopped events
5. Implement proper DisposeAsync() cleanup

title: AgentManagementPanel doesn't refresh session list via SignalR
description:
The AgentManagementPanel component (displayed on /sessions page) shows a list of active sessions with status badges and uptime, but does not subscribe to SignalR events. The session list won't show live status updates - users must refresh the page to see new agent statuses or when sessions stop.

Issues:
- Does NOT implement IAsyncDisposable
- Does NOT have SignalR subscription
- Status indicators and uptime calculations become stale
- New sessions won't appear without refresh

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription and refresh the sessions list when events are received.

title: CloneManagementPanel doesn't refresh session status via SignalR
description:
The CloneManagementPanel component displays session status badges in the branch table, but does not subscribe to SignalR events. The session status won't update when a session stops or changes status.

Issues:
- Does NOT implement IAsyncDisposable
- Does NOT have SignalR subscription
- Session status badges in branch rows won't update in real-time

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription to refresh session status when events are received.

title: AgentControlPanel doesn't refresh session status via SignalR
description:
The AgentControlPanel component displays a session status badge but does not subscribe to SignalR events. If a session stops on the server, the badge won't update unless the user clicks another control.

Issues:
- Does NOT implement IAsyncDisposable
- Does NOT have SignalR subscription
- Session status badge shows stale data

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription following the IssueDetailPanel.razor pattern.

title: Close issue edit page and go back to the project page when saving and editing issues
description: When the Save & Run Agent button is clicked on the 'Edit Issue' page, this should immediately return back to the main 'Project' when clicked. The agent startup should happen asynchronously on the server.

title: Mini agent prompts should run in Local Agent Execution mode by default
description: Mini agent prompts should run in the same process as the server and not spin up a new container as the memory usage is likely very small. The only current example of the mini agent prompts is the working branch ID generator, but the overall infrastructure for mini prompts should use this local execution pattern.

title: Simplify agent worker container management
description:
The agent worker container management system for both the Docker execution mode and Azure Container Apps execution modes should be updated to follow similar behaviour, ideally using the same interface if possible.

The behaviour should be as follows:

- The server should maintain a list of all currently running containers and the clone that they are working on.
- When an agent is started to work on an issue in a clone, the list is checked to determine if there is a container already running for that clone. If there is a container already running, the server should check the state of the agent in that container. The following states are possible:
  - No currently running session: a new session is started
  - Session in "working", "question" or "plan" state: the user is notified that there is already an agent working in that clone.
  - Session in "waiting" state: the user is asked whether they would like to terminate that session.
  - Session in "error" state: a new session is started
- If there is no container running, a new container is started with that clone folder mounted as a volume (this is already implemented).

title: Mount .claude folder
description:
The original cloning behaviour was meant to have the following structure (from the perspective of the main application container):

- /data/repos/{project}/.clones : Contains all clones of the main branch that agents are working on.
- /data/repos/{project}/.clones/{clone}/.claude : The Claude folder mounted for the agent. This is where the agents working on each clone store their session information. This should be mounted as /home/homespun/.claude for each agent container.
- /data/repos/{project}/.clones/{clone}/workdir : The code for the repository should be cloned in this folder. This is mounted as /workdir for each agent container.

Instead of this, only the /workdir folder seems to be mounted and the /data/repos/{project}/.clones/{clone}/ folder contains all of the cloned repo source code.

title: Agent goes into waiting status when plan is submitted with clear context instructions
description: When starting execution on a new plan in the session chat for an agent in docker execution mode, the agent starts executing but the status remains as 'waiting' instead of 'working' in the Homespun app. Fixed by adding missing BroadcastSessionStatusChanged calls in SendMessageAsync.

title: Clear context and continue often doesn't work
description:
When I clear context and start implementation of a plan, this often results in the plan options UI being left at the bottom of the session message window. The session status also often stays as "waiting" or "plan ready".

Once the "clear context and continue" button is clicked, the UI should disappear and the status should be set to working unless there is an error in executing this on the worker container.

title: Fix order of worker Dockerfile to install dotnet before building app
description: The worker container Dockerfile appears to build the server app before installing Dotnet, which doesn't allow caching of the Dotnet app. Review and optimise the worker container Dockerflie.

title: Ensure that stopping sessions kills container
description:
When sessions are stopped the parent container that they were running in is not necessarily killed. This should always happen when the "stop session" button is pressed, both the button in the session and in the sessions list window.

Currently only killing the containers from the container window stops the Docker containers.

title: Show all worker containers and their status
description: I would like an additional page in the side bar for worker containers. This should page should show a list of all current worker containers and their status that have been started by the application, and include information about the project and issue they were started on, the state that the agents are in and a button to manually kill them.

title: Remove all logging from tests
description: Many tests log results to the console. Any ILogger that is being used in tests should log to file only in a TestResults folder to improve the test performance. Update the logging behaviour of the test projects to ensure that this is happening.

title: Stop streaming messages
description: In the docker agent execution mode, messages are streamed word-by-word from the agent container all the way to the UI. This is causing some issues with the ordering of some messages, and tool usages and thinking messages are getting confused. Instead of this, just show complete messages in the UI and attempt to do this at the source by either disabling the streaming of partial messages in the worker container's use of the agent SDK.

title: Install Chromium on worker containers to enable Playwright MCP tools
description: Chromium isn't installed on the worker containers so the Playwright MCP tools cannot be used. Update the Dockerfile to install Chromium in the container image.

title: Handle post-merge testing
Run branch ID mini-prompt in background
description: When a new issue is created the branch ID is generated from the title using a mini prompt. This is done synchronously and blocks opening of the editing page until the ID is generated. Instead of this, it should run in the background, with the "suggest" button in a loading state until the branch ID has been generated.

title: Task graph loading waits for GitHub PRs to load
description: The graph

title: When starting an agent on a PR, clone the branch of the PR before starting agent
Messages out of order
description:
The way that messages are shown in a chat has changed with streaming message disabled and this has generally resulted in a cleaner output. However, some messages (like summaries and implementation plans) are shown at the top of the message stack (i.e. directly under the last input) rather than at the very bottom of the conversation, in order of how they were sent by the agent.

Review the message ordering system - they should generally be shown in order that they were sent back from the app. Also, change the display of thinking messages so they appear to be of lower visual priority when compared to direct messages from the agent. Update the design system with a thinking message type that is lighter.

title: Default timeline mode
description:
When a project page is opened the old mode of rendering the timeline is shown first before switching to the task graph mode. Only the task graph mode should be shown as a method of rendering the timeline - remove the other mode from the codebase entirely.

Ensure that at the top of the timeline the 5 most recent PRs as well as a button to load previous PRs is shown, similar to how the old system worked.

title: Containers page doesn't show correct session
description: The linked session link shown on the container page does not have the correct ID to allow it to navigate to the current session page. Review the way that a session ID is assigned to a container to ensure that it corresponds with the session IDs that are used to show sessions in the Homespun app. The word "conversation" may also be used to distinguish the difference between a session operating on an issue and a session in the Claude Code sense of the word. I would suggest instead just using the issue ID for sessions as we want to maintain a "one clone and one agent container per issue" policy, with a GUID used only if the agent is not operating on an issue (e.g. it is operating directly on a branch).

title: Consolidate ID types
Make logs one line
description:
All logs are currently logged to two lines.

Simplify the log implementation to just single line logs. Show the file name, class and line number where the log is called.

title: Add plan indicator to agent status page
description:
When a session is in plan ready mode, it should have an indication on the /sessions page and should be pushed to the top of the list. The order should be:

- Plan ready
- Question
- Waiting
- Working

title: Mount correct .claude folder
description:
Currently a .claude folder is created per issue next to the workdir folder. However the .Claude folder that is mounted for the agent container is one level up, in the project folder.

Change the mounted folder to the one created per issue to avoid contention over session storage.

title: Add front end observability
description: Add logging to the front end. In Azure Container Apps mode this should log to the Application Insights resource that is set up in the Azure infrastructure. Let me know your thoughts on logging for the Docker and local (i.e. dotnet run) modes.

title: Pull session mode and model from agent container
description:
Investigate the way that session statuses are pulled from the agent worker container. They currently appear to be storing this when the agent is first run, resulting in the "mode" being either Plan or Build and staying like that regardless of the prompts that come afterwards (which can change the permissions mode).

The model also always just shows the same model that the agent was launched with.

Finally, the status often stays as "Planning" or "Waiting" after plans are sent for execution. The agent container should be aware that the agent is working and sending back responses, so should be able to store and send back status updates (along with the current model and permissions mode) through a "/status" endpoint.

title: Update graph rendering
description:
Make the following updates to the graph rendering on the project page.

- Merged and closed PRs are not shown in the task graph. They should be shown at the top, above the "Issues" divider. Only the most recent 5 should be shown, with a button for loading 5 more PRs at the very top of the list.
- Add markers for PR and agent status to each issue in the graph. If a PR is linked to an issue, a PR badge should show up with the PR number, that when clicked takes the user to GitHub. The agent status should show whether the agent is currently working. This has been implemented in previous versions of the graph rendering component but has since disappeared, review the codebase to port this behaviour over and clean up the old graph display.
- Add a marker showing the type of the issue.
- Show all merged PRs and issues with circles. Issues without descriptions should be hollow circles and issues with descriptions should be solid circles. The colour of the circle should be based on the agent status.

title: PR caching not used on project load
description: It appears that the PR cache is not being used when the projects page is first opened, resulting in a long "loading" wait while the page loads. The PR cache that is saved in the project folder (/data/src/{project}/pull_requests.jsonl) should be used for the initial load, with updated PRs loaded in the background.

title: Fix browser exceptions
description: There are quite a number of browser errors and observability exceptions thrown in the front end. Navigate to the running application at http://homespun-dev.tail0d83d3.ts.net:8080/ and look around the various project and branch pages using the Playwright MCP tools to get an understanding of the errors that are being logged in the browser and put together a plan to fix them.

title: Change planning indicator colour to orange on sessions page
description: When a "plan ready" is shown on the sessions page the dot at the top right corner is the same colour as a "working" session. It should be the same colour (orange) as the working agent in the top right hand corner of the screen. Review the CSS structure of the application to ensure that these are all using the same global style.

title: Status doesn't update while plan is executing
description:
When I commence work on a plan by clicking on the "Clear Context & Start Implementation", the status often remains as "Plan ready" in all of the various status indicators. Please review whether it is the front end, server or worker container that is causing this to happen. The container should generally be polled directly, and is apparently regularly polled by the front end to ensure that the containers are up to date, so it is most likely an issue with the agent container or server passing messages through.

The session window also often has the buttons for executing the plan remain in place while the agent is working. Once one of these buttons is clicked this should trigger the plan mode to be removed **and** the status to be updated. Review in detail.

title: Workdir not mounted correctly and .Claude doesn't contain plans
description:
When a new agent container is started in Docker agent execution mode, incorrect folders are mounted to the new container by the Homespun application. Instead of mounting `/data/src/{project}/.clones/{id}/workdir` to `/workdir` and `/data/src/{project}/.clones/{id}/.claude` to `/home/homespun/.claude`, the entire `/data/src/{project}/.clones/{id}` folder is mounted to `/workdir`, resulting in the repository being located at `/workdir/workdir/` and the `/home/.claude/` folder just being in the container's local storage rather than the persisted storage of the parent machine. I believe this error was introduced in PRs #250 or #253.

See logs below of commands used to inspect the worker container's structure.

azureuser@vm-devvm-n0cb75:~/repos/Homespun$ docker exec homespun-issue-a4b29e8d-e1a4-4b6c-ae79-2990a21ce363-WJpWmJ ls -la /workdir
total 16
drwxr-xr-x  4 homespun homespun 4096 Feb 17 00:40 .
drwxr-xr-x  1 root     root     4096 Feb 18 03:48 ..
drwxr-xr-x  2 homespun homespun 4096 Feb 17 00:40 .claude
drwxr-xr-x 12 homespun homespun 4096 Feb 17 00:40 workdir

title: Add arbitrary environment variables to worker
description: I would like to be able to add additional environment variables to the worker containers when they are started such as license keys etc. Add a secrets management page for each project that allows keys to be stored locally per project (in a secrets.env in the project folder, next to the .clones and main folder). These can then be injected when the container is started.

title: Issue sync behaviour broken
description:
The issue synchronisation behaviour is generally broken, with two significant issues:

- It appears to commit changes to the fleece issues first before pulling changes from the origin. This causes divergence and merge conflicts. Instead, it should always pull first before committing such that the fleece issue changes are unstaged and can just sit on top of the pulled changes. Due to the hashing behaviour this should result in two sits of issues files that can then be merged. Tests should be added using temporary git repositories to simulate different git conditions with multiple merged branches containing changes issues files to confirm that if the only local changes that occur in the main branch used by the Homespun application are in the ./fleece folder to the issues_{hash}.jsonl and tombstones_{hash}.jsonl files, that there are never any merge conflicts that prevent syncing.
- The full issues data from disk is not read and used to replace the issues data in memory when the sync button is pressed. As a result, if the file changes at some point the front end does not receive issues information such as the description from the back end. Clicking on an issue in this condition results in no changes to the UI, but clicking on pre-existing issues that haven't changed still works.

title: Fix the reported port in the ./scripts/mock.ps1 and mock.sh files
description: The ports reported in the mock script files are not correct and are reported before the build takes place. Remove these and rely on the application logging to show the port locations.

title: Max turns error caused shutdown of container
description:
During agent execution there was an error logged by the container that resulted in the Claude Code process exiting prematurely. Different errors were shown, however only one container had the error passed through to the Homespun front end.

Error 1:
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_max_turns', is_error=true
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_max_turns'
ERROR [logger.js:26] query forwarder error for session 'd80727c7-49bf-44f8-a362-15452f4ea02d': Claude Code process exited with code 1

Error 2:
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution', is_error=true
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution'
ERROR [logger.js:26] query forwarder error for session 'b9f5ea1d-7952-4680-be33-8d4ec2bdc694': Claude Code process exited with code 1
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution', is_error=true
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution'
ERROR [logger.js:26] query forwarder error for session 'f713a37a-83ba-4377-a89a-de3a1611b8a7': Claude Code process exited with code 1

Please review and diagnose the issue. Refer to the Claude Agent SDK documentation at https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript#sdk-result-message. Fail gracefully with an error message in the chat window, and try to allow reconnection to the same session if possible.

title: Fix graph display of merged PRs
description:
Merged PRs are displayed in order of newest at top and oldest at the bottom in the projects page of the UI. They should instead be shown with the oldest at the top and the newest at the bottom, so that the issues appear like they are being "pulled" up into the PRs. There should also be a vertical line coming down from the PRs and connecting to the left side of the "next" nodes that are in the left-most lane of the graph. Push all issue nodes over +1 lane to the right to allow the left-most lane to hold the edges between the main PR and the nodes.

The merged PR nodes also only have a line extending from the bottom of the nodes. They should have lines extending out of the top and bottom as they are always connecting to another merged PR (except for the very oldest one at the top after all PRs are expanded.

title: Show PR status in graph where linked
description:
Show the PR status in the issue graph for linked PRs:
- Red if tests have failed
- Grey if no tests have been run
- Orange if there are merge conflicts
- Yellow if tests are running
- Green if all tests pass and PRs are mergeable.

This status should be asynchronously loaded and does not need to be persisted. This status should be polled by the server and sent to the client through SignalR updates (with trace logging as a maximum) along with the polling for merged PRs and new open PRs. Polling should occur only for open PRs.

title: Add details to container card
description:
Update the container card on the /containers page:

- Display issue for the container in the title of the Container card on the "containers" page (the span with class `container-name`). Currently it just has the name of the container, which should be in the card below under the "Issue:" detail row.
- The "Project" also comes up as "Unkown" - ensure that this shows the project that the container is working in, and the cards are grouped by project similar to the "Sessions" page.
- The "Project" value should be a link to the project page that the container is working in.
- The "Issue" value should be a link to the project page and should select the issue on navigation (possibly with a # anchor to trigger the selection).
- When a plan is ready or there are other status updates there is a little badge at the bottom of the card saying "Plan Approval Pending" - this should just be shown in the "Status" detail row to replace the text.

title: Consolidate project and src folder
description:
Currently when a session is started in Docker Agent Execution mode, a .claude/ folder and a workdir/ folder is mounted to the container as volumes, allowing the container to persist it's memory. This is logged as such:

```
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting working directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/src/Homespun/.clones/bug+fix-status-update+vHU2gY/workdir -> /workdir
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting .claude directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/projects/default/issues/vHU2gY/.claude -> /home/homespun/.claude
```

While the mount targed on the worker container is correct, there is some confusion in where these folders are being created. They should be consolidate d in the projects folder and under the same project title. This means that the above should read:

```
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting working directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/projects/Homespun/.clones/bug+fix-status-update+vHU2gY/workdir -> /workdir
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting .claude directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/projects/Homespun/.clones/bug+fix-status-update+vHU2gY/.claude -> /home/homespun/.claude
```

The changes are:
- Repository working directory is created in the `projects` folder, not the `src` folder. Otherwise this is OK
- .claude folder is created in the `projects` folder but with the project repository name included in the path. The .claude folder should sit beside the workdir folder on the application server file system, so should include the full branch name similar to the working directory.

title: Re-render graph when issues are changed
description: SignalR events are not triggering the graph to be re rendered when things like agent status changes or issues get added.

title: Show only last 5 merged PRs
description: Currently the first 5 merged PRs are shown at the top of the graph. Instead of this, the **most recent** 5 merged PRs should be shown. The sort order is correct.

title: Put "next" issues with descriptions at the top
description: The Fleece library was just updated to version 1.4.0 to use a new sorting logic for the graph layout service. Update the library dependency to update the graph layout in this application. Review the PRs references in the release notes on GitHub for this new library version. Run build and fix all tests as required.

title: Update agent status on task graph with SignalR
description:
The agent status in the task graph on the /projects/{project} page doesn't update like the agent status at the top right of the page. Use SignalR to ensure that the agent status is updated on the task graph.

This seems to be particularly the case when an agent is started.

Also make it such that clicking on the agent status badge takes the user to the appropriate session window.

title: Clearing context and executing a plan still results in a container entering "waiting" status
Combine global and project prompts
description:
There is a lot of duplication between the global prompts and the project prompts, and it looks like they are copied in by default. Instead of this, consider the project prompts to be in addition to the global prompts. Use the prompt names as keys - if the project prompts contains a new prompt with the same key, the project prompts should override the global prompt.

Add a control to the project prompts page that allows the user to create a new prompt based on an existing global prompt - this should create a new project prompt that copies the global prompt in for editing.

title: Remove Azure Container Apps execution mode
description: Due to the slow performance issues with running development workloads on Azure Container Apps, just get rid of Azure Container Apps and keep the Docker execution mode. Remove the infra folder and it's content as well as this was set up to work with ACA.

title: Fix branch ID generation
description:
The mini prompt service seems to be failing due to rate limiting issues. Please investigate. The logs are below:

title: Improve load performance for large projects with many PRs
description:
Improve the load performance, particularly for very large projects by using the PR cache more effectively. When a project page is opened the PR cache in the project folder and the issues JSONL should be read first (if not already read into memory) and should be used to render the graph.

When the PRs are synced, only information on the open PRs should be read in to update the status of PRs and link PRs to issues.

Following this, the open PRs that were cached should be checked against this recently pulled list of open PRs to see which ones are still open. For the ones that were not in the list pulled in, the status of these should then be checked to update them to either closed or merged as appropriate.

This should all happen asynchronously after the graph has been rendered.

title: Issues not being written with full description with CTRL+Enter
description: Occasionally issues are not being persisted with their full descriptions. Review the issue updating logic to ensure that the full issue description is written to the Fleece issues JSONL file in the repository. This appears to be the case when CTRL + Enter is used to save the changes instead of clicking the "Save Changes" button, and when the user hasn't lost focus on the description box.

title: Open sessions at bottom of chat
description: When a session page is opened, it should show the chat screen scrolled all the way to the bottom of the chat. It should auto scroll to the bottom of the chat screen when it is scrolled to the bottom, but if not it should just remain as is and show the "scroll down" button that is currently shown.

title: Decouple client, server and worker with AG-UI
description:
The AG-UI message protocol should be used instead of the custom/translated message formats that are used in the application. This should be done as follows:

- The worker containers should translate the messages and events emitted by the Claude Agent SDK to the messages and events from the A2A library available with `npm install @a2a-js/sdk`.
- These should be received and handled by the server, and then translated into the AG-UI  protocol and passed on to the client.
- The client should then render these messages.
- The Microsoft Agent Framework contains an implementation of AG-UI in the Nuget package Microsoft.Agents.AI.Hosting.AGUI.AspNetCore.
