Review the documentation at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/integrations/ag-ui/?pivots=programming-language-csharp as well as other Microsoft Agent Framework tools to consider whether the entire worker architecture could be abstracted as an implementation of IChatClient, or if it should remain as is.

Review the online documentation for these libraries to understand what needs to be done here.

title: Show plans in session window
description:
Add a new tab to the session window that shows the plans that are generated in the sessions. They can be found in the /data/src/{project}/.clones/{issue}/.claude/plans folder as Markdown files for each issue.

Show a preview of the plan content that can be expanded and add a button to copy the plan file path or content to the clipboard.

Note that there may be multiple plan files.

title: Show full issue title in containers window and associate with projects
description: In the containers page the containers are not being linked to the correct projects and issue information to display the name of each - it just says unknown project and the issue ID. Instead of this, it should show the full name. The name of the project and the issue ID is in the container name, use this for identification and update the displayed name.

title: Update sync box to just a sync button
description:
The sync "box" on the projects page that contains the "sync fleece" and "sync github" should be updated to just be a manual "sync" button that replaces the "refresh" link at the top of the page.

This should just perform the fleece sync as this also syncs the main branch, as well as perform the PR sync for open PRs only. This should all be done asynchronously.

title: CTRL+Enter behaviour still doesn't save descriptions while editing
description: A recent PR (#273) was meant to fix the behaviour in which saving changes to an issue from the edit page with CTRL+Enter only worked if the description lost focus. This didn't work as planned, however, and the application still doesn't save the changes made in the description box if CTRL+Enter is pressed without losing focus. Review this in closer detail and add tests specifically aimed at fixing this problem.

title: Improve logging
description: Logs in the server are currently written in a two line format. Change this so that all log entries are written to one line, including the line number, class that the log is coming from and level of the log.

title: Split sync into pulling main and committing issues
description:
Split the sync button into "sync" and "push". Sync should stash the issues changes, pull the latest changes from the origin, merge the issues and serialise them but do not commit and push the issues.

The push button should first perform the sync functions and then commit and push to main.

title: Install browser in worker container to allow E2E tests to run
description: The worker containers aren't able to run the E2E tests due to an issue with the installation of browsers in the worker container. Run dotnet test to find this issue, then run commands as required to install the browser required. Once the E2E tests can run successfully, update the worker container dockerfile or start.sh as required to fix this problem.

title: Get branch name from linked PR if one exists
description: If there is a linked PR that contains an issue ID for an issue, always use the branch name in the PR to find the clone and launch the agent in the appropriate location. If there is no PR, also check the folders in the /.clones folder to see if there are any folders with the same issue ID as the issue. Occasionally if the branch name has not been persisted and a PR is created, creating a new session for the issue can result in a new branch being created with no reference to the already created branch/PR (which has the same issue ID but different branch ID and therefore different branch name).

title: Fix flaky issue sync test
Some branch names are not being when AI generated
Update issue service mock implementation with full persistence
Render full graph edges when in insert mode
description:
Currently the display of the graph edges when new issues are being created don't match the condition that they're being added - they always show a line coming out the top of the node when (for example) if they are being added between two siblings they should show a line from both the top and bottom.

The graph rendering algorithm should treat these "new" issue nodes that haven't yet been created exactly the same as the other issues, with the exception that an editing control is shown instead of the issue title etc.

Consider refactoring the graph rendering algorithm and Blazor component to treat the new draft issues as being normal issues, and render the edges as they will be after the issue is created.

title: Worker A2A Integration
description:
Make the worker emit A2A-compliant events instead of custom SSE events.

## Tasks
1. Install @a2a-js/sdk in worker: `npm install @a2a-js/sdk`
2. Create A2A Event Translator (new file: src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/a2a-translator.ts)
   - Translate Claude SDK events to A2A protocol:
   - `assistant` message → A2A `Message` with `role: agent`
   - `tool_use` → A2A `Message` with structured content
   - `result` → A2A `TaskStatusUpdateEvent` with state `completed`/`failed`
   - `stream_event` → A2A streaming message parts
   - `question_pending` → A2A `TaskStatusUpdateEvent` with state `input-required`
   - `plan_pending` → A2A `TaskStatusUpdateEvent` with state `input-required`
3. Implement A2A Task Lifecycle in session-manager.ts
4. Update SSE Writer to emit A2A-formatted events

## Files to Modify
- src/Homespun.Worker/package.json
- src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/session-manager.ts
- src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/sse-writer.ts
- src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/a2a-translator.ts (new)

title: Server A2A Consumer + AG-UI Producer
description:
Server receives A2A events from worker, translates to AG-UI for client.

## Tasks
1. Install AG-UI NuGet package: `dotnet add package Microsoft.Agents.AI.Hosting.AGUI.AspNetCore --prerelease`
2. Create A2A Message Parser (new file: src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Data/A2AMessages.cs)
   - Define C# records: A2AMessage, A2ATask, A2AArtifact
   - Define events: A2ATaskStatusUpdateEvent, A2ATaskArtifactUpdateEvent
3. Create AG-UI Event Emitter (new file: src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AGUIEventService.cs)
   - A2A Message → AG-UI TextMessageStart/Content/End
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (working) → AG-UI RunStarted
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (completed) → AG-UI RunFinished
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (input-required for question) → AG-UI Custom event 'QuestionPending'
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (input-required for plan) → AG-UI Custom event 'PlanPending'
4. Update DockerAgentExecutionService to parse incoming SSE as A2A events
5. Update ClaudeCodeHub to emit AG-UI events

## Files to Modify
- src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Data/A2AMessages.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Data/AGUIEvents.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AGUIEventService.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/DockerAgentExecutionService.cs
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Hubs/ClaudeCodeHub.cs

title: Client AG-UI Consumer
description:
Client receives and renders AG-UI events.

## Tasks
1. Update SignalR Client (ClaudeCodeSignalRService.cs)
   - Add handlers: OnRunStarted, OnRunFinished, OnRunError
   - Add handlers: OnTextMessageStart, OnTextMessageContent, OnTextMessageEnd
   - Add handlers: OnToolCallStart, OnToolCallArgs, OnToolCallEnd, OnToolCallResult
   - Add handlers: OnStateSnapshot, OnStateDelta
2. Update Shared DTOs
   - Add AG-UI compatible event types: AGUIRunEvent, AGUITextMessageEvent, AGUIToolCallEvent
   - Map to existing ClaudeMessage/ClaudeMessageContent for rendering
3. Update Message Display Service
   - Handle AG-UI event sequences (Start/Content/End pattern)
4. Update Rendering Components
   - TextBlock.razor - Handle TextMessageContent streaming
   - ThinkingBlock.razor - Handle ReasoningMessageContent
   - ToolUseBlock.razor - Handle ToolCallStart/Args/End
   - ToolResultBlock.razor - Handle ToolCallResult

## Files to Modify
- src/Homespun.Client/Services/ClaudeCodeSignalRService.cs
- src/Homespun.Client/Services/MessageDisplayService.cs
- src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Sessions/AGUIEvents.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Shared/Hubs/IClaudeCodeHubClient.cs
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/TextBlock.razor
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/ThinkingBlock.razor
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/ToolUseBlock.razor
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/ToolResultBlock.razor

title: Protocol Integration Testing
description:
Test the full A2A → AG-UI message flow across all components.

## Tasks

### Unit Tests
Create new tests in tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/ClaudeCode/:
- A2AMessageParserTests.cs - Test A2A message parsing
- AGUIEventServiceTests.cs - Test A2A to AG-UI translation
- AGUIClientTests.cs - Test client event handling (bUnit)

### Integration Tests
Modify tests/Homespun.Api.Tests/:
- Test full message flow with AG-UI events
- Verify A2A events are correctly parsed from worker
- Verify AG-UI events are correctly emitted to client

### E2E Tests
Modify tests/Homespun.E2E.Tests/:
- Verify UI renders AG-UI events correctly
- Test session flow: start session, verify events reach client
- Test question flow: trigger AskUserQuestion, verify Custom event with QuestionPending
- Test plan approval: run plan mode, verify Custom event with PlanPending

## Verification Steps
1. Build worker: cd src/Homespun.Worker && npm run build
2. Build server: dotnet build src/Homespun.Server
3. Run tests: dotnet test
4. Start mock container: ./scripts/mock.sh
5. Manual verification of full flow

title: Show issues related to merged PRs
description: Merged PRs should allow selection and show the linked issue information if there is one. This should allow issues to be edited, and potentially re-opened if it is found that the merged PR did not resolve the issue.

title: Create logging container
description: The client, server and worker containers should all log to a PLG stack of containers running on the host machine. The client should send telemetry back to the server with a new endpoint on the server. Create a Docker Compose file for starting these containers alongside the server container and connecting them. The ./scripts/run.sh and ./scripts/run.ps1 scripts should be adjusted to use Docker Compose, while the ./scripts/mock.sh and ./scripts/mock.ps1 can remain as is, just using dotnet run.

title: Keyboard controls don't place in correct hierarchy
description:
When creating a new issue using the keyboard controls, the TAB and Shift+TAB keys should allow the new issue to move up and down lanes (if available, a node can only move up or down one lane). If created up a lane, it should become the parent of the issue directly above it. If created down a lane, it should become a child to the issue below it.

Think through the other permutations of issues above and below the "new" issue and how the hierarchy should be modified. Ask me if there are any questions. Consider both the parallel and series execution modes.

Add E2E tests for all permutations, modifying the mock data if required to fit these in.

title: New issues need to be refreshed to show up
Adding a new issue at the bottom of the issues shows two inputs
description: When I select the very last issue in the graph and create a new issue below using 'o', two inline issue editors appear and one is focused. Create tests to replicate this and then create a plan to fix it.

title: Clicking on the issue type badge opens context menu to change it
description: Clicking on the type badge of an issue on the projects page should allow the type of an issue to be changed.

title: Add buttons to issue rows for editing issues and starting agents.
description:
In the issue rows on the project page, add inline buttons to edit the issue and run agents on the right hand side. These should show up on hover and selection only.

The run agent should open a context panel that contains the agent control panel (i.e. the ability to start an agent with a particular prompt

title: Scroll down with selected issues
description: When the selected issue scrolls past the bottom of the screen using the keyboard controls, scroll the window down with it. Add the keyboard shortcuts g and shift+G to select the first and last issues as well.

title: Review correct hierarchy
PR status is always "Checks running"
description: When looking at any PR status in the client the status is always "checks running". Review the PR status service and see if you can find out what the issue is here.

title: Allow function keys to pass through on projects screen
description: Hitting the function keys while on the task graph doesn't do anything because they are being blocked by the keyboard controls. Allow the function keys to pass through to the browser (e.g. F5 to reload, F12 for browser dev tools, etc.)

title: Add dedicated Tailscale sidecar container to Docker Compose
description: Replace the embedded Tailscale setup in the homespun container with a dedicated Tailscale sidecar container in docker-compose.yml. Currently Tailscale runs inside the main homespun container (start.sh) and proxies services via tailscale serve. A dedicated sidecar would provide cleaner separation of concerns, allow Tailscale to persist independently of app restarts, and make it easier to expose additional services (e.g. Grafana, Loki) over the tailnet.

title: Emit JSON structured logs from server, worker, and client
description:
The Promtail log pipeline expects JSON-formatted log entries (with fields like Timestamp, Level, Message, Exception, SourceContext) but the server currently emits plain-text logs (e.g. `[2026-02-20 14:01:40.271] [INF] [GitHubService] ...`). This means the JSON parsing stage in promtail-config.yml silently falls through, losing structured fields like level, category, and timestamp extraction.

Switch all log outputs to JSON format:
- **Server (ASP.NET)**: Configure Serilog/logging to use a JSON formatter (e.g. CompactJsonFormatter or RenderedCompactJsonFormatter)
- **Worker (TypeScript/Hono)**: Ensure structured JSON logging with consistent field names
- **Client telemetry**: Ensure any client-side telemetry forwarded to the server follows the same JSON schema

This will enable the existing Promtail pipeline to properly extract log levels, timestamps, source contexts, and session IDs for Grafana dashboards.

title: Change default prompt to "plan"
description: Change the default agent prompt selection from 'Build' to 'Plan' in AgentSelector.razor, IssueEdit.razor, and AgentPrompts.razor modal

title: Fix client and worker logging
description: Update the Grafana dashboards to show the client and worker logs. Currently the worker logs and client telemetry panels are empty. Review the overall Grafana dashboard setup and suggest improvements that can be made. The current dashboard mostly shows logs in JSON.

title: PR status is always 'Checks running'
description: When looking at any PR status in the client the status is always 'checks running'. The PullRequestWorkflowService was not populating ChecksPassing, IsApproved, ApprovalCount, and ChangesRequestedCount on PullRequestInfo objects.

title: Add buttons to issue rows for editing issues and starting agents
description:
In the issue rows on the project page, add inline buttons to edit the issue and run agents on the right hand side. These should show up on hover and selection only.

The run agent should open a context panel that contains the agent control panel (i.e. the ability to start an agent with a particular prompt)

title: Consolidate icon usage to Bootstrap Icons
description: The codebase currently uses a mix of inline SVG icons and Bootstrap Icons. Consolidate all icon usage to use Bootstrap Icons consistently throughout the application.

title: Add single container mode for mini prompts and execution mode
description: Instead of the local agent execution mode that uses the Claude Agent SDK local library, add a new worker container in the Docker conpose that starts alongside the Homespun container. This should be used for all mini prompts. Get rid of the local agent execution mode and replace it with a single container agent execution mode that just sends all queries to that single container (rather than the Docker execution mode that starts new containers). Update the start script as required.

title: Update Grafana dashboard to collect worker logs separately
description: Worker logs are being captured by the PLG logging stack but are not being shown in the dashboard under "Worker logs". Update the worker logs to log these properly.

title: Limit number of levels shown in task graph
description:
When rendering the task graph, only show the tasks up to a depth of three. If there are additional parent tasks, show a little faded continuation indicator on the bottom or side of the node (depending on whether it is leading to a series or parallel parent).

Add buttons and [ ] keyboard shortcuts to increase and decrease the number of levels shown on the graph.

title: Type change menu doesn't appear when badge is clicked
description: The type change menu (where the issue type can be changed after an issue's type badge is clicked) doesn't open when the type badge is clicked. Add comprehensive test coverage for the type change menu functionality in TaskGraphView component to show that it doesn't appear, then try to fix this.

title: Fix thinking messages from AG-UI
description:
When thinking messages are streamed in, they tend to be streamed in at the top, just under the chat message. Instead of this, there should be a thinking style message that is appended between tool usages. It should be open when first streamed in and then collapsed when the next tool messages come in.

Fixed by:
1. Sorting content blocks by Index when rendering to ensure correct order
2. Auto-collapsing thinking blocks when a new tool starts streaming
3. Auto-collapsing thinking blocks when a tool block is received

title: Fix design page
description:
When I open the /design page it immediately crashes, with the following error message logged:

net_http_message_not_success_statuscode_reason, 500, Internal Server Error

   at System.Net.Http.HttpResponseMessage.EnsureSuccessStatusCode()
   at System.Net.Http.Json.HttpClientJsonExtensions.<<FromJsonAsyncCore>g__Core|12_0>d`2[[System.Collections.Generic.List`1[[Homespun.Shared.Models.Design.ComponentMetadata, Homespun.Shared, Version=1.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null]], System.Private.CoreLib, Version=10.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=7cec85d7bea7798e],[System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions, System.Text.Json, Version=10.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=cc7b13ffcd2ddd51]].MoveNext()
   at Homespun.Client.Services.HttpComponentRegistryService.InitializeAsync()
   at Homespun.Client.Pages.Design.DesignIndex.OnInitializedAsync()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ComponentBase.RunInitAndSetParametersAsync()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.RenderTree.Renderer.GetErrorHandledTask(Task , ComponentState )

Please review and fix.

title: Add priority markers to the graph view
description: The priorities of issues should be shown. Use the new graph sort features of the issue service provided by the Fleece Core library to group the "next" issues by priority. Colour the vertical line (which is currently grey) in the first lane with the priority of the "next" issues in each group, ranging from P4 to P0.

title: Fix agent run button
description: The agent run button on each issue row doesn't do anything when clicked. It should open a small context pane that contains the normal agent run control panel for running an agent. Please review and fix.

title: Fix plan status
description:
Occasionally an agent will emit additional "plan pending" messages **after** a plan has been approved and is already being executed. Logs are below showing this, although the agent was clearly working in the UI while the agent was executing tools and sending back thought messages.

Please add additional logs around this behaviour to diagnose further. Include additional logs on the worker container when messages are being sent to the server. Ideally the latest message should be used to set the worker status (i.e. if it is a tool use or a thinking message, it should be 'working', if it is a plan pending or question it is set appropriately and 'waiting' is only set when the "subtype='success'" message appears.

Add logs for when the worker changes it status, i.e. "Worker status changed from 'waiting' to 'working'" so I can also check that the client is successfully pulling the statuses live from the worker rather than storing them.

title: Fix tab key for issue levels
Fix selection after new issue creation
description: When a new issue is created using the inline editor, the selection goes all the way back to the top. Instead of this, the newly created issue should be selected. Additionally, the IssueDetailPanel doesn't show up when the item is selected - the issue details should be reloaded by the client such that new issues are treated similar to the previous issues immediately upon creation.

title: Fix display of edges with new issue creation
Fix log level parsing for server logs
description:
In Grafana the client and worker logs show the log level clearly (e.g. Info, Error, etc.). The server logs, however, do not parse this correctly and they log level is not shown even though it is being captured in the "Parsed Field" as LogLevel.

The lines in the All Logs are also difficult to read - they should be colour coded to show which are coming from the server and client, and have the Message text more visible. Please try to unify the logging output between the client, server and workers as they all look quite different.

title: Test
When selected issue changes while agent is starting, incorrect message is sent
description: When the 'start' agent button is clicked, it takes a few seconds for the agent to start. If the selected issue is changed during this time, the correct branch is cloned and the agent is started, but the message from the new selected issue is sent to the agent. Fix this such that the correct message is always sent from the issue that the agent is started on.

title: Claude Code sometimes kills session shell when trying to test mock container
description: When Claude Code uses mock.sh to run the application and then tries to close the browser, it inadvertently ends the Claude Code process by killing the wrong shell.

title: Unify session and container status
description: The session status shown in the status indicator at the top right of the page and on the Sessions page doesn't align with the container status shown on the Containers page. The Containers page status is always correct, while the Session status can be incorrect, particularly in the case of holding on to 'plan pending' and 'waiting' statuses for too long (i.e. while the agent is actually working). Remove the logic that the sessions are using to calculate their own status, and instead use the status that is being reported by the agent container, as it is correct. Given that there is one session per container, unify the /sessions and /containers pages to be just the /sessions page - I believe the only additional feature that would be required is to show the container ID in the sessions page, as the 'stop session' already kills the container.

title: Remove legacy pre-A2A and AGUI messaging
description: The application was migrated from using a proprietary messaging format between the client, server and worker containers to using A2A and AG-UI. Remove all legacy code relating to this messaging as it is no longer required for backwards compatibility.

title: Fix log level parsing for server logs
description: In Grafana the client and worker logs show the log level clearly (e.g. Info, Error, etc.). The server logs, however, do not parse this correctly and they log level is not shown even though it is being captured in the Parsed Field as LogLevel. The lines in the All Logs are also difficult to read - they should be colour coded to show which are coming from the server and client, and have the Message text more visible. Please try to unify the logging output between the client, server and workers as they all look quite different.

title: Add undo feature
description:
Add the ability to undo and redo changes to the issues list. Whenever any changes are made to issues (i.e. adding and editing them), the issues list should be asynchronously persisted in the projects/{project}/history/ folder as timestamped JSONL files using the Fleece library serialization capabilities. An undo and redo button should be added to the project page UI to allow the user to go backwards and forwards in the history, updating the graph view as required. The current timestamp that is being used for the issues should also be saved such that the application is aware where in the history the user is. If the history is at a previous timestamp to the latest one and any changes are made, this should result in all of the history between the "active" timestamp and the latest timestamp being deleted, and a new timestamp added at the end of the history.

Note that merging issues should be considered as an item in the history.

title: Move from in-memory store to Postgres database
Review bugs where issues are being deleted
Test additional permutations of new issue hierarchy creation
description:
I want to add additional tests for the creation of new issues in the graph view to ensure that they are added in the correct location in the hierarchy. Currently, new issues are added correctly when added as parents to existing issues or when they are added between two issues that are in series.

See below for documentation on the expected behaviour and tests to be added. Add this to the docs/ folder as well.

# Task Graph Issue Insertion Behaviour

## Purpose

This document specifies how new issues are inserted into the task graph dependency diagram. It is intended for test writers implementing tests against `KeyboardNavigationService` and `TaskGraphView`.

**Source files:**
- `src/Homespun.Client/Services/IKeyboardNavigationService.cs` — `PendingNewIssue` model (properties below)
- `src/Homespun.Client/Services/KeyboardNavigationService.cs` — `CreateIssueBelow`, `CreateIssueAbove`, `IndentAsChild`, `UnindentAsSibling`, `GetInheritedParentInfo`, `AcceptEditAsync`
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Requests/IssueRequests.cs` — `CreateIssueRequest` fields sent to the API
- `tests/Homespun.Tests/Services/KeyboardNavigationServiceTests.cs` — existing test patterns

## Diagram Legend

All diagrams use a text-based notation to represent the task graph:

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `o` | An issue node |
| `\|` | A vertical edge connecting a child (above) to a sibling or parent (below) |
| `+--o` | A right-angle connector from a child chain to a parent node |
| `x--o` | Same as `+--o` but indicates the new issue is being inserted into this edge |
| blank line | A gap between unrelated issues (no parent-child relationship) |

Children render **above** their parents in the task graph. Lane 0 is the leftmost column (leaf/child nodes); higher lanes are further right (parent/root nodes). "Above" and "below" in this document always refer to **visual position** on screen (render order), not hierarchy level.

## Keyboard Controls

| Key | Effect | Mode Transition |
|-----|--------|-----------------|
| `o` | Create new issue **below** the selected issue | `Viewing` → `CreatingNew` |
| `Shift+O` | Create new issue **above** the selected issue | `Viewing` → `CreatingNew` |
| `TAB` | Make the new issue a **parent** of the reference issue | Stays in `CreatingNew` |
| `Shift+TAB` | Make the new issue a **child** of the reference issue | Stays in `CreatingNew` |
| `Enter` | Accept the new issue (calls `AcceptEditAsync`) | `CreatingNew` → `Viewing` |
| `Escape` | Cancel and discard the pending issue | `CreatingNew` → `Viewing` |

The **reference issue** is the issue that was selected when `o` or `Shift+O` was pressed.

## Code Properties Quick-Reference

When a new issue is created, the `PendingNewIssue` properties determine what gets sent to the API in `CreateIssueRequest`:

| Action | `PendingChildId` | `PendingParentId` | `InheritedParentIssueId` | API: `ChildIssueId` | API: `ParentIssueId` | API: `ParentSortOrder` |
|--------|-------------------|--------------------|--------------------------|-----------------------|-----------------------|------------------------|
| Default (no TAB) | `null` | `null` | From `GetInheritedParentInfo` | `null` | = `InheritedParentIssueId` | = `InheritedParentSortOrder` |
| TAB | = `ReferenceIssueId` | `null` | `null` (cleared) | = `PendingChildId` | `null` | `null` |
| Shift+TAB | `null` | = `ReferenceIssueId` | `null` (cleared) | `null` | = `PendingParentId` | `null` |

**`GetInheritedParentInfo` logic:** Looks up the reference issue's parent. If the reference issue has a parent, the new issue inherits that parent (sibling creation). If the reference issue has no parent, returns `null` for both fields (orphan/standalone creation).

All issues in this document are drawn as "series" issues. For "parallel" issues the same parent/child rules apply — only the sort order computation differs.

---

## 1. Default (Sibling) — no TAB / Shift+TAB

New issue is created at the same hierarchy level as the reference issue. The new issue inherits the reference issue's parent (if any) via `GetInheritedParentInfo`.

### 1.1 Between two children

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
| <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2
o Child issue 2
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

```
o Child issue 1
|
o New issue
|
o Child issue 2
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentSortOrder` = midpoint between Child issue 1 and Child issue 2 sort orders

### 1.2 Between a child and parent

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
| <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Parent issue
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
o New issue
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentSortOrder` = computed sort order after Child issue 2

### 1.3 Between an orphan and a child hierarchy

This depends on whether the new issue is created below the orphan or above the child.

#### Location

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
  <-- insert here: `Shift+O` above Child issue 1, or `o` below Next issue
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 1

The reference issue is Child issue 1, which has Parent issue as its parent. `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns Parent issue. The new issue becomes a sibling of Child issue 1 under Parent issue.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)

o New issue
|
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"

#### Expected result — `o` below Next issue

The reference issue is Next issue, which has no parent. `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns `null`. The new issue is an orphan.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)

o New issue (orphan)

o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = `null`
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 1.4 After a parent and before an orphan

The reference issue determines the behaviour. The parent issue has no parent of its own, so whether inserting below it or above the orphan, the new issue is an orphan.

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
      <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue, or `Shift+O` above Next issue
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Expected result

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue

o New issue (orphan)

o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = `null` (Parent issue has no parent; Next issue has no parent)
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 1.5 Between two adjacent hierarchies

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
  <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue 1

The reference issue is Parent issue 1. Parent issue 1 has no parent, so `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns `null`. The new issue is an orphan.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1

o New issue (orphan)

o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = `null`
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1

The reference issue is Child issue 2.1. Its parent is Parent issue 2, so `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns Parent issue 2. The new issue becomes a sibling of Child issue 2.1 under Parent issue 2.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1

o New issue
|
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue 2"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue 2"

---

## 2. TAB (Become Parent) — new issue becomes parent of reference

Pressing `TAB` after `o`/`Shift+O` sets `PendingChildId = ReferenceIssueId`. The new issue becomes a **parent** of the reference issue. All previously inherited parent info is cleared.

### 2.1 Between two children

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 3
o Child issue 3
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

The new issue becomes the parent of the reference issue. This breaks the relationship between Parent issue and all children above the insertion point (tree behaviour).

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
+--o New issue

o Child issue 3
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = reference issue ID (Child issue 2 or Child issue 3)
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 2.2 Between a child and parent

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Parent issue
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

The new issue becomes parent of the reference issue. Parent issue loses its children; the new issue inherits them.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
+--o New issue

o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 2.3 Between an orphan and a child hierarchy

#### Location

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
  <-- insert here: `Shift+O` above Child issue 1, or `o` below Next issue
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 1 + TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` Creating a parent above a root-level child makes no sense — the new issue would be inserted between two unrelated hierarchies with no children of its own in that context.

#### Expected result — `o` below Next issue + TAB

The new issue becomes parent of Next issue.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
|
+--o New issue

o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = "Next issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = "Next issue"

### 2.4 After a parent and before an orphan

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
      <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue, or `Shift+O` above Next issue
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue + TAB

The new issue becomes parent of Parent issue.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
   |
   +--o New issue (parent of Parent issue)

o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = "Parent issue"

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Next issue + TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` Creating a parent above a root-level orphan with no children makes no structural sense.

### 2.5 Between two adjacent hierarchies

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
  <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue 1 + TAB

The new issue becomes parent of Parent issue 1.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
   |
   +--o New issue (parent of Parent issue 1)

o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = "Parent issue 1"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = "Parent issue 1"

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1 + TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` The new issue would be inserted between two unrelated hierarchies. Child issue 2.1 already has a parent (Parent issue 2), and the new issue would have no children in this context.

---

## 3. Shift+TAB (Become Child) — new issue becomes child of reference

Pressing `Shift+TAB` after `o`/`Shift+O` sets `PendingParentId = ReferenceIssueId`. The new issue becomes a **child** of the reference issue. All previously inherited parent info is cleared.

### 3.1 Between two children

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 3
o Child issue 3
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

The new issue becomes a child of the reference issue.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
o New issue
|
+--o Child issue 3
   |
   +--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = reference issue ID (Child issue 2 or Child issue 3)
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = `null`

### 3.2 Between a child and parent

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Parent issue
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` The parent cannot have a child two levels down — the new issue would need to become a grandchild, which is not supported by a single Shift+TAB operation.

### 3.3 Between an orphan and a child hierarchy

#### Location

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
  <-- insert here: `Shift+O` above Child issue 1, or `o` below Next issue
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 1 + Shift+TAB

The new issue becomes a child of Child issue 1.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)

o    New issue (child of Child issue 1)
|
+--o Child issue 1
   |
   o Child issue 2
   |
   x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = "Child issue 1"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Child issue 1"

#### Expected result — `o` below Next issue + Shift+TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` It doesn't make sense in context to the orphan Next issue — the new issue would become a child of an orphan, but the visual position (between two unrelated groups) is ambiguous.

### 3.4 After a parent and before an orphan

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
      <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue, or `Shift+O` above Next issue
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue + Shift+TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` There could be many levels to travel down from Parent issue.

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Next issue + Shift+TAB

The new issue becomes a child of Next issue.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue

o New issue (child of Next issue)
|
+--o Next issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = "Next issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Next issue"

### 3.5 Between two adjacent hierarchies

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
  <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue 1 + Shift+TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` There could be many levels to travel down from Parent issue 1 — the system cannot determine the correct depth.

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1 + Shift+TAB

The new issue becomes a child of Child issue 2.1.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1

o New issue (child of Child issue 2.1)
|
+--o Child issue 2.1
   |
   +--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = "Child issue 2.1"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Child issue 2.1"

---

## 4. Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behaviour |
|----------|-------------------|
| Empty graph (no issues) | `CreateIssueBelow`/`CreateIssueAbove` returns immediately (`SelectedIndex < 0`) — no issue created |
| Single issue in graph | Normal behaviour — `o` creates at index 1 (end), `Shift+O` at index 0 (start) |
| TAB then Shift+TAB (switching) | Mutually exclusive — Shift+TAB overwrites TAB state. No way to return to default sibling mode without `Escape` + restart |
| Pressing TAB twice | Idempotent — `PendingChildId` is set to the same reference issue ID again |
| Escape during creation | Clears `PendingNewIssue`, returns to `Viewing` mode, no API call |
| Empty title then Enter | `AcceptEditAsync` returns without creating, stays in `CreatingNew` mode |
| Series vs Parallel parents | Same parent/child rules apply; only difference is sort order computation for sibling creation |

---

## Summary of Blocked Scenarios

All "blocked" scenarios mean the key press is a **no-op** — nothing happens. The code does not currently implement blocking validation in `IndentAsChild()` or `UnindentAsSibling()`. These are marked `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` throughout this document. Test writers should write TDD-style failing tests for these scenarios.

| Section | Scenario | Reason |
|---------|----------|--------|
| 2.3 | `Shift+O` above root child + TAB | New issue would have no children |
| 2.4 | `Shift+O` above orphan + TAB | New issue would have no children |
| 2.5 | `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1 + TAB | New issue would be between unrelated hierarchies with no children |
| 3.2 | Both directions + Shift+TAB | Cannot create grandchild in one operation |
| 3.3 | `o` below orphan + Shift+TAB | Ambiguous context between unrelated groups |
| 3.4 | `o` below parent + Shift+TAB | Many levels to travel down |
| 3.5 | `o` below Parent issue 1 + Shift+TAB | Many levels to travel down |

title: Show closed PRs as branched off
description: In the 'merged PRs' section at the top of the projects page, the closed PRs should be shown as branched off the main vertical line (i.e. one lane to the right, with e a connector).

title: Tab/Shift+Tab closes inline issue creation input
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls (o/O), pressing Tab or Shift+Tab to change the lane position closes the inline input instead of adjusting the lane.

## Root Cause
In InlineIssueCreateInput.razor, the Tab key is handled but the browser's default Tab behavior is not prevented. This causes focus to move away from the input, triggering HandleBlur which cancels the issue creation.

## Expected Behavior
- Tab should increase the lane offset (making new issue a parent of adjacent issue)
- Shift+Tab should decrease the lane offset (making new issue a child of adjacent issue)
- The inline input should remain focused and visible

## Actual Behavior
- Tab/Shift+Tab causes the input to lose focus
- HandleBlur is triggered, canceling the creation
- No lane adjustment occurs

## Fix Required
Add @onkeydown:preventDefault for Tab keys OR call e.preventDefault() in the JavaScript interop for Tab key handling in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor.

## Related Tests
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs document this bug and will fail when fixed.

title: New issues created with Tab/Shift+Tab don't establish parent-child relationships
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls with Tab/Shift+Tab lane modifiers, the parent-child relationships are not being established correctly.

## Expected Behavior

### When creating BELOW selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue above)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue above)

### When creating ABOVE selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue below)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue below)

## Current Status
This bug cannot be tested until PUgrNi (Tab closes input) is fixed.

## Implementation Notes
- The lane offset is tracked in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor
- The CreateIssueAsync call needs to include the parent/child relationship based on lane offset
- For Series execution mode, sort order should also be considered

## Test Coverage
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs include tests for relationship verification that will execute once PUgrNi is fixed.

title: Change ring around issue nodes to indicate working agents
description: A ring is currently shown around issue nodes in the task graph view of the React client to show the items that are "next". Instead of this, I would like the ring to represent the issues that have agents currently working on them, with the colour representing the agent status.

title: Add 'Enter' shortcut to open selected issues for editing
description: When an issue is selected and not in "edit" mode, hitting "Enter" should open the edit page for that issue.

title: Add keyboard shortcuts
Add 'e' shortcut to open run agent panel
description: When an issue is selected and the edit mode isn't on, pressing the 'e' key should open the run agent panel that allows users to select a prompt and run an agent.

title: Add 's' shortcut to open status editing panel
description: When in 'select' mode, the 's' key should open a panel that allows the current issue status to be selected (similar to the type selection panel).

title: Remove horizontal edges between the base line and siblings of next issues
description: When there is a "next" issue with multiple siblings in series, a horizontal line connects all of the sibling issues with the root edge (the vertical line in the leftmost lane). This is not really correct as the issues below the first sibling are blocked from being executed next - only show a horizontal line connecting the root edge with the topmost issue which is definitively marked "next". This does not apply to the parallel issues.

title: Inline issue details panel
description: Issue rows should be able to expand when double clicked, showing all of the information in the current issue detail panel such as the PR status, description and so on. The issue detail panel should be refactored to suit this control usage, as we will not be using the detail panel in it's current position anymore.

title: Remove issue details panel
description: Make the issue details panel a collapsible side bar rather than a floating panel that scrolls down with the screen. It should collapse on the right and be collapsed by default. Only open when an issue is clicked (not selected with the keyboard controls).

title: Add 'Tab' shortcut to change issue type
description: Hitting 'Tab' on an issue when it is selected and in 'select' mode should cycle through the issue types. Add a 3 second debounce to this.

title: ExitPlanMode hangs when the plan is written locally
Add single container mode for mini prompts and execution mode
description: Add a worker sidecar container that runs alongside the main Homespun container to handle mini prompts. Route mini prompt requests to the sidecar via HTTP instead of using the ClaudeAgentSdk C# library.

title: Show all issues that have open PRs regardless of status
description: When an issue is matched to a PR, the issue should always be shown in the graph regardless of its current status.

title: Update branch ID in issue when an agent is run
Add drag and drop for issue rearrangement
Get worker container status from last message
description: The container statuses are quite flaky. Review the way that statuses are held in the worker containers. They should be determined by the last message - i.e. if the last message is pending plan the status should be "awaiting plan", when the last message is "success" the status should be "awaiting input" and so on. Review the message types in the Claude Agent SDK documentation and put together a plan to fix the status endpoint on the container.

title: Float menu bar at top of page over contents
description: The menu bar should float at the top of the page over the page contents, and remain in place as the user scrolls.

title: Add controls for editing execution mode
Set up Grafana MCP
Update the Grafana dashboards to filter by issue
description: For worker containers and relevant parts of the server and client, add an IssueId field to logs that contains the issue ID related to the log. The aim of this is to allow the Grafana dashboards to be filtered to only the logs relating to particular issues.

title: Back to sessions button when session isn't found leads to "agents" page
description: When the session can't be found, a button saying "back to agents" is shown. Clicking this links to the agent prompts page rather than the sessions page. Fix this.

title: Make "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" consistent
description:
The titles "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" are used inconsistently throughout the application.

- Prompts should refer to the actual prompt "build", "plan", "rebase", etc. that are injected into the session when it is started. The label on the AgentControlPanel and in the left side bar should be "Prompt", rather than "Agent".
- Agents refer to a running instance of Claude Code on an issue. This should be used to replace nearly all of the "Sessions" text throughout.
- Sessions refer to a particular conversation thread that is either currently running or has been run on an agent. There will be very little user interaction with individual sessions, as the agents tend to only require one or two sessions to complete their work and this is mostly managed by the instances of Claude Code.

Put together a plan to make this terminology consistent throughout the application.

Be very careful with links between pages - after updating addresses ensure that the correct reference is used (e.g. if the /sessions/ page is changed to /agents/, change all instances of these links.

title: Make session control consistent
description: The session control differs between the agents page on the project and the global agents page. It should just be a single Blazor component that is used on both pages. The only real difference between the two pages is that one is filtered for just the agents related to a project and the other has all agents but is grouped by project.

title: Fix secret injection
description: The secrets page on project is allowing me to successfully save secrets to environments, but they don't appear to be injected into worker containers as environment variables. Please review the container creation code to confirm they are being included.

title: Fix inline agent run panel
description: When using the inline agent run panel, selecting a prompt and running it successfully creates a new session but it doesn't inject the first prompt in the same way that running an agent from the issue details panel does. Review this and ensure that the functionality is the same and on the basis that the agent run panel uses Opus as the default model and selects main as the base branch.

title: Fix PR badge link from sessions to session
description: The PR agent status badge links to "/sessions/{id}" rather than "/session/{id}", which is the correct link. Fix this.

title: Restart container on sessions
description: When an agent enters an error state, there should be an option to restart the worker container on the same session. This button should stop the worker container, create a new worker container and start it on the same session (having persisted the Claude Session ID from the container). Provide a /test/error endpoint to the worker containers so that we can force an error and have the server restart the container with the same session.

title: Fix display of plan controls
description:
The "execute plan" controls are often shown whether or not there is actually an active plan for execution. Review the underlying code that shows and hides the plan controls. It should be updated to be based on the agent worker container status, which checks whether the last message was a plan pending message. When a new message comes through from the agent this will update the agent status to "working" or something else, and the UI should remove the plan execution control.

Confirm that this will work to remove the plan controls without excessively polling the agent containers for their status.

title: In-session prompts don't inject issue context
description: When selecting a prompt from the list in a session, the issue context isn't injected into the placeholders when it is copied into the chat input. Update this so that it is.

title: Migrate template tools from PyRevit to Taipan
description: There are some tools used to manage standard details models in Revit in the repository at https://github.com/Northrop-Digital/nr-template. These have been written as PyRevit scripts. Clone them to a temporary folder that is ignored in the .gitignore (e.g. /.tmp) and migrate them into Revit commands. Use the existing commands in the folder as a guide.

title: Migrate standard details tools from PyRevit to Taipan
description:
There are some tools used to manage standard details models in Revit in the repository at https://github.com/Northrop-Digital/nr-standard-details. These have been written as PyRevit scripts. Clone them to a temporary folder that is ignored in the .gitignore (e.g. /.tmp) and migrate them into Revit commands. Use the existing commands in the folder as a guide. 

Create a Taipan configuration file in the %APPDATA%/Northrop/Applications.Taipan as a feature flag for this tool - it should be installed with Taipan but the buttons should only be visible for a certain group of maintenance users.

title: Hide template tools behind feature flag
Just have "plan" and "build" permissions
description: There are currently four options for permissions settings that match the options in Claude Code. Instead of this, simplify the permissions settings to just two: "Build", which maps to "Bypass Permissions" and "Plan", which maps to "Plan".

title: Persist permissions per-session and allow them to change mid-session
description:
The permissions associated with a session (i.e. the "session mode" which can be either "Build" or "Plan") is a property of the agent/worker container, and can actually be changed mid-prompt (i.e. is not necessarily sent with every prompt). Currently these seem to be persisted at the client level, and when changed in one session page changes when another session page is navigated to.

Instead of this, I would like the session mode to be pulled as part of the session information. When this session mode is changed, I would like this to be passed down to the server and then to the worker container immediately, without requiring a new prompt to be sent. There should be server and worker endpoints available to perform this function.

title: Add issue tree context to prompt
description:
Add a new {context} placeholder to prompts so that a prompt can show an issue's children and parents for context.

Only the direct children should be shown for the issue, but all of the parents and ancestors should be shown. This would look something like:

- v9n23k [feature] [open] Parent 1
  - fk28rh [feature] [open] Parent 2
    - pzk048 [task] [open] Parent 3
      - jfh481 [task] [progress] This issue
         - ruw192 [bug] [complete] Child 1
         - bfh293 [task] [complete] Child 2

Each row should include the type and status of the issue.

title: Fix page width on mobile
description: The page width on mobile is being stretched to show the full width of the item description, which allows both horizontal and vertical scrolling. Instead of this, the width should be restricted to the width of the screen. Vertical and horizontal scrolling should be in place just for the issue graph, with the top bar and bottom create issue control fixed in place at full width.

title: Don't auto open issues panel on mobile - add a button
description:
When an issue is tapped on mobile the side bar opens immediately, costing the screen. This shouldn't happen - add an extra open button next to the edit button that only shows up on mobile. This is the button that will need to be tapped for the panel to open. Otherwise, the panel should remain closed.

Also, remove the exit button from the issue details side panel and put a open/close button in the title bar. This can replace the dark mode button, which can be moved to the settings.

This button should always be there, just disabled on the pages that don't have a side bar.

title: Dont show issue ID on mobile
description: The issue row is already a little crowded on mobile - remove the issue ID on mobile display to leave more room for the issue title.

title: Fix PR status
description: The PR status is always shown as "Checks running". There was a previous PR that attempted to fix this but it didn't quite work. Please review again and let me know if there are any logs that you think would be worthwhile checking in this process.

title: Add 'move to parent' and 'move to child' feature
description: Add controls that allow moving an issue to be a child of another issue or to be a parent of another issue. This should move the entire issue tree (which should be accomplished simply by changing the parent of either the first or second selected element). Add these as buttons in the toolbar that has been recently added above the task graph view.

title: Add buttons for creating new inline issues on mobile
description: On mobile we are currently relying on the issue creation bar at the bottom of the screen for creating new issues. Instead of this, add buttons to the issue row control at the top and bottom of the row that provides the ability to create issues above (what would usually be done with 'shift+o') and below (what would usually be done with 'o') the currently selected issue. This should open a new inline issue editor, which should be updated with OK (shortcut: 'Enter'), OK and edit description (shortcut: 'Shift+Enter', not currently implemented on the inline control but is implemented on the issue creation bar) and cancel (shortcut: 'Escape') on the right side of the bar.

title: PR checks don't show merge status
description: PR checks now correctly show whether checks are passing or not, but doesn't show whether the PR can be merged (i.e. if there are merge conflicts). Update this to be included in the PR status check

title: Remove the Git Graph view
description: There is a legacy "git graph" view of the issues (rather than the current default task graph view). Remove it.

title: Show placeholders for PR status
Issue details side panel should squash the main view on desktop, not overlay it
description: The issue details side panel currently overlays the main view when it is open, hiding the PR and edit buttons on desktop. It should instead reduce the width of the display on desktop and only overlay the main view on mobile screens.

title: Show dotted extension line only for hidden issues
description: When the limited task graph depth was introduced in PR #352, dashed lines were added below all issues that had parent issues hidden by the depth limit. However, this ended up being added for all issues that had any ancestor hidden and not just the direct children of the hidden issues. Fix this.

title: Inline agent run panel starts session but doesn't include prompt
description: When I start an agent using the inline run panel (the run button on the issue row) it starts the agent but doesn't inject the prompt into the session. The issue detail panel works well, investigate the differences between the inline agent run and the agent control panel in the issue detail panel. We should just have one panel UI component - make the "model" and "base branch" parts collapsed by default and just show the prompt selection dropdown and run button in the new control and use it in all locations that agents can be run from.

title: Create toolbar
Add file name and PR search
description:
When the user types @ or # into the chat box or description for editing an issue, this should bring up a modal window that allows the user to browse the repository files (with @) or cached PRs (with #).

The repository file search should be a simple text match of the file names, and the PRs should be similar. A list of matches from best to worst with a limit of 20 should be shown. When OK or enter is pressed, this should add "@{file path from repo root}" or "PR #{number}" to the prompt at the location where the key was first pressed.

The list of files and list of PRs should be asynchronously loaded into the client on page load and cached, and the search should happen on the client. The cache should be hashed to check when it should be updated by the server (which should also maintain a hash of the content).

title: Fix branch name when branches are created
Select on search
description: The highlighting of issues that match the search query happens when "ENTER" is pressed. Instead of this, they should be highlighted when the input text changes, and the selection of the first element should happen when "ENTER" is pressed. Currently pressing ENTER doesn't select any issues at all, and focus on the main task graph view is lost.

title: Add toolbar to top of page and move edit and run controls
description:
Add a toolbar to the top of the project page, just below the tabs. The project title and toolbar should be sticky when scrolled down, but the tabs can be scrolled off screen.

This toolbar should have small icon buttons on it for the main actions. Hovering over the buttons should show a tooltip with an explanation and keyboard shortcuts.

For now, just move the inline edit and run controls to the toolbar. These should still be displayed inline with the issue on desktop but should be hidden on mobile to leave more room for the issue title.

title: Add "create above" and "create below" to toolbar
description: Add buttons to the toolbar for creating issues above and below the selected issue. This is usually triggered by the 'o' and 'shift+o' keys on a desktop application, but should be able to be used on mobile with buttons. Choose appropriate icons for the buttons.

title: Remove quick issue creation bar
description: With the creation of the new toolbar buttons and inline issue creation for creating new issues on mobile, remove the quick issue creation bar completely from the repository as it is no longer needed.

title: Show current branch commit in agent session panel and on branches page
Set worker mode from prompt setting
description: The agent prompts have a "mode" setting that can be either "Build" or "Plan". It appears for the current Claude implementation that the "BypassPermissions" mode (that maps to "Plan") is always used when the agent is started. The "Plan" mode should be passed in when the prompt mode is "Plan". Please review and fix.

title: New messages not shown in session window when sent
description:
When a message is sent to the worker agent from the UI (say to answer a question or execute a plan) the following messages are not being streamed back from the server over SignalR unless the page is refreshed. Please review the set up of the session page to ensure that all chat messages are being streamed back in live from the agent worker when the session page is working.

This should have been fixed by PR #559 but the messages are still not streaming into the chat window when I open one that is running.

title: Allow type to be changed in inline issue creator
Consolidate toolbar buttons
description: There are multiple different buttons in three different locations that are all intended to be in the same toolbar. The "create above", "create below" and "move up", "move down" buttons are in the correct location. Move all other buttons to this location, group them with a new `ToolbarSeparator` component and make their styling all similar. The Edit and Run buttons have the correct button and tooltip styling.

title: Update agent page UI
Make page height full size
Add go to next agent button
description: Next to the back button in the agent session page at the top left hand corner of the page there should be a next button that takes the user to the next agent session that is not in a "running" state.

title: Improve tool output view
description:
Currently the tool usage and tool output view don't show any of the internal details of the input and output of the tool usage, and are presented as two separate chat bubbles. I believe that these results are sent through from the server and this is mostly a UI issue.

Tool usage and tool results should be contained within a single message bubble, and multiple subsequent uses of tools (without interrupting chat messages) should be combined into a single bubble that show the number of tool usages. It should show the latest two usages that can be expanded to show all of the grouped tool usages. The tool usages should just show up on one line with a basic output, and be expandable to show the full details of the tool usage. Refer to the Homespun.Client Blazor application for an example of this being implemented reasonably well, then port this over to the Homespun.Web React application.

title: Use Blazor Blueprint
description:
Install the Blazor Blueprint MCP on the client workers by executing this command after Claude is installed:

claude mcp add blazorblueprint --transport stdio -- npx -y @blazorblueprint/mcp@latest --scope user

Consider whether this should go in the Dockerfile or the application.

This should install the MCP server and allow it to be used by the workers building Homespun. Also install the library to the client application using the instructions here: https://blazorblueprintui.com/docs/installation

title: Install C# LSP on workers
description:
Add the following commands to the Dockerfile to install the C# LSP and Claude code plugins:

dotnet tool install --global csharp-ls
claude plugin install csharp-lsp@claude-plugins-official

title: Migrate to Blazor Blueprint
description: Migrate all standard components over to Blazor Blueprint while still maintaining the custom theme that was used in the application (specifically the colours). Use the Blazor Blueprint MCP tools for assistance. Update the design system at /design to suit.

title: Add "move up" and "move down" buttons for series siblings
description: When a sibling is selected, "move up" and "move down" buttons should become available in the toolbar above the task graph view. This should allow the issue to be moved up and down the list of siblings, functionality which should be available in the Fleece issue service.

title: Prompts selector shows double the number of prompts
Fix permissions mode on agent launch
description: When an agent is launched using the agent control panel, the permissions mode is not set to the mode from the selected prompt but seems to always default to "BypassPermissions" or "Build". This has specifically just happened for the default "Plan" prompt.

title: Use general model versions
description:
A specific model version is being passed in on SDK initialisation, as can be seen in the worker logs:

```
SDK init: permissionMode='bypassPermissions', model='claude-opus-4-5-20251101'
```

This would seemingly start the agent with an older model, as Opus 4.6 is now available. The model passed in should always just be 'opus', 'sonnet' or 'haiku' to allow Claude Code to select the latest version. There are also some messages that show just 'opus', so it is possible that the correct model is being passed in:

```
POST /sessions - mode=Build, model=opus, workingDirectory=/workdir, resumeSessionId=none
```

However, generally speaking just fix this such that 'opus' is always used without versions to be on the safe side.

title: Control application containers with Komodo
description: Add Komodo for container management with Nerdbank.GitVersioning for automated versioning

title: Add Komodo install script
Tidy up client code
Restart server and reconnect to workers
description: It should be possible to restart the server container and have it reconnect to worker containers that it started running before being restarted. This will need the server to be able to discover worker containers, and for worker containers to locally persist their conversations such that the server can catch up on any messages that happened during the restart.

title: Fix CD action
description: The recent commit to main didn't trigger the CD workflow which should be triggered on all pushes to main to build a new working container. Review this.

title: Pull changes from main before creating a new branch
description: When running a new agent a new branch is created from the default branch. When this is done, the recent changes to the default branch should be pulled from main with the fleece issues merged, using the same action that the "pull" button does on the main project page.

title: Add root page
Add scroll and float to project title and toolbar
description:
The project title and toolbar control on the project issues tab should start in it's current position at the top of the  page but float down when the page is scrolled such that it is always at the top of the screen and visible.

On mobile screens when the toolbar is too small to fit all of the controls on one row it should maintain one row and scroll horizontally.

title: Make nav menu responsive content match sidebar
description: The sidebar is only shown on larger screens, so when a smaller screen is used the responsive nav menu is used to display another sidebar. Change this such that the content between these two is identical as they both represent the same information.

title: Make pull button show background progress
description: When the pull button at the top of the page is clicked it doesn't appear that anything happen. Use a Blazor Blueprint loading symbol while all changes are being pulled.

title: Change notifications to Blazor Blueprint alerts
description: There is a notifications control in the client. Delete this and use the Blazor Blueprint alerts component instead throughout the application. Refer to the Blazor Blueprint documentation for more information.

title: Always show agent status in nav bar
description: The nav bar should always show the agent status on the right hand side even on small screens. The breadcrumbs should have a horizontal scroll to suit, and the main app title should be removed on small screens.

title: Scrolling to bottom of issues tab doesn't work
description: The issues tab isn't able to scroll all the way to the bottom of the page, seemingly due to the way that the project toolbar and heading float is set up. Remove the floating of the heading, I am happy for this to just scroll off screen. However, I do need the project toolbar to stick to the top of the screen when scrolling down, and for it to be possible to scroll all the way down to the bottom of the page. I have also noticed a scrolling issue on the edit issue page, where on mobile the page doesn't scroll all the way to the bottom. Review any reasons why this might be occurring.

title: Port frontend to React+Vite+Tailwind+TanStack Router+TanStack Query+Zustand
description:
The current frontend client application is written using Blazor with the Blazor Blueprint front end library. I would like to instead create a new front end using a Typescript stack, as I am having some performance issues with Blazor.

This port will be quite significant, so I will need to implement it in a number of stages.

**Target Stack:**
- Language: TypeScript
- Frontend Framework: React
- Build Tool: Vite
- UI Components: shadcn/ui
- Styling: Tailwind CSS
- Routing: TanStack Router
- Data Fetching: TanStack Query
- Client State: Zustand
- API Client: OpenAPI-TS (hey-api/openapi-ts)

**Location:** src/Homespun.Web
**Hosting:** Standalone Vite dev server with API proxy to ASP.NET backend
**Migration Strategy:** Coexist with Blazor during development

See .claude/plans/react-frontend-port-plan.md for detailed technical plan.

title: Phase 1: Foundation & Infrastructure
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase establishes the foundational infrastructure for the React frontend port in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **rXJGro** - Project Setup and Toolchain (Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui)
- [ ] **0ApnTK** - OpenAPI Client Generation (hey-api/openapi-ts)
- [ ] **B46NgY** - Core Layout and Routing (TanStack Router)
- [ ] **9S4Xeg** - SignalR Client Setup

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following integration points in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm the Vite dev server starts successfully with `npm run dev`
2. Verify TanStack Router navigation works between routes
3. Confirm OpenAPI client can make API calls to the backend
4. Verify SignalR connection establishes and receives events
5. Check that shadcn/ui components render correctly with Tailwind styling

Mark each child issue checkbox above as verified, then mark this phase complete.

title: Project Setup and Toolchain
description:
Initialize the new React frontend project at src/Homespun.Web with the complete toolchain.

**Tasks:**
1. Create Vite + React + TypeScript project using `npm create vite@latest`
2. Configure Tailwind CSS with the project's design tokens
3. Install and configure shadcn/ui component library
4. Set up TanStack Router with file-based routing structure
5. Configure TanStack Query with default options
6. Set up Zustand store scaffolding
7. Configure ESLint, Prettier, and TypeScript strict mode
8. Add dev and build scripts to package.json
9. Configure Vite proxy to forward /api requests to ASP.NET backend (port 5000)
10. Update docker-compose.yml to include the new frontend service

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `npm run dev` starts the Vite dev server successfully
- `npm run build` produces a production build without errors
- TypeScript compilation has no errors with strict mode
- shadcn/ui components can be imported and rendered
- API requests to /api/* are proxied to the backend

title: OpenAPI Client Generation
description:
Set up OpenAPI-based type generation using hey-api/openapi-ts to create a fully typed API client.

**Tasks:**
1. Verify ASP.NET backend exports OpenAPI JSON at /swagger/v1/swagger.json
2. Install @hey-api/openapi-ts as a dev dependency
3. Create openapi-ts.config.ts configuration file
4. Add `generate:api` npm script to fetch OpenAPI spec and generate client
5. Generate TypeScript types for all API endpoints
6. Create base API client with:
   - Fetch or Axios wrapper
   - Error handling interceptors
   - Authentication header injection (for future use)
7. Verify all 19 API services have generated types

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Running `npm run generate:api` generates TypeScript types in src/api/generated/
- All API endpoints have typed request/response interfaces
- API client includes proper error handling
- Types match the C# DTOs in Homespun.Shared

title: Core Layout and Routing
description:
Create the base application layout and routing structure using TanStack Router.

**Tasks:**
1. Create RootLayout component with:
   - Sidebar (projects navigation, settings link)
   - Header (breadcrumbs, agent status indicator placeholder)
   - Main content area with Outlet
2. Set up TanStack Router routes matching Blazor routes:
   - / - Projects list
   - /projects/new - Create project
   - /projects/$projectId - Project detail
   - /projects/$projectId/issues - Issues tab
   - /projects/$projectId/branches - Branches tab
   - /projects/$projectId/prompts - Prompts tab
   - /projects/$projectId/secrets - Secrets tab
   - /projects/$projectId/settings - Settings tab
   - /projects/$projectId/issues/$issueId/edit - Edit issue
   - /sessions - Sessions list
   - /sessions/$sessionId - Session chat
   - /settings - Global settings
3. Implement ErrorBoundary component
4. Create NotFound (404) page
5. Set up breadcrumb context/hook
