title: Task Graph View
description:
Implement the issues tab with TaskGraphView - the primary visualization for issues on a project.

**Primary Component: TaskGraphView**
The TaskGraphView renders issues in a lane-based hierarchical graph with SVG connectors showing parent-child relationships.

**Tasks:**
1. Create useIssues hook with filtering support (GET /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/graph)
2. Port TaskGraphLayoutService algorithm:
   - Convert TaskGraphResponse into render lines
   - Lane assignment for hierarchical depth
   - Handle hidden parent detection when filtered by depth
   - Series execution mode support (vertical chaining)
3. Build TaskGraphRow component showing:
   - Lane-based positioning with indent
   - SVG connectors to parent/child nodes
   - Issue ID, title, type badge, status badge
   - Execution mode indicator (series chain)
   - Agent status with pulse animation
   - Search term highlighting (yellow border)
4. Implement row selection (click or keyboard nav)
5. Add real-time updates via SignalR IssuesChanged event
6. Handle expand/collapse with Space key or double-click for inline details
7. Support move target selection mode (crosshair cursor)

**Render Line Types:**
- TaskGraphIssueRenderLine (issues with lane, parent lane, execution info)
- TaskGraphPrRenderLine (merged/closed PRs)
- TaskGraphSeparatorRenderLine (divider)
- TaskGraphLoadMoreRenderLine (load more PRs)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Issues display in lane-based graph visualization
- Parent-child relationships shown via SVG connectors
- Series vs parallel execution modes are visually distinct
- Clicking a row selects it for keyboard navigation
- Space/double-click expands inline details
- Color coding by type (Bug=red, Task=blue, Feature=green, Chore=gray)
- New/updated issues appear without page refresh

title: Task Graph Tree View
description:
**ARCHIVED: Merged with Task Graph View (jFBW7H)**

The original plan had separate 'List View' and 'Tree View' components with a toggle between them. The Blazor implementation uses a single unified TaskGraphView that combines both concepts:
- Lane-based positioning provides tree hierarchy visualization
- List-style vertical layout with rows
- No toggle needed - single unified view

The TaskGraphView in jFBW7H now contains all tree visualization functionality.

title: Inline Issue Details
description:

title: Inline Issue Creation
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline issue creation within the task graph using Vim-like keyboard commands.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'bottom bar' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline creation triggered by keyboard commands.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueEditor**
A text input that appears in-place within the task graph when creating/editing an issue.

**Creation Commands (from Viewing mode):**
- `o` - Create issue below current selection
- `Shift+O` - Create issue above current selection

**Editing Commands (while viewing):**
- `i` - Start editing at beginning of title
- `a` - Start editing at end of title
- `r` - Replace title (clear and edit)

**Hierarchy Commands (while editing):**
- `Tab` - Make new issue PARENT of reference (indent)
- `Shift+Tab` - Make new issue SIBLING of reference parent (outdent)

**Editor Actions:**
- `Enter` - Save and return to viewing mode
- `Shift+Enter` - Save and open full edit page (for description)
- `Escape` - Cancel edit, discard changes

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueEditor component with:
   - Text input for title
   - Action buttons (Save, Save+Edit, Cancel)
   - Lane-based positioning matching selected row
2. Implement cursor position control (start, end, replace)
3. Handle Tab/Shift+Tab for hierarchy changes during creation
4. Create useCreateIssue mutation hook
5. Focus management and keyboard event handling
6. Smooth insertion animation
7. Optimistic updates (show new issue immediately)
8. Type cycling with Tab (in viewing mode): Task → Bug → Feature → Chore

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `o`/`O` opens inline editor at correct position
- Tab/Shift+Tab changes hierarchy relationship
- Enter saves, Escape cancels
- New issues appear in graph immediately
- Focus returns to graph after save/cancel

title: Chat Message List
description:
Create the chat message display component using PromptKit components with streaming support.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `ChatContainer` - Root container with auto-scroll functionality
- `ChatContainerContent` - Content wrapper for messages
- `ChatContainerScrollAnchor` - Auto-scroll anchor
- `ScrollButton` - Scroll-to-bottom button
- `Message`, `MessageAvatar`, `MessageContent`, `MessageActions`, `MessageAction` - Message display
- `Markdown` - Markdown rendering with syntax highlighting
- `Loader` - Loading state indicators

**Install Commands:**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/chat-container.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/message.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/markdown.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/scroll-button.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/loader.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Create session page at /sessions/$sessionId
2. Install and configure PromptKit ChatContainer, Message, and Markdown components
3. Build message list using PromptKit Message components:
   - User messages with MessageAvatar and MessageContent (right-aligned styling)
   - Assistant messages with MessageAvatar and MessageContent (left-aligned styling)
   - System messages (centered, muted styling)
4. Create useSession hook to fetch session data
5. Implement auto-scroll using ChatContainer and ScrollButton components
6. Handle message streaming via SignalR:
   - Subscribe to ReceiveMessage event
   - Update message content incrementally
7. Use PromptKit Markdown component for assistant message rendering
8. Add MessageActions with copy, regenerate actions
9. Show timestamps on hover via tooltips
10. Add Loader component for historical message loading state
11. Handle session not found state

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Messages display correctly using PromptKit Message components
- Auto-scroll follows new messages via ChatContainer
- ScrollButton appears when scrolled up
- Streaming messages update in real-time
- Historical sessions load properly with Loader indicator
- Markdown renders correctly with syntax highlighting

title: Chat Input Component
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Create the chat input component using PromptKit PromptInput with permission and model selection.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `PromptInput` - Auto-resizing textarea with action buttons
- `PromptInputTextarea` - The text input area
- `PromptInputActions` - Container for action buttons
- `PromptInputAction` - Individual action button with tooltip
- `FileUpload` - Optional file attachment support

**Install Commands:**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/prompt-input.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/file-upload.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Install and configure PromptKit PromptInput component
2. Build chat input using PromptInput with:
   - PromptInputTextarea for multi-line text entry with auto-resize
   - PromptInputActions for send and action buttons
3. Add send button as PromptInputAction with loading state via `isLoading` prop
4. Create permission mode selector (dropdown in actions area):
   - Default
   - Bypass permissions
   - Accept edits
   - Plan mode
5. Create model selector dropdown in actions area:
   - Opus (default)
   - Sonnet
   - Haiku
6. Keyboard shortcuts are built-in to PromptInput:
   - Enter: Send message (via onSubmit prop)
   - Shift+Enter: New line
7. Use `disabled` prop when agent is processing
8. Store selected model/permission in Zustand for persistence
9. Handle send via SignalR or REST API
10. Optionally add FileUpload component for attachments

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Messages send correctly via onSubmit handler
- Permission mode is passed to API
- Model selection works
- Input is disabled during agent processing (via disabled prop)
- Keyboard shortcuts work as expected (built-in to PromptInput)
- Auto-resize textarea grows with content up to maxHeight

title: Tool Result Renderers
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Create specialized components for rendering different tool outputs using PromptKit components.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `Tool` - Base component for visualizing AI tool usage with states (input-streaming, input-available, output-available, output-error)
- `CodeBlock`, `CodeBlockCode`, `CodeBlockGroup` - Code display with syntax highlighting via Shiki
- `Steps`, `StepsTrigger`, `StepsContent`, `StepsItem` - Collapsible step sequences
- `Reasoning`, `ReasoningTrigger`, `ReasoningContent` - Collapsible reasoning/thinking display
- `ThinkingBar` - AI thinking state indicator with optional stop action
- `TextShimmer` - Animated text loading effect

**Install Commands:**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/tool.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/code-block.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/steps.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/reasoning.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/thinking-bar.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/text-shimmer.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Install and configure PromptKit Tool, CodeBlock, Steps, Reasoning, and ThinkingBar components
2. Create base ToolResultRenderer using PromptKit Tool component:
   - Leverage built-in states: input-streaming, input-available, output-available, output-error
   - Tool component includes collapsible content, state indicators, and error display
3. Create specialized renderers extending/wrapping PromptKit Tool:
   - BashToolResult: Command display with CodeBlockCode for syntax highlighting + output
   - ReadToolResult: File path + content preview with CodeBlockCode and line numbers
   - WriteToolResult: File path + diff view using CodeBlock
   - GrepToolResult: Search results with highlighted matches using CodeBlock
   - GlobToolResult: File list as tree or flat list
   - AgentToolResult: Nested agent status using Steps component
   - WebToolResult: URL + fetched content summary
4. Create ThinkingBlock using PromptKit Reasoning component:
   - Use ReasoningTrigger for "Claude is thinking..." toggle
   - ReasoningContent for expandable internal reasoning
   - Leverage `isStreaming` prop for auto-open during streaming
5. Create ToolUseBlock using PromptKit Steps component:
   - StepsTrigger shows tool invocation
   - StepsContent shows parameters
   - Links to tool result when available
6. Use CodeBlockCode with Shiki for all syntax highlighting
7. Add ThinkingBar component for active thinking state with stop action

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Each tool type renders with appropriate formatting using PromptKit Tool
- Long outputs are collapsed by default (via Tool defaultOpen=false)
- Syntax highlighting for code blocks via Shiki
- Copy functionality works (built into Tool component)
- Thinking blocks can be expanded/collapsed using Reasoning component
- ThinkingBar displays during active AI processing

title: Project Toolbar
description:
Implement the sticky toolbar above the task graph with issue management controls.

**Component: ProjectToolbar**
A horizontally scrollable toolbar positioned below the project title, containing all issue management actions.

**Layout:**
- Sticky positioning (below project title at ~52px)
- Horizontal scroll on mobile
- Button groups separated by visual dividers
- Z-index to stay above graph during scroll

**Button Groups (left to right):**

1. **Creation buttons:**
   - Create above (Shift+O) - arrow-up-from-line icon
   - Create below (O) - arrow-down-from-line icon

2. **Hierarchy buttons:**
   - Make child of another - corner-right-up icon
   - Make parent of another - corner-left-down icon
   - Both enter 'SelectingMoveTarget' mode with crosshair cursor

3. **Undo/Redo buttons:**
   - Undo (u) - undo icon
   - Redo - redo icon
   - Show action descriptions in tooltip
   - Disabled when stack is empty

4. **Edit button:**
   - Open full edit page - pencil icon
   - Disabled when no issue selected

5. **Agent Run button:**
   - Opens AgentLauncher popover
   - Shows prompt selection dropdown
   - Keyboard: `e` to open prompt selection

6. **Depth Controls (right side):**
   - Decrease depth (`[`) - minus icon
   - Current depth display
   - Increase depth (`]`) - plus icon

7. **Search Input (right side):**
   - Slash (/) to focus
   - Shows match count badge
   - `n`/`N` for next/previous match
   - Enter to embed search

**Tasks:**
1. Create ProjectToolbar component with responsive layout
2. Implement button groups with icons (lucide-react)
3. Wire up keyboard shortcuts via KeyboardNavigationService
4. Implement undo/redo integration with IssueHistoryApi
5. Create depth control state and handlers
6. Implement search input with match count
7. Add move target selection mode state
8. Mobile-responsive horizontal scrolling

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Toolbar stays fixed below project title on scroll
- All buttons trigger correct actions
- Keyboard shortcuts work when toolbar focused
- Disabled states show correctly
- Mobile: horizontally scrollable

title: Keyboard Navigation Service
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement a Vim-like modal keyboard navigation service for the task graph.

**Service: useKeyboardNavigation hook**
Manages keyboard state, edit modes, and navigation within the task graph.

**Edit Modes (enum):**
1. `Viewing` - Normal navigation mode (default)
2. `EditingExisting` - Editing an existing issue title inline
3. `CreatingNew` - Creating a new issue inline
4. `SelectingAgentPrompt` - Navigating prompt selection dropdown
5. `SelectingMoveTarget` - Selecting target for move operation

**Navigation Controls (Viewing Mode):**
- `j` / `↓` - Move selection down one issue
- `k` / `↑` - Move selection up one issue
- `h` / `←` - Navigate to parent (move right in tree)
- `l` / `→` - Navigate to first child (move left in tree)
- `g` - Jump to first issue
- `G` - Jump to last issue

**Issue Creation:**
- `o` - Create issue below current (enter CreatingNew mode)
- `Shift+O` - Create issue above current

**Issue Editing:**
- `i` - Edit title at beginning (enter EditingExisting mode)
- `a` - Edit title at end
- `r` - Replace title (clear and edit)
- `Enter` - Open full edit page

**Issue Movement:**
- `J` (Shift+j) - Move issue down in sibling order
- `K` (Shift+k) - Move issue up in sibling order

**Agent/Prompts:**
- `e` - Enter SelectingAgentPrompt mode
- `j`/`k` in prompt mode - Navigate prompts
- `Enter` - Accept selected prompt

**Depth Controls:**
- `[` - Decrease depth (show fewer levels)
- `]` - Increase depth (show more levels)

**Search:**
- `/` - Focus search input
- `n` - Next search match
- `N` - Previous search match
- `Enter` (in search) - Embed search
- `Escape` - Clear search

**Type Cycling:**
- `Tab` (viewing mode) - Cycle type: Task → Bug → Feature → Chore
- 3-second debounce per issue

**Other:**
- `Space` - Toggle inline expansion
- `Escape` - Clear selection / collapse / cancel edit / exit mode
- `u` - Undo

**Tasks:**
1. Create useKeyboardNavigation hook with state management
2. Implement edit mode state machine
3. Create useIssueSelection hook for selected issue state
4. Handle keyboard event bubbling and capture
5. Implement scroll-into-view when navigating
6. Create focus management for graph container
7. Add accessibility attributes (aria-activedescendant)
8. Type cycling with debounce

**State Structures:**
- `selectedIssueId` - Currently selected issue
- `editMode` - Current keyboard mode
- `inlineEditState` - Current edit with issue ID, title, cursor position
- `pendingNewIssue` - New issue being created with parent/child info

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Vim-like navigation works smoothly
- Mode transitions are clear and correct
- Focus management works reliably
- Scroll follows selection
- All keyboard shortcuts documented
- No conflicts with browser shortcuts

title: Add agents for modifying issues
description:
For the Homespun.Web React client, I would like to add an agent button on the project page (next to the push button). This should run an agent that can modify the fleece issues based on instructions from the user.

It should do the following:

- Open a small popup with a prompt input and run button.
- Persist any changes that have been made in the Fleece issues to the main branch without committing them.
- Create a new clone with branch name `hsp/agent+{shortened timestamp}` from main that includes the unstaged fleece issue changes in the main repository folder.
- Start a new agent with system instructions that tell the agent not to write any files in the repository and only to make modifications using the Fleece CLI tool and the user's instruction. If this was run from the toolbar with a selected issue, that issue's ID should be included in the system prompt. This should open a session much like any other agent.
- In a session window that has been created using this method, there should be a tab that shows the issue diff (which should be available in the Fleece.Core library) and two buttons that say "Accept changes" and "Cancel".
- If "Accept changes" is clicked, the issues should be merged with the issues in the main branch and be persisted again (without commit), then the session should be stopped and the browser redirected to the projects page.
- If "Cancel" is clicked the session should be stopped and the changes discarded.

title: Update chat issues considering PromptKit
description: As stated in the CLAUDE.md, I want to use PromptKit for the chat UI components. Update the fleece issues related to implementation of the chat UI in the React Web Client after researching what is available in the PromptKit components. Include the ability to answer questions and approve plans, which is currently included in the Blazor client but not in the fleece issues.

title: Question Answering UI
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement the interactive question answering interface for agent questions (AskUserQuestion tool).

**Background:**
When the Claude agent uses the AskUserQuestion tool, the UI must display the questions and allow the user to select options or provide custom answers. This mirrors the functionality in the Blazor client.

**Data Models (from Homespun.Shared):**
```typescript
interface UserQuestion {
  question: string        // The question text
  header: string          // Max 12 chars, displays as chip/tag
  options: QuestionOption[] // 2-4 options
  multiSelect: boolean    // Allow multiple selections
}

interface QuestionOption {
  label: string       // Display text for the option
  description: string // Explanation of the option
}

interface PendingQuestion {
  id: string
  toolUseId: string
  questions: UserQuestion[]
  createdAt: Date
}
```

**SignalR Events:**
- Receive: `AGUICustomEvent` with `name="QuestionPending"` containing `PendingQuestion` payload
- Send: `AnswerQuestion(sessionId, answersJson)` to submit answers

**Tasks:**
1. Create QuestionPanel component to display pending questions:
   - Header with "Claude has a question for you" title
   - Render each UserQuestion with header chip and question text
   - For each question, display QuestionOptions as radio buttons (single-select) or checkboxes (multi-select)
   - Add "Other..." option that reveals a text input for custom answers
2. Create QuestionOption component:
   - Display option label and description
   - Highlight selected state
   - Support both radio and checkbox modes
3. Implement question state management:
   - Track selected answers for each question
   - Track custom "Other" answers separately
   - Validate all required questions have answers before enabling submit
4. Create useAnswerQuestion mutation hook:
   - Send answers via SignalR `AnswerQuestion` method
   - Handle loading and error states
5. Handle SignalR QuestionPending event:
   - Parse PendingQuestion from AGUICustomEvent
   - Update session status to WaitingForQuestionAnswer
   - Clear previous answers and initialize defaults
6. Add submit button with validation:
   - Disabled until all questions have answers
   - Show loading state during submission
7. Display question UI inline in the chat message stream (after the assistant message that triggered it)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Questions display when AGUICustomEvent with QuestionPending is received
- Single-select questions show radio buttons, multi-select show checkboxes
- "Other" option reveals text input for custom answers
- Submit button is disabled until all questions have answers
- Answers are sent via SignalR when submitted
- Session status changes appropriately (WaitingForQuestionAnswer → Running)
- UI clears after successful submission

title: Plan Approval UI
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement the plan approval interface for when the agent exits plan mode (ExitPlanMode tool).

**Background:**
When the Claude agent uses the ExitPlanMode tool after creating a plan, the UI must display the plan approval interface with three options. This mirrors the functionality in the Blazor client.

**Data Models (from Homespun.Shared):**
```typescript
interface AGUIPlanPendingData {
  planContent: string    // The plan markdown content
  planFilePath?: string  // Optional file path where plan was written
}
```

**SignalR Events:**
- Receive: `AGUICustomEvent` with `name="PlanPending"` containing `AGUIPlanPendingData` payload
- Receive: `SessionStatusChanged` with `hasPendingPlanApproval=true`
- Send: `ApprovePlan(sessionId, approved, keepContext, feedback?)` to respond

**Three Approval Options:**
1. **Clear Context & Start Implementation** - Approve the plan, clear conversation context, start fresh agent session
   - Calls `ApprovePlan(sessionId, true, false, null)`
2. **Continue with Context** - Approve the plan, keep existing conversation context
   - Calls `ApprovePlan(sessionId, true, true, null)`
3. **Reject & Modify** - Reject the plan, provide feedback for revision
   - Shows textarea for feedback
   - Calls `ApprovePlan(sessionId, false, false, feedback)`

**Tasks:**
1. Create PlanApprovalPanel component:
   - Header with "Plan Ready for Implementation" title and icon
   - Description text explaining the options
   - Three action buttons with distinct styling:
     - Primary button: "Clear Context & Start Implementation"
     - Default button: "Continue with Context"
     - Danger/destructive button: "Reject & Modify"
2. Create rejection feedback form:
   - Textarea for user feedback
   - "Send Feedback" button
   - Only visible after clicking "Reject & Modify"
3. Create useApprovePlan mutation hook:
   - Send approval via SignalR `ApprovePlan` method
   - Handle the three different approval modes
   - Handle loading and error states
4. Handle SignalR PlanPending event:
   - Parse AGUIPlanPendingData from AGUICustomEvent
   - Store plan content and file path in session state
   - Update session status to WaitingForPlanExecution
   - Set hasPendingPlanApproval flag
5. Display plan content (optional):
   - Show plan markdown using PromptKit Markdown component
   - Collapsible section to view full plan
6. Handle ContextCleared event:
   - Show notification when context is cleared
   - Reset relevant UI state
7. Display plan approval UI inline in the chat message stream

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Plan approval panel displays when AGUICustomEvent with PlanPending is received
- Three distinct action buttons are available
- "Reject & Modify" reveals feedback textarea
- Approval/rejection is sent via SignalR correctly
- Session status changes appropriately (WaitingForPlanExecution → Running)
- UI clears after successful approval/rejection
- Plan content can optionally be viewed
