description: The project links should be replicated in the side bar - one per project. This was done in the Blazor application but should be replicated in the React Homespun.Web client.

title: Chat window doesn't show messages
description: The chat window in the React Homespun.Web client doesn't show the message text in the responses - it shows up as empty little empty bubbles, with one per message and tool call. Update this such that at least the message text is being rendered to the screen.

title: Fix issue graph scrolling
description: In the Homespun.Web React client, the issue titles in the task graph only have a few characters visible on mobile screens. Instead of this, allow the task graph to scroll horizontally to be able to see the full title. This should be similar to the toolbar, which can current scroll horizontally. The tabs at the top of the project page should also scroll horizontally.

title: Remaining client fixes
description:
Review and fix the following in Homespun.Web:

- The add issue above and add issue below buttons don't work. They should open the inline issue creator above or below the selected issue
- The selector to change the number of levels displayed in the graph was not ported from the Blazor app
- Add an option to the agent run prompts for "No prompt" - this should open a new session in plan mode without inject a prompt and navigate to the session page immediately.
- Add a dark mode switch to the global settings and default to the system mode.
- Change default selected model to Opus.

title: Fix agent run in client
description:
When an agent is run in the Homespun.Web client is successfully starts the new agent session but it doesn't inject the prompt into the session. I'm not sure if it sets the correct session mode either.

Please review both and fix as required.

title: Fix chat display
description:
The Homespun.Web chat display has the following problems:

- The tool result messages aren't joined to the tool call messages and end up on the user side rather than the agent side.
- There is no button to go to the bottom of the chat window. I believe this is a feature of the PromptKit UI component but if not implement it.
- The messages can exceed the screen width and have a horizontal scroll. They should be constrained to the screen width.
- Headings do not render properly in the user messages (they show up the same size) or in the agent messages (no markdown is rendered at all). All messages should be rendered assuming they are markdown and styled appropriately.

title: Add all remaining buttons to toolbar
description:
The toolbar in the Homespun.Web React frontend application is missing the following buttons that are otherwise in the Homespun.Client Blazor frontend:

- Controls for number of visible levels
- Controls for moving an issue to be parent or child of another issue

Furthermore, the task graph was not entirely updated to allow scrolling all the way horizontally to see the entire issue title. The titles are truncated after a number of characters. Fix this, and to aid visibility also remove the issue hash from the issue row information. The relevant information (type and status) should be on the far left, followed by the title.

title: Ensure issues are synchronised
description:
When issues are edited in the Homespun.Web application in the edit page, changing the status and type doesn't update the status and type of the issue. Review all items other than the description and title to ensure that they are being persisted.

Also add no priority as an option and default to it.

title: Complete feature migration
Fix graph display
description:
The graph display in the React client has the following issues when compared to the Blazor client:

- The lines from the series and parallel execution modes are swapped: series should come out of the bottom of the node but instead come out of the right side.
- Sometimes complete and closed issues are displayed when they shouldn't be. Carefully review the way that the issues are fetched from the server and how they are processed by the client in comparison between the two clients. When I look at the same issue in the Blazor client and the React client the status and type don't necessarily match. Ensure that the server is being used as the single source of truth.
- The type and status badges should open drop downs when clicked that allow these values to be changed on the fly.

title: Fix run panel
description:
When I click on the run button in the React client the application a modal panel opens but it doesn't have anything in it for me to select or a button to run. It should have the ability to select the prompt, model and base branch.

This should also be implemented in the edit issue panel, if I choose to save and run the issue - currently the save and run button doesn't do anything.

Use the same component for both for the sake of consistency.

In the edit issue panel, also add the mini prompt button for generating the branch ID, similar to the Blazor client.

title: Pull and sync button
description:
There is a split button in the Blazor application that allows the user to pull recent changes from the git repository to main and merge the fleece issues, and to optionally commit and push the new fleece issues as a "sync" operation.

This button hasn't been added to the React application, please add it in, in-line with the main project title on the projects page. This should be mostly implemented in the backend already, except for maybe the graph refreshing behaviour.

title: Create new issues in correct positions
description: When I have a parent with multiple children and I create a new issue between two of them, I expect it to inherit the parent of the issue that was selected when the 'create issue below' was clicked. This behaviour was successfully implemented in the Blazor app in PRs 293, 299 and 326.

title: Add more issue information to the sessions page
description:
The sessions page for the Homespun.Web React client should be updated to a card based interface rather than a table. This should also include more issue information like the title, agent status and the existence of a PR.

Refer to the implementation in the Blazor client.

title: Show issue and PR information on session page
description: There is a series of tabs containing issue, PR information, plans and to do's on the session page in the Homespun.Client Blazor application. Port this over to the React client in Homespun.Web.

title: Fix display of status
description: It appears that the agent status values for closed, complete, review and in progress are mixed up in the React client when compared to the enums in C#. Please review and check whether it is possible to use stronger typing in the Open API conversion of types from server to client. When I view the same issue in the React and Blazor clients they show different statuses. I am reasonably sure that the Blazor client is correct.

title: Review worker container image
description: Building a worker container image currently takes a long time compared to the other images, and there appears to be issues when pulling the image from GHCR. Could you please review the GitHub Actions for CD and the Dockerfile to try and understand the issues that are occurring with the container build?

title: Pull and sync button
description: There is a split button in the Blazor application that allows the user to pull recent changes from the git repository to main and merge the fleece issues, and to optionally commit and push the new fleece issues as a sync operation. This button hasn't been added to the React application, please add it in, in-line with the main project title on the projects page. This should be mostly implemented in the backend already, except for maybe the graph refreshing behaviour.

title: Fix agent run sequence
description: This issue is failing in CI at the linting stage. Fix these issues and add a note to CLAUDE.md to run the linting and type checking before creating a PR.

title: Tab, toolbar and notification styling
description:
I would like to update the styling of the tabs, toolbars and notifications.

Tabs:
- Remove the vertical scroll of the tabs
- Fix the tab underline, it currently always stays at "issues" even after navigating to another tab

Toolbar:
- Put a light border around the toolbar and make the toolbar background blur the contents that scroll behind it as it scrolls down
- Make the button style have an outline
- Use button groups and separators to group similar functionality (see Blazor application for guidance)
- Always show all buttons at all sizes, currently some buttons like move to child don't show up on mobile screens

Notifications:
- They currently show up as white or in light mode. Include a dark mode as well.

title: Fix worker container build
description: The worker container build failed in the recent CD workflow. It appears that the base image name is not being passed into the worker build. Update this.

title: Fix worker image build
description:
The worker image build is still failing in CI with the below problem. Please review and fix.

buildx failed with: ERROR: failed to build: failed to solve: process "/bin/sh -c useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash --uid 1000 homespun 2>/dev/null || true     && mkdir -p /home/homespun/.claude/todos /home/homespun/.claude/debug /home/homespun/.claude/projects /home/homespun/.claude/statsig /home/homespun/.claude/plans     && mkdir -p /home/homespun/.local/share /home/homespun/.config /home/homespun/.cache     && mkdir -p /workdir     && cp -r /root/.claude/plugins /home/homespun/.claude/plugins 2>/dev/null || true     && cp -r /root/.mcp.json /home/homespun/.mcp.json 2>/dev/null || true     && chown -R homespun:homespun /home/homespun /workdir /app     && chmod -R 777 /home/homespun/.local /home/homespun/.config /home/homespun/.cache /home/homespun/.claude" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1

title: Sort issues by age
description:
Update the sorting logic for the main issue graph to group the "next" issues at the root of the tree by priority, then sort by oldest at the top to newest at the bottom. Status and type can be ignored in the sorting other than filtering out complete, closed and archived issues. Whether an issue has a description can be ignored.

Let me know if this is embedded within the Fleece.Core library so I can edit that if need be.

title: Fix worker container permissions
Update status indicator to show different agent statuses
description: The status indicator currently just shows a blinking dot when an agent is running. Instead of this, show a blinking dot and a number next to it per status (waiting, working, awaiting an answer, awaiting plan execution, etc.) across all projects. When this is clicked, take the user to the sessions page.

title: Fix question answering input
description:
There are a couple of issues that need to be fixed with the question answering control (in the Homespun.Web React client):

- Multi select options don't allow selection - selection does nothing.
- Put question content under heading and make heading text and not a badge.
- Submitting answer removes question box. Currently the box stays in place.

Also, add a processing indicator to the window while the question is executing.

Refer to the Blazor client for information on this implementation.

title: Fix issue type colours in graph view
description: The issue type colours are mismatched with the meaning of the issue types (e.g. bugs are green). Fix this - I believe it was initially due to the numeric remapping of enum values.

title: Fix session chat input
description:
Sending session messages doesn't work in React client but works in Blazor client.

Number of session modes incorrect, there should be 2 (build and plan) that are converted to the Claude modes by the server.

title: Save and run agent doesn't run agent
description:
On the edit issues page, there should be prompt, model and branch selection controls above the save and run button. This should then launch the agent in the same way that the run button in the toolbar launches the agent.

Currently, clicking on the button doesn't do anything, it just seemingly saves the changes.

title: Reduce text size in session chat and fix headings
description:
The text size in the session chat in the React client is generally too large on mobile screens. Reduce the size to improve the readability and make the chat bubble width 90% of the window width.

Fix the headings in the rendered markdown so they render with heading styles - they are currently rendered with the same style as the normal text.

title: Add global prompts page
description: Implement a global prompts management page in the React frontend at /prompts. Add support for a 'None' prompt option that allows users to start agent sessions without an initial prompt. Add visual badges to distinguish between global and project-scoped prompts in the UI.

title: Add workflows
description:
Implement a workflow system for Homespun with three core concepts:

- **Session**: An individual agent session with a prompt, signaling completion state back to the workflow orchestrator via tool calls
- **Workflow**: A sequential series of steps on a single issue (like Power Automate), with configurable transitions (success/fail/retry)
- **Task Queue**: Turns an issue tree into parallel/series queues, running a configured workflow on each issue as it's dequeued

## Phases
- **Phase 1 (MVP)**: Execute a workflow on a single issue - workflow engine + UI
- **Phase 2**: Task queue + workflow integration, dispatching workflows per issue type

## Step Types
- **Agent**: Run Claude agent session (plan or build mode) with a prompt
- **ServerAction**: Run server-side logic (e.g., CI merge, deploy)
- **Gate**: Pause for human approval before continuing

## Transitions
Each step has OnSuccess/OnFailure transitions: NextStep, GoToStep, Exit, Retry

## Session Signaling
Agents call a `workflow_signal` tool to report step outcome (success/fail + data) back to the workflow orchestrator.

## Default Template
verify-implement-review-merge: Plan verification → Build implementation → Plan review → CI merge

title: Reduce chat text size and fix markdown headings on mobile
description:
The session chat in the React client has text that is too large on mobile screens, and markdown headings are not rendering with proper heading styles. This is part of fixing the chat display issues.

Problems to fix:
1. Missing Typography Plugin: The project uses prose classes (prose-sm, prose-invert) but lacks the @tailwindcss/typography plugin, causing headings to render with browser defaults
2. Fixed Chat Bubble Width: Chat bubbles use a fixed max-w-[80%] without responsive adjustments  
3. Inconsistent Prose Classes: Some components use prose prose-sm while others use only prose-sm (missing base class)
4. No Responsive Text Sizing: All screens use the same prose size regardless of viewport

Solution:
- Install @tailwindcss/typography plugin
- Implement responsive prose classes (prose-sm for mobile, prose for desktop)
- Update chat bubble widths (90% mobile, 80% desktop)
- Fix prose class consistency

title: Clean up prompt management system
description:
## Clean up prompt management system

This feature refactors the prompt management system to support global/project prompt overriding and removes the ID-based keying in favor of name+projectId composite keys.

### Completed Work
All 8 original sub-issues are complete:
- Global prompts now show correctly on the global prompts page
- isOverride flag added to prompt response DTO
- (project) suffix displayed for override prompts in UI
- Project prompt overrides created when editing global prompts on project page
- Remove override button added for project prompts
- Delete restricted to appropriate pages (project prompts on project page, global on global page)
- Project prompts page shows merged prompts (global + project overrides)
- Multiple selectable issue agent prompts supported

### Remaining Work
Remove the Id field from the prompt system entirely:
- Prompts keyed by name + projectId (null for global) as composite key
- Duplicate names prevented within same scope (global or per-project)
- Names are immutable (rename = delete + recreate)
- API routes change from {id} to by-name/{name}?projectId=xxx
- Frontend sends exact scope, no automatic backend resolution/fallback

### Verification
After all sub-issues complete, verify:
1. All test suites pass (dotnet test, npm test, npm test:e2e)
2. No references to prompt ID remain in codebase
3. API uses name-based routes exclusively
4. Duplicate prompt names are rejected within same scope

title: Update the README.md
description: The README.md is quite outdated. It refers to when we were previously using OpenCode as the agent harness. Review and update the content of the README to match the current state. The CLAUDE.md is up to date, this can be used as a reference.

title: Use cards for sessions page
description:
The sessions page for the Homespun.Web React client should be updated to a card based interface rather than a table. This should also include more issue information like the title, agent status and the existence of a PR.

Refer to the implementation in the Homespun.Client Blazor client and duplicate it in the React client.

title: Sending messages in chat doesn't update messages
description: Sending messages in the session chat will be sent to the agent to continue work, but the result isn't immediately streamed back into the chat message list. Instead, the page must be refreshed for the chat messages to be updated.

title: Fix flaky E2E tests
description: Fix all Typescript linting and formatting issues in this PR

title: Update mode, prompt and model selection in session window
description:
The controls in the session window above the input box have the following issues:

- The mode selector when clicked says: "Failed to invoke 'SetSessionMode' due to an error on the server" and fails to set the new session mode.
- The model selector doesn't show the model name in text when on mobile.
- There should be a prompt selector that shows the available global and project prompts (defaulting to "No prompt"). When one is selected, it should append the text from the prompt into the end of the chat input after filling in the placeholders.

title: Toolbar should stick to top of page
description: When scrolling down the main project page, the toolbar sticks to the current location relative to the top of the unscrolled page. This places it below significantly below the top of the page when it is scrolling. Fix this such that the toolbar scrolls up with the tabs and the heading and only sticks when it is at the top of the page (with the same gap that it has with the sides of the page.

title: Remove Homespun.Client and associated testing
description: Commit and push to update PR.

title: Remove "Agent idle" and relocate status indicator
description: When no agent is running, a green dot and "Agent idle" text is shown at the top right hand corner of the page. Remove this as there is no point displaying the idle status of the agent. Move the status indicator to the left hand side of the notification bell icon.

title: Show issue title in session card
description: The session cards on the /sessions page show the issue ID in the title rather than the issue title itself. Move the issue ID to the lower section with smaller text, and show the issue title in large text (this should be provided by the server).

title: Don't hide session mode and model selectors on mobile
description:
Currently the mode and model text selectors in the session chat don't show the selected text on mobile screens. Update this such as follows:

- They should always show the selected value text on all screens
- Change the mode select to be a toggle button as there are only two options
- Make Shift+Tab the shortcut for toggling the mode.
- Move the controls to above the chat text input.

title: Add session information to the session page
description: On the session page, there is a tab in the Homespun.Client Blazor application that is not present in the Homespun.Web React application. Review the implementation in Blazor and port it over to the React application. It should show relevant information about the issue that the session is related to, the PR (if one has been linked to the issue), the to-dos and the relevant plans.

title: Fix agent status colours
description:
The agent status colours in the agent indicator and in the sessions page should be matched:

Green: running
Yellow: waiting for input
Orange: waiting for plan execution
Purple: waiting for question answers
Red: error

title: Auto-generate branch ID when issue is created or issue title is changed
description: When a new issue is created (with a title) or the issue title is changed, the branch ID should be auto-generated asynchronously using the same logic as what happens when the button is clicked on the edit page. If the agent doesn't respond, this should fail gracefully with a notification and no change to the branch ID.

title: Add PR status to PR badge
description: Add visual indicators for PR status (merge conflicts and CI checks) to the PR badge in the main task graph view. The badge should show: Git branch icon for merge conflicts (red = conflicts, green = no conflicts), Check/X/Spinner icon for CI status (green check = passing, red X = failing, spinner = running)

title: Add stop button to session window
description:
The session window should include a stop button that stops the session in the same way that the stop button on the sessions page does. This should be located at the top right.

The title in the session window also gives the full ID of the session: this should be changed to be the name of the issue the session is related to.

title: Manage multiple sessions
description:
Multiple sessions on the same branch should be accessible. The Claude worker should be used as the single source of truth - I believe the management of multiple sessions is possible using the Typescript SDK.

I want to have the "session" page represent all the sessions that have been run on an issue. A session tab should be added to the information panel that shows all past sessions, each of which represents a context window. Clicking on these should open previous sessions as read-only.

When the agent session page is navigated to, show the latest active session only. There should also be a button to clear context and add a new session for this agent worker container.

Clarify the difference between "agent" (a worker container running on a cloned issue branch) and a session ("a single conversation with that "agent" when working.

title: Render issues as one large SVG
description: The task graph is currently rendered as row-by-row SVGs with text descriptions and controls adjacent to each row. This adds significant difficulties in rendering edges that bypass rows. Instead of this I want to use D3.js to calculate the layout of the nodesn and edges, and just rendering the entire task graph in one large SVG, including the issue descriptions and controls. The issue descriptions and controls should be added as <foreignObject> elements so that they can maintain their current complex behaviour.

title: Add logging for status changes in worker
description:
There are ongoing issues where the status of the worker container doesn't represent what it is actually doing (e.g. saying it is waiting while it is still streaming back input, saying it has a plan pending after the plan has been executed, etc.). The status should be persisted on the worker and changed when certain messages are sent back to the server or received (e.g. when any agent message or tool invocation is sent, the agent is clearly "working").

Add logging to the worker that indicates whenever a status is changed. It should say "Status changed from 'working' to 'waiting'" or something similar.

title: Fix agent status and session chats
Update React client logging to Promtail
description: The server container logs to a PLG stack, which is set up using Docker Compose. The Homespun.Web React client should also log to the PLG stack using OpenTelemetry. This should be tagged with Component="Client" such that the client logs can be separated.

title: Review and consolidate session IDs
description: Review the worker container in particular and confirm the method by which session IDs are being created and managed. I understand that the container and agent have an id `{project-id}-{issue-id}` and Claude Code generates a unique session ID that is used for identifying and resuming sessions. Are there any other IDs that are being used in the Homespun application to identify sessions, and if so do we need them or can we just use the container ID and Claude Code session ID?

title: Continue existing sessions
Fix permissions sent from client
description: The Homespun.Web client seems to consistently send messages with plan mode regardless of the selected option. Ensure the selected option is passed all the way through to the worker. The mode is set per session and not per message, so the server should be the main place that this state is tracked.

title: Fix loading of past pull requests
description: The past pull requests are not being loaded in when the PRs are being refreshed. There should have been some tracking implemented where merged and closed PRs are tracked when the PRs are loaded into the cache - the PRs that were registered as "open" but are no longer in the "open" list that is pulled from GitHub should be checked to determine whether they are "closed" or "merged" and the cache updated as such. However, it seems that this was being done before but has stopped working, as the PRs that are in the cache show the merged PRs only up to a certain date and newer merged PRs are not being shown.

title: Show agent status rings
description: A previous PR #469 was merged to show the agent status on running agents in the graph view. However, it doesn't appear to have worked, as the ring displayed in the graph view remains just around the "next" issues, not the issues with running agents. Please review and fix this - there should only be one "mode" for what is used to display the rings.

title: Ensure Playwright is installed for testing
Move floating toolbar position
description: The floating toolbar on the project issues page currently has a position set to 68px from the top. I have tested this in the browser and have found that this should be set to 4px. Update this.

title: Add rules for e2e test creation to CLAUDE.md
Create temporary file system and use real files as test fixture
description: Replace in-memory mock services with real file-based services that operate on a temporary folder structure. This will allow E2E tests to exercise the full stack while maintaining test isolation.

title: Test run E2E tests
description:
I have recently updated the version of Chrome that is installed in this browser for the purpose of being able to run E2E tests. Run the E2E tests, then run the development version of the app with `npm run dev` and inspect it with the Playwright MCP tools to confirm that the correct browser versions are installed.

Let me know whether this was successful or if further changes are required to the installed browsers.

title: Remove linting from CI
description: Linting failures are blocking CI constantly. Remove this from the CI pipeline, and add a new workflow for checking if there were any changes in the last day, then running the automatic lint fixes every day. This should be followed by running the Typescript unit tests and pushing to main if successful.

title: Complete implementation of issue agent
description: There is a fleece issue related to creating agents that operate on a clone of the main branch, change the fleece issues and merge in the issues without using Git. The backend has been implemented for this, please put together a plan for completing the remaining tasks required for this feature.

title: Add comprehensive logging to worker status transitions
description: Add logging to track all worker status changes, stream events, and pending states to help diagnose issues where displayed status doesn't match actual worker activity. This includes logging for idle/streaming/closed transitions, tool executions, A2A protocol state changes, and pending question/plan states.

title: Add logging to diagnose Claude failures
Install Typescript LSP and Claude Code plugin
description: In the worker container Dockerfile, install the Typescript LSP and Claude Code Typescript plugin. The plugin should be installed with `claude plugin install typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official` but the Typescript LSP server may also need to be installed. Review this by installing it locally first and verifying that it works, then updating the Dockerfile.

title: Fix model types
Session doesn't load when card is clicked
description: When the session card is clicked on the /sessions page, the page redirects to the correct session page route but the chat messages and issue title doesn't load. They do load when the page is refreshed, however. Ensure that this information is pulled correctly when the page is routed to.

title: Check changes to issue details before showing "discard changes" dialog
description: When exiting the issue edit page the a modal dialog pops up asking me whether I want to discard changes. This is regardless of whether there actually are any changes - check whether any differences exist before asking this question.

title: Add logging of message content to worker and improve message logging
description:
We have recently status change logging to the workers to determine when the statuses are changing in the workers.

However, it is difficult to do this without the message content as context. Add "debug" logging that includes tool uses (just the tool information) and the message content so that we can understand what is occurring. This should be configurable when the agent containers are started - update the server code to always set this "debug" flag to true (for now) when the containers are started in the DockerAgentExecutionService.

Also, review the Grafana dashboard in config/grafana to improve the logging outputs - currently it shows the timestamp and level, followed by the "Parsed fields" in badges and then the "Log line" as text. The log line is just `[IssueID] LogLevel [logger.js]`. I would like this to instead be `Timestamp LogLevel [SourceContext] :  Message` for the worker logs, and for the "Parsed fields" to be removed from the log output on the dashboard.

title: When a plan approval is pending, sending a message should cancel the plan
description: When a plan approval is pending and the user instead sends a prompt using the normal chat box, this should be treated the same as the user using the reject button with the text in the chat box used as the description of the changes requested by the user.

title: Branch icon doesn't show merge status in PR badge
description: The branch icon next to the PR link in the issue graph is always green regardless of the merge conflicts status. Review this and fix it such that it is red if there are merge conflicts for that PR, information that should be fetched from GitHub rather than determined locally.

title: Fix: Session doesn't load when card is clicked
description: When clicking a session card on /sessions, the page redirects to /sessions/$sessionId but chat messages and issue title don't load. They only load on page refresh.

title: Change issue rendering to differentiate between series and parallel issues
description:
The graph rendering currently shows the edges coming out of the right side of each node and then turning down to reach the parent node below. This is representative of the "parallel" execution mode (i.e. the parent is rendering in parallel) but shows up regardless of whether the parallel or series execution mode is selected.

Update this such that it only renders this way for parallel execution, and for series execution the edges come out of the bottom of the node. They should go straight down into their sibling or if the next node down is their parent they should go down first then right into the side of their parent.

Note that the execution mode drawing I am talking about is governed by the parent, not the execution mode of the node in question: execution mode signifies whether a node's children should be executed in parallel or series.

title: Show session information tab
description: A session information panel was created but is not visible in the application. Please find this tab and enable it - it should show up on the right hand side for desktop screens and as a bottom drawer for mobile screens.

title: Allow local only repositories
description: I would like to be able to run Homespun without the GitHub integrations as well - mostly for testing. Please review the architecture and plan to be able to detach the GitHub service for certain local-only projects. In this instance, creating a new project should just create the source folder adjacent to the other GitHub related projects. There will need to be a flag added to the project registration in homespun-data.json to note that it is not a GitHub backed project.

title: Allow multi-user usage
description:
# Multi-User Usage Support

This parent issue tracks all work required to enable Homespun to support multiple concurrent users working on the same project.

## Overview

Currently Homespun operates in single-user mode. This initiative adds proper multi-user support including:
- User assignment to issues
- User-based filtering
- User identity display
- Documentation for multi-user setup
- Infrastructure automation

## Child Issues

All child issues must be completed before this can be marked complete. Verification steps:

1. Verify documentation is complete and accurate for new user onboarding
2. Confirm default prompts are updated and working with user context
3. Test that the "assigned:me" filter works correctly across users
4. Validate Azure VM setup script creates proper isolated environments
5. Ensure issues created by agents are assigned to the current user

## Acceptance Criteria

- Multiple users can work simultaneously on different issues
- Each user sees their own assigned issues by default
- New users can follow documentation to get started
- Infrastructure can be provisioned via automated scripts

title: Add user assignment to issues
description:
There is an assigned user field in Fleece issues. I would like to be able to add a user email address to the application settings that is required (i.e. all routes redirect to the settings page if there is not a user set up). This setting should be persisted in the homespun-data.json file.

When it is set, all new issues that are created in the homespun application should have the assigned user set to the user's email. Changes should have the assigned user also set using that email address.

title: Move all Homespun related items to tags in the Fleece issuesa
Switch rendering state for series and parallel issues
description: The rendering of series and parallel types in the issue graph is reversed - series issues look like parallel and parallel issues look like series. Swap this.

title: Fix session mode button and model selector
description:
Clicking on the session mode button in the session chat window or using the SHIFT+TAB shortcut doesn't show any change in the session mode in the UI, and doesn't appear to pass the message on to the server or worker.

When changing the model selector it changes just below the slab title in the UI, but the server says "Failed to set model on worker for session 25e2c246-b0e9-47bf-b8a5-a5a1f3290a34".

This relates to the UI changes that were merged in PR #508 and #503.

title: Fix Grafana dashboards to include messages in simplified log lines
Show user badge
description: When there is a user assigned to an issue, show a badge with the user's name next to the issue in the graph view. Only show the section that is before the first @ if there is one in the value (i.e. show "user" for "user@email.com").

title: Add default filters
description: The filters shown for the issue graph should default to showing "next" issues and issues that are assigned to the current user. When the 'f' key is pressed, the caret in the filter box should be placed at the end of the text box for adding more filters.

title: Add filter panel
description:
Next to the search box in the toolbar, add a filter button that allows the issue graph to be filtered. 'f' should be the keyboard shortcut for this button (similar to how '/' is the shortcut for searching).

This should expand the toolbar to be two rows deep, with the second row showing the filter query (similar to how GitHub filtering works). The queries should be in the same format as introduced in these Fleece PRs:

- https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/pull/72
- https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/pull/73

Filtering should happen on "enter" being pressed. Add a '?' button next to the text box that shows a popup with some instructions on how to use the query language.

title: Add full refresh button
description: In the settings tab on the projects page, add an additional button that triggers a full update of the GitHub PRs (i.e. downloads all merged, closed and open PRs and updates the entire cache for the project).

title: Switch to running status after question answer or plan execution starts
description: When the worker receives a notification that a plan is to be executed or that a question has been answered, it should switch the status to "working". The status should only change to "waiting" when the query has returned all messages and is not awaiting plan input or question responses.

title: Serialize all enums as strings
description: There are a number of enums that are used in the DTOs that are passed between the C# server and the Typescript client. These are all currently being handled as their integer values, which has led to significant problems with mismatched values and errors. I would like to instead pass these values as strings: the C# side should serialize and deserialize all values assuming the incoming and outgoing values are strings, and the Typescript side should always just operate as strings. These are used all throughout the Typescript application, so be thorough on identifying any numeric values that need to be converted to their string values. I expect that many tests will need to be updated to suit.

title: Add button for issues agent rather than prompt
Remove ClaudeAgentSDK
description:
There is currently a project called ClaudeAgentSdk that implements a local version of the agent SDK used to send prompts to Claude Code.

This should be removed as we are now relying on the Worker containers and executing all prompts in separate workers containers.

Please remove this and the local agent execution mode entirely.

title: Assign current user when issue that is edited has no user assigned
description: When an issue is edited and there is no user assigned, assign the user that is editing the issue to the issue that is being edited.

title: Update the Fleece library version to v1.6.0
description: Update Fleece.Core from v1.4.0 to v1.6.0 and adapt Homespun to use the new features: key-value tag support, Verify issue type, and LinkedPR migration to keyed tags.

title: Fix Grafana dashboard
description: Recent changes to the Grafana dashboard were made in the browser and then copied to @config/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/json/homespun-logs.json. There now aren't any dashboards showing up in Grafana at all, where it previously had the homespun dashboard it is just empty. Review the implementation and try to understand the error.

title: Include draft status for issues
description:
Fleece.Core version 1.5.0 also included a draft status for issues. See release notes here: https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/releases/tag/v1.5.0

Update the types and UI to suit. Note that the changes for 1.6.0 have already been included.

title: Expose logs to workers
description:
The application currently logs using the PLG stack in containers that ride side by side to the worker containers. I would like to be able to have the worker containers access these logs without having to connect to tailscale. Review the connections that are available and put together a plan to have the workers read these logs so they can analyse them as they build the Homespun application.

This should take the form of instructions in the CLAUDE.md on how the worker can use curl to access logs from Loki.

title: Create live worker container tests
description:
I want to add a series of tests for the worker containers that sends them queries and expects certain response types. Because this will use live inference, I don't intend on these tests to be run automatically in CI, but instead to be just run manually while developing the worker containers.

The tests should be as follows, with each test having a new container started up in a new temporary test folder:

Test 1 - Prompts:
- With no initial prompt and on plan mode, send the query "Say 'Hello, World'".
- Expect the response "Hello, World" (not a strict assertion, just assert that there is a response and print it).

Test 2 - Reading and writing:
- In build mode with no initial prompt, send the query "Write the text 'test' to the file 'test.txt'".
- Expect that the test.txt file is created and it contains the words "test.txt".
- Edit the text in the file to "edited", then send the query "Read the file 'test.txt' and print out the answer".
- Expect that the result is "edited"

Test 3 - Asking questions, planning and clearing context:
- In plan mode with no initial prompt, send the query "I am testing the question and planning tools of this agent, as well as the ability to clear context. Remember the number 15, but don't put this into any plans that are created. Don't read any files, just use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask 'What would you like to order?', with a multiple choice question saying 'Mains' with 'steak' or 'burger' as the options, and a multiple choice question saying 'Sides', with 'chips' and 'salad' as the options with multiple answers accepted. Following this, create a plan that just says 'This is a plan for testing the UI, just repeat what the user has ordered, which is 'Steak with chips and salad.'"
- Expect that a question is returned, then answer "steak" to the first questions and "fries" and "salad" to the second question.
- Once the plan is returned, proceed with the plan and clear the context.
- When the plan has been executed, ask the question "What number was in the original prompt" and expect that 15 will not be returned as the context was cleared.

title: Add buttons for series and parallel
description: In the issues tab, there is currently a downwards arrow show if the issue is in series execution mode and nothing shown if the issue is in parallel execution mode. Instead of this, add a toggle button between the status and the title that allows the user to switch between execution modes for an issue. It should use the git-commit-vertical Lucide icon for the series mode and the git-fork icon for the parallel mode.

title: Clicking "save and run" should take the user back to the projects page without prompting for changes
description:
Currently, on the issue edit page when clicking "save and run agent" the user is left on the edit page. When exiting the edit page to go back to the issues page, a confirmation modal for abandoning edits is shown, even though the issue details haven't changed (the issue edits were presumably just saved, although this may be due to the status being changed by the run command).

Update this such that when the button is clicked the user is just taken straight back to the project page. Review the issue with the confirmation modal, if it is due to the status being changed by the run command perhaps clicking the save and run agent button should just change the value in the combo box to "In progress" pre-emptively.

title: Fix auto pulling
description:
When a new agent is run, prior to creating the clone of the branch the most recent changes to main should be automatically pulled and the fleece issues merged (the functionality that the 'Pull' button on the project page performs).

This used to work, but apparently doesn't anymore, review the actions of creating a new agent and their ordering carefully.

title: Fix auto branch ID issue creation
description: When issues are created or the title is changed, the working branch ID should be automatically generated from the title using the mini prompt. This used to work, but has since stopped - I must go into the issue edit panel and click the generate button manually. Review the issue and fix it.

title: Update the sessions page using SignalR
description:
The sessions page shows the active agent sessions, but the status is often out of sync with the agent status display at the top right hand corner of the screen.

I believe this is due to a SignalR wiring issue, where the live results are not being pulled in. Review this and update it such that they are kept up to date.

title: Fix status issues for plan execution and waiting
description:
Review the worker logs for issues n8kTk6 and YfCSMn.

For issue n8kTk6, the status was left at "awaiting plan execution" while the agent was working, and for issue YfCSMn, the status was set to "waiting" but the agent kept emitting prompts.

I believe the worker status updates were improved in a recent PR, but this was evidently insufficient. Identify the issue from the logs and fix it.

title: Create live worker container tests
description: Add a series of tests for the worker containers that sends them queries and expects certain response types. Because this will use live inference, these tests are not run automatically in CI, but instead run manually while developing the worker containers.

title: Fix working status rings and prevent multiple concurrent agents
description: The issues graph used to contain rings around each of the nodes that had an agent working on them, with the colour of the ring representing the status. This is no longer shown, likely due to the changes in PR #526. Please review and fix the issue.

title: Prevent multiple concurrent agents
description: It's currently possible to run multiple concurrent agents on the same issue. This should be prevented by having the agent execution service check whether there are any agents running on an issue. If so the run button should change to an open button to open the relevant session page.

title: Expand MockAgentExecutionService with realistic mock responses
description:
The MockAgentExecutionService created in the remove-claude-agent-sdk PR is minimal. Expand it to provide realistic mock responses similar to MockClaudeSessionService, including simulated tool use, thinking blocks, and configurable response content.

It should use simple keyword matching to respond in particular ways to a prompt:
- "think": Send back a thinking block, then respond with a message.
- "tool": Send back a read file tool usage and a write file tool usage, then respond with a message.
- "question": Write a message, then ask one question with single select options and one question with a multi-select option. After finishing, create a plan.
- "plan": Enter plan mode and send a plan for execution.

These should be implemented with the correct statuses returned from the agent at the correct times. Add appropriate wait times for sending the messages back asynchronously.

This keyword matching should be used to assemble the overall query - if more than one keyword is matched, the assembled query should still run in order and shouldn't duplicate the plan creation.

For example, "Use a tool and then think about the response. Ask a question and create a plan" should result in the thought block ("I'm thinking about the prompt"), a returned message ("I have thought about the message"), a tool block ("Read a file 'test.txt'", "Wrote a file 'test.txt'), a returned message ("I used some tools"), a question ("Here are two questions, one with multiple choice answers and one with single choice answers"), a returned message after the question is answered ("You responded to the first question with yellow and the second question with purple, blue and pink."), a returned message ("I am creating a plan"), a plan ("Here is my plan") and finally a message ("I am now working on the plan with cleared context").

In this example, the plan is not being sent twice.

title: Move issue modification prompt to special session type
description:
A new prompt type and session features was added for running an agent whose purpose is to modify the issues directly based on a prompt from the user (e.g. "I want to implement feature X, review the codebase and create Fleece issues for the steps that are required to implement this feature").

This is now showing up in the global prompts and in the "run agent" prompt list. Instead of this, I want to consider these as a separate system from the UI point of view but runs using the same backend as the normal sessions.

Add a button to the toolbar that will run a new "Issues agent". When this button is clicked, a new session with the special "IssueModificationAgent" type should start with an empty chat box. This session should be created in a new branch without a new issue (creating a session on a branch was added in a recent PR #540. A system prompt should provide the instructions that the agent is only to read the codebase and make edits to the Fleece issues using the Fleece CLI. This should still show up in the "Sessions" pages, but have some visual distinction to show that it is a different type of agent.

There should be a link on the session page to a separate page that shows two side-by-side issues graphs representing a diff between the "main" issue graph (the same one that is shown in the projects page) and the issue graph from the branch that was created for this session. On this page there should be an "accept" and "cancel" button. If "accept" is clicked, the issues should be merged into the main branch (without committing) and the session stopped.

title: Fix issues agent button
description: When the issues agent button is clicked, a new branch is cloned and a new issues agent session is started and opened. When a prompt is typed in, however, the agent immediately goes into an error state. Review the logs using your logs skill. The worker container appears to have not been started when I use `docker ps` on the host machine.

title: Create sessions on branches
description:
I want to be able to create new sessions on branches rather than on issues. Add a new button to the branches tab that opens a modal. The modal should prompt the user for a new branch name, with OK and Cancel. When a valid name is entered and OK is clicked, the latest changes to main should be pulled, then a clone with that branch name should be created. This should also be pulled in case there is a branch already with that name on the remote.

Following this, a new agent session should be started on that branch.

title: Fix status issues
description: Use the logs skill to review the recent worker container logs from Loki. I am still having some difficulty with the agent status not being updated as expected (Awaiting plan execution status after the plan has been executed, awaiting input status while the agent is still responding, etc.). Please try to identify these issue and work out how to fix them.

title: Get plan files straight from the .claude/plans folder
description: The plans tab in the session window is intended to show all of the plans that exist for the current Claude Code agent. It currently doesn't work, often showing errors or old plans from unrelated agents. The plans shown should be read directly from the projects/{project}/.clones/{branch-id}/.claude/plans folder, update this so it just looks in that folder directly for plan files.

title: Remove background of top section of side bar
description: The side bar of the main page has a lighter colour than the main page background. Remove this background colour from the section that has the Homespun title - just show it for the section that contains "Projects" and "Global"

title: Fix card layout for session cards
description:
The layout for session cards (as shown on the global "Sessions" page has an issue where the "Chat" and "Stop" buttons often float off the side of the card and underneath the adjacent card. Fix this by removing the "Chat" button and making the entire card clickable to take the user to the relevant session page.

The stop button should be a smaller red icon button so it fits in the card.

The issue title also doesn't go all the way across the card but is instead stuck at a smaller width. Instead of having the cards be in multiple columns, always just have one column of cards that are full width and wrap the issue title text so it always displays the full thing.

title: Make filters bar part of toolbar and restore float
description:
When there is a filter applied to the issues, it shows up as a separate toolbar below the main project toolbar. This was introduced in PR #535 It should instead sit within the same toolbar, just as a separate row that expands and collapses when the filter button is clicked.

The toolbar used to float above the issues as well but no longer does. Fix this. Remove the default filter that is applied when the page is opened - it should be unfiltered. Add a button next to the ? button that applies these default filters with the square-user Lucide icon.

title: Check log access works
Copy unstaged issues when cloning
description:
When issues are added using the Homespun UI, the modifications to the Fleece issues file in the repository is left unstaged and not committed until manually committed with the 'sync' action.

When a new agent is started on an issue, the cloning of the repository to create the branch doesn't copy these issues over as the cloning only includes the issues at the last commit. Update this such that after the cloning occurs the entire contents of the .fleece folder is copied over to replace the newly cloned .fleece folder.

title: Add Clones tab to project page with cleanup automation
description:
## Overview

Add a new "Clones" tab to the project page that displays all clones found in the `.clones` folder. The tab will show clone metadata, link to related PRs and issues, and provide deletion capabilities for stale clones.

## Key Features

### 1. Clones Tab Display
- Add "Clones" tab to project page navigation (between Branches and Prompts tabs)
- Show two separate lists:
  - **Feature Clones**: Clones linked to issues/PRs (branch name contains issue ID via `+{issueId}`)
  - **Issues Agent Clones**: Clones with branch names starting with `issues-agent-`

### 2. Clone Information Display
Each clone card should display:
- Clone folder name and branch name
- Current commit SHA with relative time
- Linked Issue (if extractable from branch name via `BranchNameParser.ExtractIssueId()`)
  - Issue title and status from Fleece
- Linked PR (if branch matches a cached PR via `IGraphCacheService`)
  - PR number, title, status (Open/Merged/Closed)
- Clone status indicators:
  - On correct branch vs detached/wrong branch
  - Has active session indicator

### 3. Delete Button Logic
Show a delete button on clones that meet ANY of these criteria:

**PR-based deletion criteria:**
- Linked PR has status `Merged`
- Linked PR has status `Closed`

**Issue-based deletion criteria:**
- Linked issue has status `complete`
- Linked issue has status `archived`
- Linked issue has status `closed`
- Linked issue cannot be found (deleted)

### 4. Delete All Button
- Button at top of the page to "Delete All Stale Clones"
- Only visible when there are deletable clones
- Shows count of clones that will be deleted
- Requires confirmation dialog before proceeding
- Deletes all clones matching the deletion criteria

## Technical Implementation

### Backend Changes

1. **New API Endpoint: `GET /api/clones/enriched`**
   Create a new endpoint that returns enriched clone data:
   ```csharp
   public class EnrichedCloneInfo
   {
       public CloneInfo Clone { get; set; }
       public string? LinkedIssueId { get; set; }
       public FleeceIssue? LinkedIssue { get; set; }  // Include issue details
       public PullRequestInfo? LinkedPr { get; set; }  // Include PR details
       public bool IsDeletable { get; set; }  // Pre-calculated deletion eligibility
       public string? DeletionReason { get; set; }  // Why it is deletable
       public bool IsIssuesAgentClone { get; set; }  // Branch starts with "issues-agent-"
   }
   ```

2. **New API Endpoint: `DELETE /api/clones/bulk`**
   Bulk delete endpoint for "Delete All" functionality:
   - Accept list of clone paths
   - Return success/failure for each
   - Invalidate relevant caches

3. **Service: `CloneEnrichmentService`**
   New service to enrich clone data:
   - Extract issue ID from branch name using `BranchNameParser.ExtractIssueId()`
   - Look up issue details via `IFleeceService`
   - Look up PR via `IGraphCacheService.GetCachedPRData()` matching branch name
   - Calculate `IsDeletable` based on PR/issue status

### Frontend Changes

1. **Route: `/projects/:projectId/clones`**
   - Create new route file: `projects.$projectId.clones.tsx`
   - Add tab to project page navigation

2. **Component: `ClonesTab`**
   - Main container component
   - Two sections: "Feature Clones" and "Issues Agent Clones"
   - Header with "Delete All Stale" button

3. **Component: `EnrichedCloneCard`**
   - Display enriched clone information
   - Show linked issue with status badge (links to issue detail)
   - Show linked PR with status badge (links to PR URL)
   - Conditional delete button based on `isDeletable`
   - Loading state while checking PR/issue status

4. **Hooks:**
   - `useEnrichedClones(projectId)` - Fetch enriched clone data
   - `useBulkDeleteClones()` - Mutation for bulk deletion

5. **UI Patterns:**
   - Follow existing BranchCard styling patterns
   - Use existing badge components for status
   - Use existing confirmation dialog for delete actions
   - Empty state when no clones exist

## Test Plan

### Backend Tests
- Test `CloneEnrichmentService` issue ID extraction
- Test PR matching via branch name
- Test deletion eligibility calculation for each status type
- Test bulk delete endpoint

### Frontend Tests
- Test `EnrichedCloneCard` renders correctly with all data states
- Test delete button visibility based on `isDeletable`
- Test "Delete All" button shows correct count
- Test confirmation dialog flow

### E2E Tests
- Navigate to Clones tab
- Verify clones display with correct linked data
- Verify Issues Agent clones are separated
- Test single clone deletion
- Test bulk deletion

## Files to Create/Modify

### Backend
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Git/Controllers/ClonesController.cs` - Add enriched and bulk delete endpoints
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Git/CloneEnrichmentService.cs` - New service
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Git/EnrichedCloneInfo.cs` - New model
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Requests/CloneRequests.cs` - Add bulk delete request

### Frontend
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.clones.tsx` - New route
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.tsx` - Add tab navigation
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/` - New feature folder
  - `components/clones-tab.tsx`
  - `components/enriched-clone-card.tsx`
  - `hooks/use-enriched-clones.ts`
  - `hooks/use-bulk-delete-clones.ts`
  - `index.ts`

### Tests
- `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/Git/CloneEnrichmentServiceTests.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/components/clones-tab.test.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/components/enriched-clone-card.test.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/e2e/clones-tab.spec.ts`

title: Fix issue diff view in issue agents
description: In the sessions page for issue agents there is a button that says "view changes". When clicked, this adds /issue-diff to the route and should take the user to another page that shows the changes to the issues between the graph shown on the main project page and the issues that are in the issue agent cloned branch. However, when clicked there is no redirection and the diff is not shown. Please review and fix this to show the issue page.

title: Add simple issue tree view
description:
I want to try using traditional hierarchical tree view of the issues to see if this is more appropriate. Add a button that switches the view mode to the toolbar. Instead of the current task graph view that shows the next issues at the root and their parents as leaves, flip this to show the parent issues on the left side and their children progressing down and to the right. Still show the graph nodes and edges but matching the new tree structure.

If a node has multiple parents, just show it once when it is first traversed in the tree.

Remove the merged and closed pull requests from the top of the graph in this view mode.

title: Fix issues agent issue merging
description:
I just used the issues agent for the first time to add a description to an issue. It successfully modified the issue details in the cloned branch that was created, however the diff view did not seem to pick up that there were changes in the issues - I assume this is because the diff is only loaded once. The issues from the clone and main branch should be re-read whenever the diff view is opened, and when the issues from the cloned branch are to be merged back in.

In addition to this, in the graph view only show the issues that have changed.

title: Fix session streaming
description: The session window doesn't stream incoming messages from the server - instead, I need to continually refresh the window to show new messages. Fix this such that messages are always streamed to the window when open.

title: Set type and status button width to 56px
description:
The type and status button widths in the issues graph are currently variable in width. Set them to exactly 56px in width such that they always fit the text within them and always remain the same width for alignment purposes.

Also add an outline for the execution mode button and set the height to match the type and status buttons.

title: Fix issue agent merging
description: The FleeceChangeDetectionService and merging of issues into main appears to adopt the changes from the issue agent branch entirely rather than merge the issues from the main branch and the issues from the issue agent branch using the timestamp sensitive merging algorithm provided by Fleece.Core. Review this and update it to use the merging algorithm for both the diff calculation and the merging of accepted.

title: Ensure changes are being read from the correct issues agent branch
description: When the issue agent is run it successfully edits the issues in the correct branch. The issue diff component was updated such that it only shows changes in the issues rather than the full issues tree in PR #558, but the actual issues are seemingly not being read from the correct location for the changes instigated by the agent, as even after the changes are made the issue diff window doesn't show any changes. Add logging that shows when the issues are being read and from which folder they are being read from, and add a refresh button to the issue diff screen to allow manual refreshing of both the main and modified issues. When the "accept" button is clicked, the issues in the main branch are not updated and a notification pops up saying "modified 0 issues", so it would appear that the issue is with where the server is drawing the issues from rather than with the front end.

title: Add scroll bar to issue information tab
description: The issue information panel in the session window has a series of tabs that show different information in them. It doesn't all fit in the width of the panel, however. Make these tabs horizontally scrollable so I can see all of them.

title: Change status text from "In Progress" to "Progress"
description: THe status buttons on the issues page currently contain the text "In Progress" when the issue status is "in progress". Instead of this, change the text in the button to just "Progress" for this status to make the button more compact - this text currently doesn't fit in the button at it's current fixed width. Leave the status as "In progress" on the edit page, as there is more room there.

title: Fix dashboards
Review parsing of status when loading diff
Fix issue copying during clone creation
description:
The unstaged issues should be copied to the cloned folder when a new agent is run. This is failing with the following logs:

2026-03-21 13:07:50.414 [Homespun.Features.Git.GitCloneService] : Failed to copy .fleece changes to clone: An error occurred trying to start process 'rsync' with working directory '/data/projects/Northrop/main'. No such file or directory

2026-03-21 13:07:50.414 [Homespun.Features.Commands.CommandRunner] : Command exception: rsync -av --exclude=.git "/data/projects/Northrop/main/.fleece/" "/data/projects/Northrop/.clones/issues-agent-20260321-020836/workdir/.fleece/" in /data/projects/Northrop/main | Duration=0ms | User=ubuntu | PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/root/.dotnet/tools

2026-03-21 13:07:50.414 [Homespun.Features.Git.GitCloneService] : Copying unstaged .fleece changes from /data/projects/Northrop/main to clone at /data/projects/Northrop/.clones/issues-agent-20260321-020836/workdir

2026-03-21 13:07:50.414 [Homespun.Features.Fleece.Controllers.IssuesAgentController] : Creating Issues Agent clone for branch issues-agent-20260321-020836 from base main

title: Review fleece prime hook
description: There should be a hook that runs `fleece prime` and injects it into the system prompt of the agent whenever a new agent is started. This provides the agent with basic instructions on how to use the fleece CLI tool. Review whether this is still implemented by checking that it is being injected into your prompt, then look at the codebase to ensure that this happens for both the normal agent invocation and the issues agent invocation.

title: Add prompt "code" mode
description:
In the prompts pages (both the local and global pages), add a tab that switches between the normal prompts view (with the prompts listed in cards) and a "code" mode that shows a text box with the prompts serialized as raw JSON. I want to be able to use this to be able to "import" and "export" all prompts quickly.

There should be an apply button at the bottom that checks that the JSON is valid and then updates the prompts, and a revert button that deserializes the prompts again and undoes and changes in the text box.

title: Add issue specific issues agent button and prompt
description:
There is currently a prompt named "IssueModify" in the prompt list, and an "issues agent" button in the toolbar.

Do the following:
- The "IssueModify" prompt should be moved to a separate category with the heading "Issue agent prompts", where there is a fixed single prompt used when running the issue modification agent on an issue, and a fixed system prompt that is used for all issue agents. These can be modified - they are fixed in the sense that there cannot be more than one prompt for each.
- Change the behaviour of the issues agent button in the toolbar to be specific for the selected issue. The modal that pops up should have a text box that allows for instructions to be added in before running the agent (e.g. rename this issue), and should show which issue is selected below this. When the Start Agent button is clicked, this should then start an issue agent, use the IssueModify prompt and replace the placeholders with details of the selected issue and the instructions, and send the prompt to the agent similar to running other agents. If no instructions are provided, just open the session page with no prompt, but include the system prompt.

title: Match the status in the session window with the global indicator
description:
The agent status badge that shows up at the top of the session page (just below the title) doesn't always match the status that is shown on the session list page, and the status in the agent indicator at the top of the page.

The badge in the session page is usually correct (i.e. shows working while the agent is working, etc.), while the global indicator is often incorrect. Review the difference between these do and rectify this issue.

title: Issues agent run doesn't inject prompt
description:
When I run an issues agent with a selected issue and fill in the prompt box on the modal (as introduced in PR 571) the agent is started and the clone is created, but the prompt is not generated or injected into the agent session. Additionally, on the prompts page, there is only one issue agent prompt "IssueAgentSystem" and no "IssueAgentModification" prompt that should be used to create the first "user" message.

Do the following:
- Add the additional fixed "modification" prompt to the prompts list. This should be as below
- Ensure that this prompt is passed in to the agent session when the issue agent session is started.

## Issue Modification Request

You are an agent designed to modify Fleece issues based on user instructions.

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
- You may ONLY use the Fleece CLI tool to make modifications
- Do NOT write any files in the repository
- Do NOT make any code changes
- Focus solely on issue modifications using fleece commands

{{#if selectedIssueId}}
**Selected Issue:** {{selectedIssueId}}
{{/if}}

Available fleece commands:
- fleece list --oneline - List all issues
- fleece show <id> --json - Show issue details
- fleece edit <id> -t <title> -s <status> -d <description> - Edit issue
- fleece create -t <title> -s <status> -y <type> - Create new issue
- fleece edit <id> --parent-issues <parent>:<order> - Set parent hierarchy
- fleece list --tree - View issue hierarchy
- fleece list --next - View task graph

User request: {{userPrompt}}

title: Fix Dockerfile to specify version of Fleece CLI to install
description: Old versions of the Fleece CLI are being cached in the build process. This is installed in @{Dockerfile.base}. Change this command so that it installs a specific version of the Fleece CLI, and update the CLAUDE.md with a note that whenever the Fleece.Core version is changed in the Homespun project that the Fleece CLI version in the Dockerfile must be updated to match it.

title: Make agent starting fully asynchronous
description: When the start agent button is clicked the UI pauses on the modal while the clone is created and the session is started. This should be updated such that the starting of the agent happens in the background (it is already executed on the server, the client shouldn't need to wait). If there is an error, a notification should pop up.

title: Fix file search string interpolation
description:
When the file search is run in an input box with the "@" symbol, this opens a small window above the input box that shows the matching results, and when the enter button is pressed the file name is added to the chat window enclosed in curly braces. Change this behaviour such that curly braces are not added - quotation marks should be added only if there is a space in the name (e.g. `@"file name.txt"`). 

The curly braces are used to delimit where the search is run so that the "search mode" only applies when the cursor is within the curly braces. Update this behaviour according to the assumption that file names don't contain spaces unless they are enclosed in quotation marks.

Make the search results window that pops up the full width of the input box so it shows more of the file path.

The PR search doesn't include the curly braces and as a result the search window always pops up after the "#" is added. This should be fixed to assume that there are no spaces within a PR number and the search window should only pop up if the cursor is directly after the "#" without spaces. I am happy to not be able to add spaces in the search term, as this would break the above proposed functionality.

title: Add CTRL+C shortcut to stop sessions and go back to session page
description:
When in a session page, ctrl+c should stop the agent and go back to the session list page.

Clicking the stop button should also take the user back to the session list page.

title: Fix search behaviour for search bar
Fix issue agent diff issues
description:
When the issue agent diff is run it shows every issue as being modified rather than just the changes that have been implemented. When I start a new agent session without a prompt so there are no changes, I expect to see no differences in the issues when I go to the "review changes" page however there are a large number of changes on this page when I view it.

This appears to be an issue with copying the unstaged changes from the .fleece folder in main to the new clone, which should happen when all clones are created. Review this behaviour and ensure that the .fleece folder is **deleted** from the clone after it is created and the main .fleece folder is fully copied over.

These are the logs:

title: Search issues
description:
Create a simple text search feature that highlights and selects issues similar to Vim.

The '/' key should open the search bar and focus on it. This should be at the top of the page. When text is typed into this bar, the relevant issues should be highlighted with a border and the text that matches should be highlighted with a yellow highlight.

Hitting enter should then "embed" the search term into the viewer and hide the search bar. The first match should be selected. Typing 'n' or 'SHIFT + n' should then scroll forwards and backwards through the search results. Escape should remove the search term for the viewer and reset to the normal state. When in the search state, all other keyboard controls should act as normal - the user should still be able to use the j/k controls to go up and down the un-matched issues.

title: Side panel and tool expansion doesn't work
description: When loading the session page, an unhandled exception occurs in the side panel (Issue/PR/To-do's/Files area): 'InvalidCharacterError: Failed to execute setAttribute on Element: @onclick is not a valid attribute name.' This crashes the Blazor Server circuit, breaking all interactive functionality on the page. The error occurs consistently on every session page load. The @onclick directive is being rendered as a literal HTML attribute instead of being processed by Blazor's render tree, likely in a component that renders raw HTML or uses MarkupString incorrectly.

title: Task graph loading waits for GitHub PRs to load
description: The graph

title: Update graph rendering
description:
Make the following updates to the graph rendering on the project page.

- Merged and closed PRs are not shown in the task graph. They should be shown at the top, above the "Issues" divider. Only the most recent 5 should be shown, with a button for loading 5 more PRs at the very top of the list.
- Add markers for PR and agent status to each issue in the graph. If a PR is linked to an issue, a PR badge should show up with the PR number, that when clicked takes the user to GitHub. The agent status should show whether the agent is currently working. This has been implemented in previous versions of the graph rendering component but has since disappeared, review the codebase to port this behaviour over and clean up the old graph display.
- Add a marker showing the type of the issue.
- Show all merged PRs and issues with circles. Issues without descriptions should be hollow circles and issues with descriptions should be solid circles. The colour of the circle should be based on the agent status.

title: Combine global and project prompts
description:
There is a lot of duplication between the global prompts and the project prompts, and it looks like they are copied in by default. Instead of this, consider the project prompts to be in addition to the global prompts. Use the prompt names as keys - if the project prompts contains a new prompt with the same key, the project prompts should override the global prompt.

Add a control to the project prompts page that allows the user to create a new prompt based on an existing global prompt - this should create a new project prompt that copies the global prompt in for editing.

title: Issues not being written with full description with CTRL+Enter
description: Occasionally issues are not being persisted with their full descriptions. Review the issue updating logic to ensure that the full issue description is written to the Fleece issues JSONL file in the repository. This appears to be the case when CTRL + Enter is used to save the changes instead of clicking the "Save Changes" button, and when the user hasn't lost focus on the description box.

title: Get branch name from linked PR if one exists
description: If there is a linked PR that contains an issue ID for an issue, always use the branch name in the PR to find the clone and launch the agent in the appropriate location. If there is no PR, also check the folders in the /.clones folder to see if there are any folders with the same issue ID as the issue. Occasionally if the branch name has not been persisted and a PR is created, creating a new session for the issue can result in a new branch being created with no reference to the already created branch/PR (which has the same issue ID but different branch ID and therefore different branch name).

title: Adding a new issue at the bottom of the issues shows two inputs
description: When I select the very last issue in the graph and create a new issue below using 'o', two inline issue editors appear and one is focused. Create tests to replicate this and then create a plan to fix it.

title: Clicking on the issue type badge opens context menu to change it
description: Clicking on the type badge of an issue on the projects page should allow the type of an issue to be changed.

title: Type change menu doesn't appear when badge is clicked
description: The type change menu (where the issue type can be changed after an issue's type badge is clicked) doesn't open when the type badge is clicked. Add comprehensive test coverage for the type change menu functionality in TaskGraphView component to show that it doesn't appear, then try to fix this.

title: Fix design page
description:
When I open the /design page it immediately crashes, with the following error message logged:

net_http_message_not_success_statuscode_reason, 500, Internal Server Error

   at System.Net.Http.HttpResponseMessage.EnsureSuccessStatusCode()
   at System.Net.Http.Json.HttpClientJsonExtensions.<<FromJsonAsyncCore>g__Core|12_0>d`2[[System.Collections.Generic.List`1[[Homespun.Shared.Models.Design.ComponentMetadata, Homespun.Shared, Version=1.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null]], System.Private.CoreLib, Version=10.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=7cec85d7bea7798e],[System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions, System.Text.Json, Version=10.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=cc7b13ffcd2ddd51]].MoveNext()

   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ComponentBase.RunInitAndSetParametersAsync()
