Please review and fix.

title: Add undo feature
description:
Add the ability to undo and redo changes to the issues list. Whenever any changes are made to issues (i.e. adding and editing them), the issues list should be asynchronously persisted in the projects/{project}/history/ folder as timestamped JSONL files using the Fleece library serialization capabilities. An undo and redo button should be added to the project page UI to allow the user to go backwards and forwards in the history, updating the graph view as required. The current timestamp that is being used for the issues should also be saved such that the application is aware where in the history the user is. If the history is at a previous timestamp to the latest one and any changes are made, this should result in all of the history between the "active" timestamp and the latest timestamp being deleted, and a new timestamp added at the end of the history.

Note that merging issues should be considered as an item in the history.

title: Tab/Shift+Tab closes inline issue creation input
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls (o/O), pressing Tab or Shift+Tab to change the lane position closes the inline input instead of adjusting the lane.

## Root Cause
In InlineIssueCreateInput.razor, the Tab key is handled but the browser's default Tab behavior is not prevented. This causes focus to move away from the input, triggering HandleBlur which cancels the issue creation.

## Expected Behavior
- Tab should increase the lane offset (making new issue a parent of adjacent issue)
- Shift+Tab should decrease the lane offset (making new issue a child of adjacent issue)
- The inline input should remain focused and visible

## Actual Behavior
- Tab/Shift+Tab causes the input to lose focus
- HandleBlur is triggered, canceling the creation
- No lane adjustment occurs

## Fix Required
Add @onkeydown:preventDefault for Tab keys OR call e.preventDefault() in the JavaScript interop for Tab key handling in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor.

## Related Tests
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs document this bug and will fail when fixed.

title: New issues created with Tab/Shift+Tab don't establish parent-child relationships
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls with Tab/Shift+Tab lane modifiers, the parent-child relationships are not being established correctly.

## Expected Behavior

### When creating BELOW selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue above)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue above)

### When creating ABOVE selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue below)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue below)

## Current Status
This bug cannot be tested until PUgrNi (Tab closes input) is fixed.

## Implementation Notes
- The lane offset is tracked in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor
- The CreateIssueAsync call needs to include the parent/child relationship based on lane offset
- For Series execution mode, sort order should also be considered

## Test Coverage
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs include tests for relationship verification that will execute once PUgrNi is fixed.

title: Add 'Tab' shortcut to change issue type
description: Hitting 'Tab' on an issue when it is selected and in 'select' mode should cycle through the issue types. Add a 3 second debounce to this.

title: Get worker container status from last message
description: The container statuses are quite flaky. Review the way that statuses are held in the worker containers. They should be determined by the last message - i.e. if the last message is pending plan the status should be "awaiting plan", when the last message is "success" the status should be "awaiting input" and so on. Review the message types in the Claude Agent SDK documentation and put together a plan to fix the status endpoint on the container.

title: Make "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" consistent
description:
The titles "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" are used inconsistently throughout the application.

- Prompts should refer to the actual prompt "build", "plan", "rebase", etc. that are injected into the session when it is started. The label on the AgentControlPanel and in the left side bar should be "Prompt", rather than "Agent".
- Agents refer to a running instance of Claude Code on an issue. This should be used to replace nearly all of the "Sessions" text throughout.
- Sessions refer to a particular conversation thread that is either currently running or has been run on an agent. There will be very little user interaction with individual sessions, as the agents tend to only require one or two sessions to complete their work and this is mostly managed by the instances of Claude Code.

Put together a plan to make this terminology consistent throughout the application.

Be very careful with links between pages - after updating addresses ensure that the correct reference is used (e.g. if the /sessions/ page is changed to /agents/, change all instances of these links.

title: Persist permissions per-session and allow them to change mid-session
description:
The permissions associated with a session (i.e. the "session mode" which can be either "Build" or "Plan") is a property of the agent/worker container, and can actually be changed mid-prompt (i.e. is not necessarily sent with every prompt). Currently these seem to be persisted at the client level, and when changed in one session page changes when another session page is navigated to.

Instead of this, I would like the session mode to be pulled as part of the session information. When this session mode is changed, I would like this to be passed down to the server and then to the worker container immediately, without requiring a new prompt to be sent. There should be server and worker endpoints available to perform this function.

title: Fix branch name when branches are created
Set worker mode from prompt setting
description: The agent prompts have a "mode" setting that can be either "Build" or "Plan". It appears for the current Claude implementation that the "BypassPermissions" mode (that maps to "Plan") is always used when the agent is started. The "Plan" mode should be passed in when the prompt mode is "Plan". Please review and fix.

title: Task Graph View
description:
Implement the issues tab with TaskGraphView - the primary visualization for issues on a project.

**Primary Component: TaskGraphView**
The TaskGraphView renders issues in a lane-based hierarchical graph with SVG connectors showing parent-child relationships.

**Tasks:**
1. Create useIssues hook with filtering support (GET /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/graph)
2. Port TaskGraphLayoutService algorithm:
   - Convert TaskGraphResponse into render lines
   - Lane assignment for hierarchical depth
   - Handle hidden parent detection when filtered by depth
   - Series execution mode support (vertical chaining)
3. Build TaskGraphRow component showing:
   - Lane-based positioning with indent
   - SVG connectors to parent/child nodes
   - Issue ID, title, type badge, status badge
   - Execution mode indicator (series chain)
   - Agent status with pulse animation
   - Search term highlighting (yellow border)
4. Implement row selection (click or keyboard nav)
5. Add real-time updates via SignalR IssuesChanged event
6. Handle expand/collapse with Space key or double-click for inline details
7. Support move target selection mode (crosshair cursor)

**Render Line Types:**
- TaskGraphIssueRenderLine (issues with lane, parent lane, execution info)
- TaskGraphPrRenderLine (merged/closed PRs)
- TaskGraphSeparatorRenderLine (divider)
- TaskGraphLoadMoreRenderLine (load more PRs)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Issues display in lane-based graph visualization
- Parent-child relationships shown via SVG connectors
- Series vs parallel execution modes are visually distinct
- Clicking a row selects it for keyboard navigation
- Space/double-click expands inline details
- Color coding by type (Bug=red, Task=blue, Feature=green, Chore=gray)
- New/updated issues appear without page refresh

title: Inline Issue Details
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline detail rows for viewing issue/PR details within the task graph.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'side panel' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline expandable rows, not a separate panel.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueDetailRow**
Expands below the issue row when selected and triggered by Space key or double-click.

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueDetailRow component showing:
   - Issue ID, Type badge, Status badge, Priority badge
   - Branch name with copy button
   - Parent issue links (clickable navigation)
   - Description (markdown rendered)
   - Linked PR status badges
   - Active session info (if agent running)
   - Session cache history
2. Create InlinePrDetailRow for merged/closed PRs showing:
   - PR number, title, state badge
   - Description (markdown rendered)
   - Commits list
   - Related issues
3. Expand/collapse triggers:
   - Space key when row is selected
   - Double-click on row
4. State management for expanded rows
5. Smooth expand/collapse animation
6. Close on Escape or clicking elsewhere

**IssueRowActions (hover actions):**
- Edit button (navigate to full edit page)
- Open Panel button (for mobile full-width view)
- Run Agent button with dropdown launcher

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Space/double-click expands inline details below selected row
- All issue metadata displayed clearly
- Markdown description renders correctly
- PR status badges show checks/approval state
- Actions (Edit, Run Agent) work correctly
- Escape or click elsewhere closes expansion

title: Inline Issue Creation
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline issue creation within the task graph using Vim-like keyboard commands.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'bottom bar' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline creation triggered by keyboard commands.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueEditor**
A text input that appears in-place within the task graph when creating/editing an issue.

**Creation Commands (from Viewing mode):**
- `o` - Create issue below current selection
- `Shift+O` - Create issue above current selection

**Editing Commands (while viewing):**
- `i` - Start editing at beginning of title
- `a` - Start editing at end of title
- `r` - Replace title (clear and edit)

**Hierarchy Commands (while editing):**
- `Tab` - Make new issue PARENT of reference (indent)
- `Shift+Tab` - Make new issue SIBLING of reference parent (outdent)

**Editor Actions:**
- `Enter` - Save and return to viewing mode
- `Shift+Enter` - Save and open full edit page (for description)
- `Escape` - Cancel edit, discard changes

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueEditor component with:
   - Text input for title
   - Action buttons (Save, Save+Edit, Cancel)
   - Lane-based positioning matching selected row
2. Implement cursor position control (start, end, replace)
3. Handle Tab/Shift+Tab for hierarchy changes during creation
4. Create useCreateIssue mutation hook
5. Focus management and keyboard event handling
6. Smooth insertion animation
7. Optimistic updates (show new issue immediately)
8. Type cycling with Tab (in viewing mode): Task → Bug → Feature → Chore

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `o`/`O` opens inline editor at correct position
- Tab/Shift+Tab changes hierarchy relationship
- Enter saves, Escape cancels
- New issues appear in graph immediately
- Focus returns to graph after save/cancel

title: Update CLAUDE.md with instructions for developing React client and add vitest
description:
Update the CLAUDE.md with instructions for developing the React client. Remove the references to the Blazor front end as we will just be focusing on the front end. In these instructions, ensure that shadcn/ui with Base UI and default Tailwind are used throughout as much as possible, with the default theme of shadcn/ui (which we will style ourselves later). For the chat components, I want to use the prompt-kit library pre-built components: https://www.prompt-kit.com/docs/installation.

Add notes about using a Vertical Slice Architecture with features organised at the top level if this doesn't already exist in the CLAUDE.md.

I want to test the application using vitest. Please add this in and add instructions to the CLAUDE.md if this doesn't already exist.

title: Add web client build and test to CI and CD
description: A React web client was recently added to the repository under src/Homespun.Web. However, building and testing of this client has not been added to the CI and CD GitHub action files. Please add this.

title: Add agents for modifying issues
description:
For the Homespun.Web React client, I would like to add an agent button on the project page (next to the push button). This should run an agent that can modify the fleece issues based on instructions from the user.

It should do the following:

- Open a small popup with a prompt input and run button.
- Persist any changes that have been made in the Fleece issues to the main branch without committing them.
- Create a new clone with branch name `hsp/agent+{shortened timestamp}` from main that includes the unstaged fleece issue changes in the main repository folder.
- Start a new agent with system instructions that tell the agent not to write any files in the repository and only to make modifications using the Fleece CLI tool and the user's instruction. If this was run from the toolbar with a selected issue, that issue's ID should be included in the system prompt. This should open a session much like any other agent.
- In a session window that has been created using this method, there should be a tab that shows the issue diff (which should be available in the Fleece.Core library) and two buttons that say "Accept changes" and "Cancel".
- If "Accept changes" is clicked, the issues should be merged with the issues in the main branch and be persisted again (without commit), then the session should be stopped and the browser redirected to the projects page.
- If "Cancel" is clicked the session should be stopped and the changes discarded.

title: Update application config to launch React web client
description: Review the Tailscale and Docker Compose setup to expose the React web client at the root HTTPS address. Use port 3200 for exposing the original Blazor web application.

title: Chat window doesn't show messages
description: The chat window in the React Homespun.Web client doesn't show the message text in the responses - it shows up as empty little empty bubbles, with one per message and tool call. Update this such that at least the message text is being rendered to the screen.

title: Remaining client fixes
description:
Review and fix the following in Homespun.Web:

- The add issue above and add issue below buttons don't work. They should open the inline issue creator above or below the selected issue
- The selector to change the number of levels displayed in the graph was not ported from the Blazor app
- Add an option to the agent run prompts for "No prompt" - this should open a new session in plan mode without inject a prompt and navigate to the session page immediately.
- Add a dark mode switch to the global settings and default to the system mode.
- Change default selected model to Opus.

title: Add more issue information to the sessions page
description:
The sessions page for the Homespun.Web React client should be updated to a card based interface rather than a table. This should also include more issue information like the title, agent status and the existence of a PR.

Refer to the implementation in the Blazor client.

title: Tab, toolbar and notification styling
description:
I would like to update the styling of the tabs, toolbars and notifications.

Tabs:
- Remove the vertical scroll of the tabs
- Fix the tab underline, it currently always stays at "issues" even after navigating to another tab

Toolbar:
- Put a light border around the toolbar and make the toolbar background blur the contents that scroll behind it as it scrolls down
- Make the button style have an outline
- Use button groups and separators to group similar functionality (see Blazor application for guidance)
- Always show all buttons at all sizes, currently some buttons like move to child don't show up on mobile screens

Notifications:
- They currently show up as white or in light mode. Include a dark mode as well.

title: Fix issue type colours in graph view
description: The issue type colours are mismatched with the meaning of the issue types (e.g. bugs are green). Fix this - I believe it was initially due to the numeric remapping of enum values.

title: Update mode, prompt and model selection in session window
description:
The controls in the session window above the input box have the following issues:

- The mode selector when clicked says: "Failed to invoke 'SetSessionMode' due to an error on the server" and fails to set the new session mode.
- The model selector doesn't show the model name in text when on mobile.
- There should be a prompt selector that shows the available global and project prompts (defaulting to "No prompt"). When one is selected, it should append the text from the prompt into the end of the chat input after filling in the placeholders.

title: Manage multiple sessions
description:
Multiple sessions on the same branch should be accessible. The Claude worker should be used as the single source of truth - I believe the management of multiple sessions is possible using the Typescript SDK.

I want to have the "session" page represent all the sessions that have been run on an issue. A session tab should be added to the information panel that shows all past sessions, each of which represents a context window. Clicking on these should open previous sessions as read-only.

When the agent session page is navigated to, show the latest active session only. There should also be a button to clear context and add a new session for this agent worker container.

Clarify the difference between "agent" (a worker container running on a cloned issue branch) and a session ("a single conversation with that "agent" when working.

title: Add logging for status changes in worker
description:
There are ongoing issues where the status of the worker container doesn't represent what it is actually doing (e.g. saying it is waiting while it is still streaming back input, saying it has a plan pending after the plan has been executed, etc.). The status should be persisted on the worker and changed when certain messages are sent back to the server or received (e.g. when any agent message or tool invocation is sent, the agent is clearly "working").

Add logging to the worker that indicates whenever a status is changed. It should say "Status changed from 'working' to 'waiting'" or something similar.

title: Fix agent status and session chats
Update React client logging to Promtail
description: The server container logs to a PLG stack, which is set up using Docker Compose. The Homespun.Web React client should also log to the PLG stack using OpenTelemetry. This should be tagged with Component="Client" such that the client logs can be separated.

title: Install Typescript LSP and Claude Code plugin
description: In the worker container Dockerfile, install the Typescript LSP and Claude Code Typescript plugin. The plugin should be installed with `claude plugin install typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official` but the Typescript LSP server may also need to be installed. Review this by installing it locally first and verifying that it works, then updating the Dockerfile.

title: Add logging of message content to worker and improve message logging
description:
We have recently status change logging to the workers to determine when the statuses are changing in the workers.

However, it is difficult to do this without the message content as context. Add "debug" logging that includes tool uses (just the tool information) and the message content so that we can understand what is occurring. This should be configurable when the agent containers are started - update the server code to always set this "debug" flag to true (for now) when the containers are started in the DockerAgentExecutionService.

Also, review the Grafana dashboard in config/grafana to improve the logging outputs - currently it shows the timestamp and level, followed by the "Parsed fields" in badges and then the "Log line" as text. The log line is just `[IssueID] LogLevel [logger.js]`. I would like this to instead be `Timestamp LogLevel [SourceContext] :  Message` for the worker logs, and for the "Parsed fields" to be removed from the log output on the dashboard.

title: Allow local only repositories
description: I would like to be able to run Homespun without the GitHub integrations as well - mostly for testing. Please review the architecture and plan to be able to detach the GitHub service for certain local-only projects. In this instance, creating a new project should just create the source folder adjacent to the other GitHub related projects. There will need to be a flag added to the project registration in homespun-data.json to note that it is not a GitHub backed project.

title: Move all Homespun related items to tags in the Fleece issuesa
Switch rendering state for series and parallel issues
description: The rendering of series and parallel types in the issue graph is reversed - series issues look like parallel and parallel issues look like series. Swap this.

title: Fix session mode button and model selector
description:
Clicking on the session mode button in the session chat window or using the SHIFT+TAB shortcut doesn't show any change in the session mode in the UI, and doesn't appear to pass the message on to the server or worker.

When changing the model selector it changes just below the slab title in the UI, but the server says "Failed to set model on worker for session 25e2c246-b0e9-47bf-b8a5-a5a1f3290a34".

This relates to the UI changes that were merged in PR #508 and #503.

title: Add default filters
description: The filters shown for the issue graph should default to showing "next" issues and issues that are assigned to the current user. When the 'f' key is pressed, the caret in the filter box should be placed at the end of the text box for adding more filters.

title: Fix working status rings and prevent multiple concurrent agents
description: The issues graph used to contain rings around each of the nodes that had an agent working on them, with the colour of the ring representing the status. This is no longer shown, likely due to the changes in PR #526. Please review and fix the issue.

title: Fix issues agent button
description: When the issues agent button is clicked, a new branch is cloned and a new issues agent session is started and opened. When a prompt is typed in, however, the agent immediately goes into an error state. Review the logs using your logs skill. The worker container appears to have not been started when I use `docker ps` on the host machine.

title: Add simple issue tree view
description:
I want to try using traditional hierarchical tree view of the issues to see if this is more appropriate. Add a button that switches the view mode to the toolbar. Instead of the current task graph view that shows the next issues at the root and their parents as leaves, flip this to show the parent issues on the left side and their children progressing down and to the right. Still show the graph nodes and edges but matching the new tree structure.

If a node has multiple parents, just show it once when it is first traversed in the tree.

Remove the merged and closed pull requests from the top of the graph in this view mode.

title: Fix issues agent issue merging
description:
I just used the issues agent for the first time to add a description to an issue. It successfully modified the issue details in the cloned branch that was created, however the diff view did not seem to pick up that there were changes in the issues - I assume this is because the diff is only loaded once. The issues from the clone and main branch should be re-read whenever the diff view is opened, and when the issues from the cloned branch are to be merged back in.

In addition to this, in the graph view only show the issues that have changed.

title: Fix issue agent merging
description: The FleeceChangeDetectionService and merging of issues into main appears to adopt the changes from the issue agent branch entirely rather than merge the issues from the main branch and the issues from the issue agent branch using the timestamp sensitive merging algorithm provided by Fleece.Core. Review this and update it to use the merging algorithm for both the diff calculation and the merging of accepted.

title: Review parsing of status when loading diff
Add prompt "code" mode
description:
In the prompts pages (both the local and global pages), add a tab that switches between the normal prompts view (with the prompts listed in cards) and a "code" mode that shows a text box with the prompts serialized as raw JSON. I want to be able to use this to be able to "import" and "export" all prompts quickly.

There should be an apply button at the bottom that checks that the JSON is valid and then updates the prompts, and a revert button that deserializes the prompts again and undoes and changes in the text box.

title: Add issue specific issues agent button and prompt
description:
There is currently a prompt named "IssueModify" in the prompt list, and an "issues agent" button in the toolbar.

Do the following:
- The "IssueModify" prompt should be moved to a separate category with the heading "Issue agent prompts", where there is a fixed single prompt used when running the issue modification agent on an issue, and a fixed system prompt that is used for all issue agents. These can be modified - they are fixed in the sense that there cannot be more than one prompt for each.
- Change the behaviour of the issues agent button in the toolbar to be specific for the selected issue. The modal that pops up should have a text box that allows for instructions to be added in before running the agent (e.g. rename this issue), and should show which issue is selected below this. When the Start Agent button is clicked, this should then start an issue agent, use the IssueModify prompt and replace the placeholders with details of the selected issue and the instructions, and send the prompt to the agent similar to running other agents. If no instructions are provided, just open the session page with no prompt, but include the system prompt.

title: Implement basic workflow execution service
description:
Create the basic workflow execution service with linear (non-parallel) execution for MVP.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowExecutionService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowExecutionService.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `StartWorkflowAsync(workflowId, triggerContext)` - Start execution
- `GetExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Get execution state
- `PauseExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Pause execution
- `ResumeExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Resume execution  
- `CancelExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Cancel execution

## Implementation Details (MVP - Linear Only)
- Execute nodes in topological order (one at a time)
- Track execution state (pending, running, paused, completed, failed)
- Handle node completion/failure callbacks
- Store execution state to disk for crash recovery
- Background hosted service for long-running executions

## Note
DAG parallel execution will be added in Phase 2. This task focuses on linear execution only.

title: Add WorkflowComplete MCP tool to worker
description:
Add the WorkflowComplete MCP tool to the TypeScript worker for agents to signal workflow node completion.

## Files to Modify
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/session-manager.ts` - Add tool handling in canUseTool callback

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/tools/workflow-tools.ts` - Tool definitions

## Tool Schema
\`\`\`typescript
WorkflowComplete: {
  description: "Signal workflow node completion with output data",
  input_schema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      status: { type: "string", enum: ["success", "partial", "needs_review"] },
      outputs: { type: "object", description: "Typed output data" },
      summary: { type: "string", description: "Human-readable summary" },
      artifacts: { type: "array", items: { name, path, type } }
    },
    required: ["status", "outputs", "summary"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Intercept WorkflowComplete tool calls in canUseTool callback
2. Emit workflow_tool event to SSE stream with tool data
3. Allow tool execution to complete (signals session end)
4. Only enable tool when session has workflowContext set

## Pattern Reference
Follow existing AskUserQuestion/ExitPlanMode handling in session-manager.ts

title: Implement workflow context store
description:
Create the workflow context store for managing accumulated state between nodes.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowContextStore.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowContextStore.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `GetContextAsync(executionId)` - Get full context for execution
- `SetValueAsync(executionId, key, value)` - Set a context variable
- `GetValueAsync(executionId, key)` - Get a specific value
- `MergeNodeOutputAsync(executionId, nodeId, output)` - Store node output

## Context Model
\`\`\`csharp
public record WorkflowContext {
    string ExecutionId;
    string WorkflowId;
    string WorkingDirectory;
    JsonElement TriggerData;
    Dictionary<string, NodeOutput> NodeOutputs;  // Completed node outputs
    Dictionary<string, JsonElement> Variables;   // User-defined variables
    List<WorkflowArtifact> Artifacts;            // Produced artifacts
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
- Store context as JSON file: `.workflows/context_{executionId}.json`
- In-memory cache with ConcurrentDictionary
- Persist on every write for crash recovery

POST   /api/workflows/{id}/execute        # Start execution (manual trigger)

GET    /api/executions/{id}               # Get execution state
POST   /api/executions/{id}/cancel        # Cancel execution
GET    /api/executions/{id}/context       # Get execution context
\`\`\`

- Return typed DTOs from Homespun.Shared
- Add proper error handling and validation

Follow `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Controllers/IssuesController.cs`

title: Implement DAG builder with cycle detection
description:
Create DAG building and validation utilities for workflow graphs.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowDagBuilder.cs`

## Features
1. **Build DAG from workflow definition**
   - Parse nodes and edges into graph structure
   - Identify entry nodes (no incoming edges)
   - Identify exit nodes (no outgoing edges)

2. **Cycle Detection**
   - Use DFS/BFS to detect cycles
   - Return clear error message with cycle path
   - Validate before workflow save/execution

3. **Topological Sort**
   - Return execution order for linear mode
   - Handle multiple valid orderings

4. **Utilities**
   - `GetReadyNodes(completedNodes)` - Nodes with all dependencies satisfied
   - `GetDependencies(nodeId)` - Get upstream dependencies
   - `GetDependents(nodeId)` - Get downstream dependents

WorkflowBranch: {
  description: "Signal a conditional branch decision",

    type: "object",

      decision: { type: "string", description: "Branch path to take" },

- **Branch Selection**: Before starting agent, determine correct base branch:

  - If prior issue has open PR, use that PR's branch as base

- **Dependency Validation**: Halt with warning if:
  - Issue has open child issues
  - Issue has open prior sibling issues

4. **notify**
   - Send notifications via configured channels

5. **http_request**

   - Configure method, headers, body
   - Output: response body

6. **run_command**
   - Execute shell commands
   - Configure working directory, timeout
   - Output: stdout, exit code

- Receive branch lineage information from workflow context
- Pass created PR branch to subsequent nodes for stacking

- Clear error messages for permanent failures

   - Output: parsed data structure

2. **filter**
   - JavaScript expression evaluation
   - Filter arrays/objects from context
   - Output: filtered data

3. **aggregate**
   - Collect outputs from multiple nodes
   - Merge objects
   - Concatenate arrays
   - Output: aggregated result

4. **map**
   - Rename keys
   - Transform values with JavaScript
   - Output: mapped data

5. **custom**
   - Execute JavaScript function
   - Full access to context
   - Return arbitrary data

## Implementation
- Use JavaScript interpreter (Jint) for expressions
- Sandbox execution for security
- Clear error messages for invalid expressions

title: Implement workflow trigger service
description:
Create the trigger service for managing workflow triggers.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowTriggerService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowTriggerService.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `RegisterTriggersAsync(workflow)` - Register triggers for workflow
- `UnregisterTriggersAsync(workflowId)` - Remove triggers
- `EvaluateTriggerAsync(trigger, event)` - Check if event matches trigger

## Trigger Types
1. **Manual** - No registration needed, triggered via API
2. **Event** - Subscribe to internal event bus
3. **Scheduled** - Register with scheduler service

## Event Types to Support
- `issue_created` - When new issue is created
- `issue_status_changed` - When issue status changes
- `pr_merged` - When PR is merged
- `webhook` - External webhook triggers

## Stacked PR Integration
- **pr_merged Event**: Critical for stacked PR workflow continuation
  - When a PR is merged, trigger execution of next issue in the task graph

title: Add execution history and logging
description:
Track workflow execution history for debugging and analytics.

## Features
1. **Execution Records**

2. **Execution Logs**

3. **History Queries**
   - List executions for workflow
   - Filter by status, date range
   - Get detailed execution with node history

4. **Cleanup Policy**

- `GET /api/executions/{id}` - Get execution detail

   - This helps users understand which prompts are project-specific

title: Add isOverride flag to prompt response DTO
description:
## Overview
Add an `isOverride` boolean field to the prompt response DTO to indicate when a project prompt overrides a global prompt with the same name.

## Implementation Steps

### Backend Changes

1. **Update AgentPrompt model** (`src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Sessions/AgentPrompt.cs`):
   - Add `IsOverride` boolean property (default false)
   - This field is computed at query time, not persisted

2. **Update AgentPromptService** (`src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs`):
   - In `GetPromptsForProject(projectId)` method:
     - For each project prompt, check if a global prompt exists with the same name
     - Set `IsOverride = true` for project prompts that shadow global prompts
   - Keep existing behavior of returning project prompts + non-overridden global prompts

3. **Regenerate API client**:
   - Run `npm run generate:api:fetch` in Homespun.Web

## Testing

- Write unit tests in `Homespun.Tests` verifying:
  - `IsOverride` is true when project prompt shadows global prompt
  - `IsOverride` is false for global prompts
  - `IsOverride` is false for project prompts with unique names

- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/api/` (regenerated)

title: Create project prompt override when editing global prompt
description:
## Overview
When a user edits a global prompt on the project prompts page, create a new project prompt copy that overrides the global prompt instead of modifying the global prompt directly.

## Implementation Steps

### Backend Changes

1. **Add endpoint to create override** (`src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Controllers/AgentPromptsController.cs`):
   - Add `POST /api/agent-prompts/create-override` endpoint
   - Parameters: `globalPromptId`, `projectId`, optional `initialMessage` (new content)
   - Logic:
     - Fetch the global prompt by ID
     - Create a new prompt with:
       - Same `Name` as the global prompt
       - `ProjectId` set to the provided project
       - `InitialMessage` from parameter or copied from global
       - `Mode` copied from global
     - Return the new project prompt

2. **Update AgentPromptService** (`src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs`):
   - Add `CreateOverride(globalPromptId, projectId, initialMessage?)` method
   - Validate that the global prompt exists and has no projectId
   - Create and persist the new project prompt

### Frontend Changes

3. **Update PromptsList edit handling** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/components/prompts-list.tsx`):
   - When on project prompts page and editing a prompt:
     - If the prompt is a global prompt (no `isOverride` flag and no projectId), call create-override endpoint
     - If the prompt is already a project prompt, use normal update endpoint

4. **Add new hook** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/hooks/`):
   - Create `useCreateOverride` mutation hook
   - Invalidate both project-prompts and agent-prompts queries on success

5. **Regenerate API client**:
   - Run `npm run generate:api:fetch`

## Testing

- Unit tests for `CreateOverride` service method
- API tests for the new endpoint
- Component tests for edit flow detecting global vs project prompts

## Files to Modify
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Controllers/AgentPromptsController.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/IAgentPromptService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/components/prompts-list.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/hooks/` (new hook file)

title: Add Remove override button for project prompts
description:
## Overview
Add a "Remove override" button to project prompts that override global prompts. Clicking this button deletes the project prompt, reverting to the global prompt.

## Implementation Steps

### Backend Changes

1. **Add endpoint to remove override** (`src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Controllers/AgentPromptsController.cs`):
   - Add `DELETE /api/agent-prompts/{id}/override` endpoint
   - Validates:
     - The prompt exists and is a project prompt (has projectId)
     - A global prompt with the same name exists (is actually an override)
   - Deletes the project prompt
   - Returns success with the global prompt that will now take effect

### Frontend Changes

2. **Update PromptCard component** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/components/prompt-card.tsx`):
   - When `isOverride === true`, show "Remove override" button/menu item
   - Add confirmation dialog: "This will remove the project-specific prompt and revert to the global prompt. Continue?"
   - On confirm, call the remove-override endpoint

3. **Add new hook** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/hooks/`):
   - Create `useRemoveOverride` mutation hook
   - Invalidate project-prompts and agent-prompts queries on success

4. **Regenerate API client**:
   - Run `npm run generate:api:fetch`

## UI Design

The "Remove override" action should:
- Appear in the card's dropdown menu (alongside Edit and Delete)
- Use a different icon to distinguish from Delete (e.g., undo/revert icon)
- Include tooltip: "Revert to global prompt"

## Testing

- Unit tests for remove-override service method
- API tests for the endpoint validation logic
- Component tests for button visibility based on `isOverride` flag
- Component tests for confirmation dialog flow

## Files to Modify
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Controllers/AgentPromptsController.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/IAgentPromptService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/components/prompt-card.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/hooks/` (new hook file)

title: Update project prompts page to show merged prompts
description:
## Overview
Update the project prompts page to display all available prompts (project + global) with visual distinction between them, rather than only showing project-specific prompts.

## Implementation Steps

### Frontend Changes

1. **Update useProjectPrompts hook or create new hook**:
   - Currently `useProjectPrompts` fetches only project-scoped prompts
   - Create new `useMergedProjectPrompts(projectId)` hook that:
     - Calls `getApiAgentPromptsAvailableForProjectByProjectId()` (existing endpoint)

2. **Update project prompts route** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.prompts.tsx`):

3. **Update PromptsList component** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/features/prompts/components/prompts-list.tsx`):

   - Consider adding a section header or filter: "Project Prompts" / "Inherited Global Prompts"

4. **Add visual indicators**:

title: Use normal issues merging on issues agent completion
description:
When the changes are accepted from a run of the issues agent, it looks like the changes are applied one by one by the FleeceChangeApplicationService. This seemingly results in an error - for example see below for error with uo6D4m. It is not clear what the error was, but this shouldn't be required anyway - instead of applying these changes, just copy the new issues_{hash}.json files from the issues agent branch to the main branch, and use the normal merging behaviour to read all files and merge them back together - this has been proven to in complex situations.

title: Serialize issues changes when branch ID is created
description: When the branch ID is automatically generated using the mini prompt, ensure that the issues are serialized into the issues_{hash}.jsonl file immediately afterwards. This serialization should occur whenever any issues are changed.

title: Hover and selection highlight obscures item below
description: When an item in the issue graph is hovered or selected, the resulting highlight appears over the graph rather than in the layer behind it. Either move the highlight to beneath the layer, or replace it with a solid border rather than full background shading.

title: Fix edge layout to avoid using row-by-row rendering
description: The rendering of edges in the task graph appear to still be on a row-by-row basis for the Konva graph rendering mode. This works well for issues that are connected to the issue directly below them, but when the connections are more complex the edges are often missed or terminated at the bottom of the row without continuing. The Konva graph rendering mode was added specifically to avoid this problem. Remove the row-by-row edge layout algorithm entirely and replace it with an edge layout algorithm that generates edges based on the X and Y positions of the child node and parent node. It must also obey the rules for the edge to node position and the execution mode - if the parent execution mode is series, it should leave the child at the bottom and enter the parent at the left, and if parallel it should leave the child on the right and enter the parent from the top.

title: Update to Fleece version 1.6.2

In particular, review changes in https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/pull/86 where IMergeService was added. This should be used to generate the issue diff when an issue agent is run.

title: Fix bugs with running agents
description:
I am having some issues with running agents on some issues that was seemingly introduced in PR #581. When I click the start button nothing happens - there are no logs in the server that indicate that an agent is starting, and when I look at the browser logs it says that there is an agent already starting (a response it gets from the server). The agent never starts, and I must restart the server to be able to start an agent on that issue (starting an agent on another issue seems to work).

Come up with a plan to fix this and add more diagnostic logging on the server side regarding the agent execution queue to help me work out what is happening here.

title: Have issues agent add branch ID
description:
# Have Issues Agent Add Branch ID + Post-Merge Assignment

## Overview

Two related changes to improve issue management when issue agents create or modify issues:

1. **Branch ID on issues** - Ensure the issues agent adds the branch ID to issues it creates/modifies
2. **Post-merge assignment** - After merging issue agent changes back, assign any unassigned issues to the active user

## Post-Merge Assignment Implementation

When issue agent changes are merged back into the main branch (or the active working branch), a post-processing step should check all issues that were created or modified in that merge and assign them to the active user if they don't already have an `assignedTo` value.

### Implementation Steps

1. **Identify the merge point** - Find where issue agent branch changes are merged back
   - Look in `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/` or `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/` for the merge/post-processing logic
   - This likely happens after the agent session completes and its changes are integrated

2. **Detect newly created/modified issues** - After the merge, diff the `.fleece/` directory to identify which issues were created or modified by the agent

3. **Assign unassigned issues** - For each issue that was created or modified:
   - Read the issue using `fleece show <id> --json`
   - If `assignedTo` is null or empty, assign it to the current active user
   - Use `fleece edit <id> -a <user-email>` to set the assignment

4. **Determine the active user** - The active user should be derived from the session context (the user who initiated the agent session)

5. **Add tests**
   - Unit test: post-merge hook correctly identifies unassigned issues
   - Unit test: already-assigned issues are not overwritten
   - Integration test: end-to-end flow from agent merge to assignment

### Relationship to 826Mnb

Issue `826Mnb` handles assignment at creation time by updating agent prompts. This issue provides a complementary safety net at merge time - even if an agent forgets or fails to assign a user, the post-merge step ensures all issues have an owner.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After merging issue agent changes, unassigned issues are assigned to the active user
- [ ] Issues that already have an `assignedTo` value are not overwritten
- [ ] The active user is correctly identified from the session context
- [ ] Branch ID is added to issues created by the agent

title: Add issue relationship query methods to FleeceService
description:
Add methods to FleeceService for querying issue relationships needed for stacked PR resolution.

## Implementation

### Interface Changes
**File:** `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/IFleeceService.cs`

Add methods:
```csharp
/// <summary>
/// Gets the prior sibling in series execution (sibling with lower sortOrder in same parent).
/// Returns null if no prior sibling exists.
/// </summary>
Task<Issue?> GetPriorSiblingAsync(string projectPath, string issueId);

/// <summary>
/// Gets all direct children of an issue.
/// </summary>
Task<IReadOnlyList<Issue>> GetChildrenAsync(string projectPath, string issueId);

/// <summary>
/// Checks if an issue has blocking dependencies (open children or open prior siblings).
/// Returns details about what is blocking execution.
/// </summary>
Task<BlockingIssuesResult> GetBlockingIssuesAsync(string projectPath, string issueId);
```

### Result Type
```csharp
public record BlockingIssuesResult(
    IReadOnlyList<Issue> OpenChildren,
    IReadOnlyList<Issue> OpenPriorSiblings)
{
    public bool IsBlocked => OpenChildren.Count > 0 || OpenPriorSiblings.Count > 0;
}
```

### Implementation Details
**File:** `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceService.cs`

**GetPriorSiblingAsync:**
1. Get the target issue
2. For each parent in issue.ParentIssues:
   - Find all siblings (issues with same parent)
   - Filter to those with sortOrder < target's sortOrder
   - Return the one with highest sortOrder (immediate prior)
3. Return first match or null

**GetChildrenAsync:**
1. List all issues in project
2. Filter to those where parentIssues contains the target issueId
3. Sort by sortOrder

**GetBlockingIssuesAsync:**
1. Get children, filter to open statuses (Open, Progress, Review)
2. Get prior siblings, filter to open statuses
3. Return combined result

### Unit Tests
**File:** `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/Fleece/FleeceServiceRelationshipTests.cs`

Test cases:
- GetPriorSiblingAsync with no siblings → returns null
- GetPriorSiblingAsync with prior siblings → returns immediate prior
- GetPriorSiblingAsync with only later siblings → returns null
- GetChildrenAsync with no children → returns empty
- GetChildrenAsync with children → returns sorted list
- GetBlockingIssuesAsync with no blockers → IsBlocked = false
- GetBlockingIssuesAsync with open children → returns them
- GetBlockingIssuesAsync with open prior siblings → returns them
- GetBlockingIssuesAsync with completed siblings → not blocked

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] IFleeceService interface extended with new methods
- [ ] FleeceService implements all methods
- [ ] BlockingIssuesResult type defined
- [ ] Unit tests pass for all scenarios

title: Add PR lookup by issue to GitHubService
description:
Add method to look up PRs linked to a Fleece issue for stacked PR resolution.

## Implementation

### Interface Changes
**File:** `src/Homespun.Server/Features/GitHub/IGitHubService.cs`

Add method:
```csharp
/// <summary>
/// Gets the PR linked to a Fleece issue, if one exists.
/// </summary>
Task<PullRequest?> GetPullRequestForIssueAsync(string projectId, string issueId);
```

### Implementation
**File:** `src/Homespun.Server/Features/GitHub/GitHubService.cs`

```csharp
public async Task<PullRequest?> GetPullRequestForIssueAsync(string projectId, string issueId)
{
    var prs = await _dataStore.GetPullRequestsByProjectAsync(projectId);
    return prs.FirstOrDefault(pr => pr.BeadsIssueId == issueId);
}
```

### Unit Tests
**File:** `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/GitHub/GitHubServicePrLookupTests.cs`

Test cases:
- GetPullRequestForIssueAsync with matching PR → returns PR
- GetPullRequestForIssueAsync with no matching PR → returns null
- GetPullRequestForIssueAsync with multiple PRs → returns correct one
- GetPullRequestForIssueAsync filters by projectId

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] IGitHubService interface extended with new method
- [ ] GitHubService implements the method
- [ ] Unit tests pass for all scenarios

Update default prompts

```json
    "mode": "build"
  },
  {

    "name": "Build and Merge",

**ID:** {{id}}
**Type:** {{type}}
**Branch:** {{branch}}

### Description
{{description}}

---

Please implement this issue. Start by understanding the requirements and exploring the codebase, then proceed with the implementation. Create a PR when all tests pass. When all tests pass in CI and there are no merge conflicts, perform a squash merge to main. **DO NOT** merge unless all tests pass in CI. If they do not pass the first time, stop execution.",
    "mode": "build"
  },
  {
    "id": "7559d2",
    "name": "IssueModify",
    "initialMessage": "## Issue Modification Request

You are an agent designed to modify Fleece issues based on user instructions.

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
- You may ONLY use the Fleece CLI tool to make modifications
- Do NOT write any files in the repository
- Do NOT make any code changes
- Focus solely on issue modifications using fleece commands

{{#if selectedIssueId}}
**Selected Issue:** {{selectedIssueId}}
{{/if}}

Available fleece commands:
- fleece list --oneline - List all issues
- fleece show <id> --json - Show issue details
- fleece edit <id> -t <title> -s <status> -d <description> - Edit issue
- fleece create -t <title> -s <status> -y <type> - Create new issue
- fleece edit <id> --parent-issues <parent>:<order> - Set parent hierarchy
- fleece list --tree - View issue hierarchy
- fleece list --next - View task graph

User request: {{userPrompt}}",
    "mode": "build"
  },
  {
    "id": "a91ce6",
    "name": "Review PR comments",
    "initialMessage": "Some comments have been left in the PR related to this branch. Review these comments and address them, writing a return comment describing what you have changed.

Commit and push to update the PR once all comments have been addressed and all tests pass.",
    "mode": "build"
  },
  {
    "id": "81d107",
    "name": "IssueAgentSystem",
    "initialMessage": "# Issue Agent System Prompt

You are an agent specialized in managing Fleece issues. Your role is to help users organize, modify, and maintain their issue tracking.

The issues contain descriptions that will be executed by coding agents. Make the descriptions contain suitably detailed plans such that they can be executed without further planning, and of a suitable size that they can be executed within the context window of an agentic coding system like Claude Code.

Break up tasks into a issue hierarchy. Parent issues can have a "series" or "parallel" execution mode that govern whether the child issues are executed in series or parallel. Consider this when planning - try to create parallel issues where possible to improve execution speed but do not create plans where parallel issues would overlap.

Note that all child issues must be complete for a parent issues to be executed - the description of parent issues should contain an overarching summary of the tasks and instructions on how to verify all child issues were completed.

## CONSTRAINTS

**IMPORTANT: You must follow these constraints strictly:**

1. **Only use the Fleece CLI** - All modifications must be done through `fleece` commands
2. **No file modifications** - Do NOT write, create, or modify any files in the repository
3. **No code changes** - Do NOT make any code changes or edits to source files
4. **Focus on issues only** - Your sole purpose is issue management

## AVAILABLE COMMANDS

### Viewing Issues
- `fleece list --oneline` - List all issues in compact format
- `fleece list --tree` - View issues as parent-child hierarchy
- `fleece list --next` - View task graph showing execution order
- `fleece show <id> --json` - Show full details of a specific issue

### Modifying Issues
- `fleece edit <id> -t <title>` - Update issue title
- `fleece edit <id> -s <status>` - Update status (open, progress, review, complete, archived, closed)
- `fleece edit <id> -d <description>` - Update description
- `fleece edit <id> -y <type>` - Update type (task, bug, chore, feature)
- `fleece dependency --parent <parent-id> --child <child-id> [--first|--last|--after <id>]` - Set parent relationship

### Creating Issues
- `fleece create -t <title> -s <status> -y <type>` - Create new issue
- `fleece create -t <title> -y <type> --parent-issues <parent-id>:<lex-order>` - Create as child of existing issue

## BEST PRACTICES

1. **Always verify first** - Before modifying an issue, use `fleece show <id> --json` to understand its current state
2. **Use the hierarchy** - Break down large tasks into sub-tasks using parent-child relationships
3. **Status workflow** - Issues typically flow: open → progress → review → complete
4. **Be precise** - Make targeted changes based on user instructions
5. **Confirm changes** - After making changes, verify with `fleece show <id> --json`

## ISSUE TYPES
- `task` - General work items
- `bug` - Defects or issues to fix
- `feature` - New functionality
- `chore` - Maintenance or housekeeping

## STATUS VALUES
- `open` - Not yet started
- `progress` - Currently being worked on
- `review` - Ready for review
- `complete` - Finished
- `archived` - No longer relevant
- `closed` - Abandoned or won't fix",
    "mode": "build",
    "sessionType": "issueAgentSystem"
  },
  {
    "id": "b96201",
    "name": "IssueAgentModification",
    "initialMessage": "## Issue Modification Request

You are an agent designed to modify Fleece issues based on user instructions.

{{#if selectedIssueId}}
**Selected Issue:** {{selectedIssueId}}
{{/if}}

User request: {{userPrompt}}",
    "mode": "build",
    "sessionType": "issueAgentModification"
  }
]
```

title: Create Azure VM infra with Bicep templates
description:
# Create Azure VM Infrastructure with Bicep

Create Bicep templates to provision Azure VMs for Homespun deployment, enabling easy multi-user infrastructure setup using Infrastructure as Code.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Create Bicep template files**
   - Add `infra/main.bicep` - Main orchestration template
   - Add `infra/modules/vm.bicep` - VM resource definition
   - Add `infra/modules/network.bicep` - Networking (VNet, NSG, public IP)
   - Add `infra/main.bicepparam` - Parameter file with defaults

2. **VM Provisioning (Bicep)**
   - Define resource group via deployment scope
   - Provision VM with parameterized size (default Standard_D4s_v3)
   - Configure NSG rules for ports 80, 443, SSH
   - Set up managed identity for Azure resources
   - Use cloud-init custom data for software bootstrapping

3. **Cloud-Init Script**
   - Add `infra/cloud-init.yaml` for VM software setup
   - Install Docker and Docker Compose
   - Install required dependencies (Git, etc.)
   - Clone Homespun repository
   - Set up SSL certificates (Let's Encrypt or Azure-managed)
   - Start Docker containers with auto-restart policy

4. **Configuration**
   - Generate secure secrets for the deployment
   - Configure environment variables via Bicep parameters
   - Set up GitHub OAuth application details
   - Configure Loki/Grafana if using observability stack

5. **User Setup**
   - Create initial admin user
   - Document how to add additional users
   - Set up SSH access for administrators

6. **Parameterization**
   - Accept parameters for: resource group name, VM size, region, domain name
   - Use Bicep parameter validation (`@allowed`, `@minLength`, `@description`)
   - Provide sensible defaults in parameter file

7. **Deployment script**
   - Add `scripts/deploy-infra.sh` - Wrapper to deploy Bicep via `az deployment sub create`
   - Add `scripts/teardown-infra.sh` - Cleanup script using `az group delete`

8. **Documentation**
   - Add step-by-step documentation for setting up a VM on Azure using these templates.

## Files to Create

- `infra/main.bicep` - Main Bicep template
- `infra/modules/vm.bicep` - VM module
- `infra/modules/network.bicep` - Networking module
- `infra/main.bicepparam` - Parameter file
- `infra/cloud-init.yaml` - Cloud-init configuration
- `scripts/deploy-infra.sh` - Deployment wrapper script
- `scripts/teardown-infra.sh` - Teardown wrapper script
- `docs/AZURE_DEPLOYMENT.md` - Documentation for the templates

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Bicep templates provision working Azure VM with Homespun running
- [ ] All required ports are accessible via NSG rules
- [ ] Docker containers start automatically on boot via cloud-init
- [ ] Deployment is idempotent (Bicep handles incremental deployments)
- [ ] Teardown script cleanly removes all resources
- [ ] Documentation explains all parameters and deployment steps
- [ ] Bicep linting passes (`az bicep lint`)

title: Implement TaskQueue class
description:
Create a TaskQueue class that represents a single sequential execution pipeline for processing issues.

## Requirements

### TaskQueue Class
- Location: `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/TaskQueue.cs`
- Each queue has a unique ID, a state (Idle, Running, Blocked, Completed), and an ordered list of pending issues
- Processes one issue at a time by delegating to AgentStartBackgroundService
- Emits events when: an issue starts, completes, fails, or the queue state changes
- Supports enqueue, dequeue, pause, and cancel operations
- Tracks the current executing issue and maintains execution history

### Queue State Machine
```
Idle → Running (when issue enqueued and processing starts)
Running → Blocked (when current issue is blocked by dependencies)
Running → Idle (when current issue completes and queue is empty)
Running → Running (when current issue completes and more are queued)
Running → Completed (when all issues processed or queue cancelled)
Blocked → Running (when blocking dependency resolves)
```

### Interface
- `ITaskQueue` interface for testability
- Async enumerable or callback-based completion notification
- CancellationToken support for graceful shutdown

## Tests
- Unit test queue state transitions
- Test enqueue/dequeue ordering
- Test cancellation behavior
- Test blocked state when dependency not met

title: Implement QueueCoordinator service
description:
Create a QueueCoordinator service that manages multiple TaskQueues for a project, spawning queues based on issue execution modes.

## Requirements

### QueueCoordinator Class
- Location: `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/QueueCoordinator.cs`
- Manages all active TaskQueues for a single project
- Determines how many queues to create based on the issue hierarchy's execution modes:
  - **Series parent**: feeds children into one queue sequentially
  - **Parallel parent**: creates one queue per child, running concurrently
- Enforces a configurable max concurrency limit per project
- Tracks overall execution progress across all queues
- Handles queue lifecycle: creation, monitoring, teardown

### Queue Spawning Logic
Given a root issue to execute:
1. Fetch its children from FleeceService
2. Check the parent's execution mode
3. If series: create one queue, enqueue children in order
4. If parallel: create N queues (one per child), each with that child enqueued
5. For nested hierarchies, recursively apply the same logic as each queue processes child issues that themselves have children

### Coordination
- When a queue completes, check if all sibling queues are also complete
- When all queues for a parallel group complete, signal the parent as ready
- Provide a method to start execution from any issue in the tree
- SignalR broadcasting for queue status changes

### Interface
- `IQueueCoordinator` with methods: `StartExecution(projectId, issueId)`, `GetActiveQueues(projectId)`, `CancelAll(projectId)`, `GetStatus(projectId)`
- Register as singleton in DI

## Tests
- Test series mode creates single queue with correct ordering
- Test parallel mode creates multiple queues
- Test max concurrency enforcement
- Test nested series-within-parallel and parallel-within-series
- Test completion signaling when all queues finish

title: Refactor agent start to support workflow context
description:
Extend AgentStartBackgroundService to carry workflow execution context when starting agent sessions.

## Changes

### Add Workflow Fields to AgentStartRequest
- `WorkflowExecutionId` (string?, nullable) - ID of the active workflow execution
- `WorkflowStepIndex` (int?, nullable) - Index of the current step being executed
- `WorkflowStepId` (string?, nullable) - ID of the current workflow step

### Modify StartAgentAsync
When workflow fields are present on the request:
1. After session creation, notify `WorkflowExecutionService.OnStepStarted(executionId, stepId, sessionId)`
2. Register the session with `WorkflowSessionCallback` for completion tracking
3. Include `workflow_signal` tool in the session's available tools

When workflow fields are absent:
- Behavior unchanged (standalone agent start, current flow)

### Integration Point
This connects the QueueCoordinator → AgentStartBackgroundService → WorkflowExecutionService pipeline:
- QueueCoordinator creates AgentStartRequest with workflow fields
- AgentStartBackgroundService starts the session and registers it with the workflow
- WorkflowSessionCallback handles completion signaling back to the workflow engine

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/AgentStartBackgroundService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/AgentStartRequest.cs`

## Tests
- With workflow fields: OnStepStarted called, session registered with callback
- Without workflow fields: behavior unchanged
- workflow_signal tool included only for workflow sessions

title: Queue status API with per-issue-type workflow config
description:
Add REST API endpoints for queue status and control, with per-issue-type workflow configuration.

## API Endpoints

### Queue Control
- `POST /api/projects/{projectId}/queue/start` - Start queue execution on a root issue
  - Body: `{ issueId, workflowMappings: { "task": "workflow-id-1", "bug": "workflow-id-2" } }`
  - Per-issue-type workflow mapping: which workflow to run for each issue type
- `GET /api/projects/{projectId}/queue/status` - Get current queue coordinator state
  - Returns: status, active queues, running count, pending issues
- `POST /api/projects/{projectId}/queue/cancel` - Cancel all active queues

### Queue Status Model
Expose `QueueCoordinatorState` through the API with additional detail:
- Per-queue breakdown: queue ID, state, current issue, pending count, history
- Overall progress: total issues, completed, failed, remaining

## Controller
Wire through `OrchestrationController` (or create dedicated `QueueController`):
- Inject `IQueueCoordinator`
- Validate project exists before starting
- Return 404 if no active execution for status/cancel

## Workflow Mappings
- Accept `Dictionary<string, string>` mapping issue type → workflow ID
- Validate all referenced workflow IDs exist before starting
- Store on the ProjectExecution so QueueCoordinator can look up per-issue

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Controllers/OrchestrationController.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/IQueueCoordinator.cs` (add StartExecution overload with mappings)

## Tests
- API integration tests: start, status, cancel
- Validation: invalid project, invalid workflow ID
- Status returns correct queue breakdown

title: Create static React documentation site for GitHub Pages
description:
# Create Static React Documentation Site for GitHub Pages

Build a static documentation site using React that is hosted on GitHub Pages and shares visual styling with the Homespun web client.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Project Setup
- Create a `docs-site/` directory at the repo root
- Initialize a Vite + React + TypeScript project
- Configure TailwindCSS v4 with the same theme as the web client:
  - Zinc base color palette
  - Dark theme support
  - Same font stack and spacing
- Install and configure shadcn/ui (New York style) for consistent component styling

### 2. Documentation Framework
- Use a markdown-based content system (e.g., `@mdx-js/react` or similar)
- Read documentation content from `docs/` markdown files
- Create layouts:
  - Sidebar navigation with collapsible sections
  - Main content area with table of contents
  - Mobile-responsive layout
  - Search functionality (client-side, e.g., Fuse.js)

### 3. Pages and Navigation
- Home/landing page with project overview
- Installation guide (from `docs/installation.md`)
- Multi-user setup (from `docs/multi-user.md`)
- Usage guide (from `docs/usage.md`)
- Troubleshooting (from `docs/troubleshooting.md`)
- API reference (if applicable)

### 4. Styling Consistency
- Share or replicate the TailwindCSS configuration from `src/Homespun.Web/`
- Use the same color scheme, typography, and component styles
- Include the Homespun branding/logo
- Ensure dark mode works correctly
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting

### 5. GitHub Pages Deployment
- Add GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/docs.yml`)
- Build the static site on push to main
- Deploy to GitHub Pages
- Configure base URL for GitHub Pages path

### 6. Testing
- Add basic tests for documentation site components
- Verify all markdown files render correctly
- Test responsive layout
- Test navigation and search

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Documentation site builds as a static Vite React app
- [ ] Visual style matches Homespun web client (TailwindCSS v4, shadcn/ui, Zinc theme)
- [ ] All documentation pages render from markdown source files
- [ ] Sidebar navigation works with collapsible sections
- [ ] Site is mobile-responsive
- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main
- [ ] Dark mode is supported and matches web client theme

title: Fix issue with agent runs being blocked
Add toolbar buttons to move issues
description:
## Summary

Add \"Move Up\" and \"Move Down\" buttons to the issue toolbar (`project-toolbar.tsx`) that allow users to reorder issues among their siblings within a parent issue. The buttons should be disabled when the movement is not valid (e.g., already first/last). If siblings lack lexical ordering (have a parent ID but no `sortOrder`), assign lexical ordering to all siblings first, preserving the currently displayed order, then perform the move.

## Prerequisites

The backend already has a `POST /api/issues/{issueId}/move-sibling` endpoint in `IssuesController.cs` (line ~363) that accepts a `MoveSeriesSiblingRequest` with `projectId` and `direction` (Up/Down). The `FleeceService.MoveSeriesSiblingAsync` method (line ~731) swaps sort orders between adjacent siblings. However, it currently errors if siblings have no sort order — this needs to be handled.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Backend: Handle unordered siblings in MoveSeriesSiblingAsync

**File:** `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceService.cs`

Before performing the swap, check if any siblings lack a `sortOrder`. If so:
- Get the current display order of all siblings (the order they're returned from the cache/store)
- Assign lexical sort orders to all siblings using `LexOrderUtils.ComputeMidpoint()`, maintaining the current order
- Persist these sort orders
- Then proceed with the swap as normal

**Tests:** Add test cases in `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/Fleece/FleeceServiceTests.cs`:
- Test moving when siblings have no sort order (should assign orders first, then move)
- Test that existing sort orders are preserved when some siblings have them and others don't

### 2. Frontend: Add Move Up/Down buttons to toolbar

**File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/project-toolbar.tsx`

Add two new buttons to the toolbar button group (near the hierarchy buttons):
- **Move Up** button with `ChevronUp` icon
- **Move Down** button with `ChevronDown` icon

Props to add to the toolbar:
- `onMoveUp: () => void` — callback when Move Up is clicked
- `onMoveDown: () => void` — callback when Move Down is clicked  
- `canMoveUp: boolean` — whether the selected issue can move up (disable button if false)
- `canMoveDown: boolean` — whether the selected issue can move down (disable button if false)

### 3. Frontend: Add keyboard shortcuts

**File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/hooks/use-toolbar-shortcuts.ts`

Add keyboard shortcuts for move up/down:
- `Ctrl+Shift+ArrowUp` or `K` for Move Up
- `Ctrl+Shift+ArrowDown` or `J` for Move Down

### 4. Frontend: Wire up move mutation and state

**File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.issues.index.tsx`

- Create a mutation using the existing `postIssuesMoveSibling` API endpoint from the generated client
- Compute `canMoveUp` and `canMoveDown` from the selected issue's position among its siblings in the task graph data
- Pass the callbacks and disabled states to `ProjectToolbar`
- On successful move, invalidate the task graph query to refresh the display

### 5. Frontend: Determine sibling position for disable logic

To determine if an issue can move up/down:
- Find the selected issue in the task graph data
- Find its parent issue
- Get the parent's children sorted by their current display order
- If the issue is the first child, `canMoveUp = false`
- If the issue is the last child, `canMoveDown = false`
- If the issue has no parent, both should be `false` (root-level issues cannot be reordered this way)

## Key Files

- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceService.cs` — Backend move logic
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Controllers/IssuesController.cs` — API endpoint
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/project-toolbar.tsx` — Toolbar UI
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/hooks/use-toolbar-shortcuts.ts` — Keyboard shortcuts
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.issues.index.tsx` — Route/page wiring
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/api/generated/sdk.gen.ts` — Generated API client
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Utilities/LexOrderUtils.cs` — Lexical ordering utility
- `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/Fleece/FleeceServiceTests.cs` — Backend tests

## Testing

### Backend Tests
- Moving issue up/down when siblings have no sort order assigns lexical ordering first
- Moving preserves relative order of non-moved siblings
- Edge cases: single child, issue without parent

### Frontend Tests  
- Toolbar renders move buttons
- Buttons are disabled when issue is first/last sibling
- Buttons are disabled when no issue is selected or issue has no parent
- Clicking buttons triggers the correct API call

title: Fix incorrect blocking for parallel issues and series issues
description: A task scheduling system was added that blocked issues from starting if there were previous issues in the tree that were not yet complete. This is effectively working for series issues, but it also blocks parallel issues from starting if there is another earlier sibling that is incomplete. If the parent has a parallel execution mode, this shouldn't happen as the parallel execution mode explicitly means that both issues can run at the same time.

title: Create likec4 architecture diagrams for current system
description:
Install likec4 and create architecture diagrams documenting the current Homespun system.

Tasks:
1. Install likec4 as a dev dependency (`npm i -D likec4`) or globally
2. Create `docs/arch/` workspace with likec4 configuration
3. Produce 4 architecture views:

**System Context View:**
- Homespun system boundary
- External actors: Developer, GitHub API, Claude API (Anthropic)
- Interactions: Developer uses Homespun, Homespun integrates with GitHub and Claude

**Container Diagram:**
- ASP.NET Server (Homespun.Server)
- React Frontend (Homespun.Web via Vite)
- TypeScript Agent Worker (Homespun.Worker with Hono + Claude Agent SDK)
- PLG observability stack (Prometheus, Loki, Grafana)
- Docker-outside-of-Docker worker containers
- SQLite/JSONL data stores

**Component Diagram (Server):**
- Feature slices: AgentOrchestration, ClaudeCode, Commands, Fleece, Git, GitHub, Projects, PullRequests

- Data flow between components

**Deployment View (Azure VM):**
- Azure VM with Docker Compose
