- Docker socket mounting (DooD pattern)
- Volume mounts for data persistence

Reference existing code structure in `src/` for accuracy. Verify diagrams render correctly with `npx likec4 serve`.

title: Clean up endpoints
description: Review the OpenAPI specification and look for opportunities to consolidate the API to be legible and follow modern patterns. There are a lot of endpoints with long nondescript names - these could potentially be rewritten as longer routes that are organised into sub-paths. Breaking changes are allowed as the only consumer of these endpoints currently is the React client in this repository but ensure that if any endpoints are changed that they are changed on both the client and the server.

title: Simplify workflow models from DAG to sequential steps
description:
Replace the DAG-based workflow models (Nodes + Edges) with a sequential step list model.

## Changes

### New Model: WorkflowStep
Create `WorkflowStep` with fields:
- `Id` (string), `Name` (string), `StepType` (enum: Agent, ServerAction, Gate)
- `Prompt` (string, nullable - inline prompt text)
- `PromptId` (string, nullable - reference to named AgentPrompt template)
- `SessionMode` (Plan/Build)
- `OnSuccess` (StepTransition - NextStep/Exit/GoToStep with target step ID)
- `OnFailure` (StepTransition - NextStep/Exit/GoToStep/Retry with target step ID)
- `MaxRetries` (int, default 0)
- `RetryDelaySeconds` (int, default 30)
- `Condition` (string, nullable - simple expression for skip logic)
- `Config` (JsonElement - step-type-specific config, e.g. ServerAction type)

### Modify WorkflowDefinition
- Replace `List<WorkflowNode> Nodes` and `List<WorkflowEdge> Edges` with `List<WorkflowStep> Steps`
- Remove `WorkflowSettings.MaxConcurrentNodes` (not applicable to sequential)
- Keep `WorkflowTrigger` but simplify to Manual only for MVP
- Keep version tracking, timestamps

### New Model: StepExecution
Replace `NodeExecution` with `StepExecution`:
- `StepId`, `StepIndex`, `Status` (Pending/Running/WaitingForInput/Completed/Failed/Skipped)
- `RetryCount`, `StartedAt`, `CompletedAt`, `DurationMs`
- `Output` (Dictionary), `SessionId` (for agent steps), `ErrorMessage`

### Modify WorkflowExecution
- Replace `List<NodeExecution> NodeExecutions` with `List<StepExecution> StepExecutions`
- Add `CurrentStepIndex` (int)

### Delete
- `WorkflowEdge.cs` (entire file)
- `NodeConfigs/` directory (AgentNodeConfig, GateNodeConfig, ActionNodeConfig, TransformNodeConfig)
- Simplify `WorkflowNodeType` enum → `WorkflowStepType` (Agent, ServerAction, Gate)

### Keep Unchanged
- `WorkflowContext.cs` and `ContextInterpolation` - well-designed, reusable
- `WorkflowStorageService.cs` pattern (update serialization for new schema)

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/WorkflowDefinition.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/WorkflowStep.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/WorkflowNode.cs` (delete or rename)
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/WorkflowEdge.cs` (delete)
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/NodeConfigs/` (delete directory)
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/WorkflowExecution.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/NodeExecution.cs` (rename to StepExecution)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowStorageService.cs`

## Tests
- Model serialization round-trip tests
- Storage service read/write with new schema
- StepTransition validation (GoToStep targets valid step ID)

title: Implement sequential step execution engine
description:
Rewrite WorkflowExecutionService to execute steps sequentially with transition logic.

## Implementation

### Rewrite ExecuteWorkflowAsync
Replace the topological sort loop with sequential step iteration:
1. Start at step index 0
2. Execute current step based on StepType
3. On completion, evaluate transition (OnSuccess/OnFailure)
4. Advance to next step, jump to target, retry, or exit

### Step Transition Logic
```
OnSuccess transitions: NextStep (default), GoToStep(stepId), Exit(success)
OnFailure transitions: Retry (up to MaxRetries with RetryDelaySeconds delay), GoToStep(stepId), Exit(fail)
```

### Step Executors (IStepExecutor interface)
Create `IStepExecutor` with `Task<StepResult> ExecuteAsync(WorkflowStep step, WorkflowContext context, CancellationToken ct)`:
- `AgentStepExecutor`: Resolves prompt (inline or template), starts Claude session via AgentStartBackgroundService, marks step as Running, waits for external callback
- `ServerActionStepExecutor`: Dispatches to action-specific handlers (CI merge, etc.), runs synchronously on the server
- `GateStepExecutor`: Marks step as WaitingForInput, pauses execution until approval API called

### Execution State
- Track `CurrentStepIndex` on WorkflowExecution
- Update StepExecution status at each transition
- Persist state after each step transition for crash recovery
- Support pause/resume/cancel at step boundaries

### Condition Evaluation
- If step has a `Condition`, evaluate it against WorkflowContext
- If condition is false, skip step and advance to next
- Simple expression evaluation (e.g., "steps.verify.output.approved == true")

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowExecutionService.cs` (rewrite)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IStepExecutor.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/AgentStepExecutor.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/ServerActionStepExecutor.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/GateStepExecutor.cs` (new)

## Tests
- Step sequencing: steps execute in order
- Retry logic: retries up to MaxRetries then follows OnFailure
- GoToStep: jumps to correct step
- Exit: terminates workflow with success/failure
- Condition skip: step skipped when condition is false
- Pause/resume at step boundaries
- Crash recovery: resumes from persisted CurrentStepIndex

title: Implement session-to-workflow signaling
description:
Enable agent sessions to signal step completion/failure back to the workflow engine.

## Implementation

### workflow_signal Tool
Define a tool that agents call to report step outcome:
```json
{
  "name": "workflow_signal",
  "description": "Signal workflow step completion with result data",
  "input_schema": {
    "properties": {
      "status": { "enum": ["success", "fail"], "description": "Step outcome" },
      "data": { "type": "object", "description": "Output data for workflow context" },
      "message": { "type": "string", "description": "Summary of what was done" }
    },
    "required": ["status"]
  }
}
```

### WorkflowSessionCallback
Create callback service that bridges session events to workflow engine:
1. Register callback when AgentStepExecutor starts a session
2. Listen for session end events or workflow_signal tool calls
3. Parse result and invoke `WorkflowExecutionService.OnStepCompleted` or `OnStepFailed`
4. Store session output in `WorkflowContext.StepOutputs[stepId]`

### Session Lifecycle Integration
- Hook into `ClaudeSessionService` / `ClaudeSessionStore` session lifecycle events
- When a session ends without calling workflow_signal, treat as implicit success (session completed normally)
- When a session errors/crashes, treat as step failure
- Include the tool in the session's available tools only when running within a workflow context

### Context Passing
- Step outputs stored in context as `steps.<stepName>.output.*`
- Subsequent steps can reference via `{{steps.verify.output.approved}}`
- Final response text captured as `steps.<stepName>.output.response`

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowSessionCallback.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/ClaudeSessionService.cs` (add workflow hooks)
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/tools/` (add workflow_signal tool definition)

## Tests
- Callback invoked on session completion
- workflow_signal tool call parsed correctly
- Step output stored in WorkflowContext
- Session crash triggers step failure
- Tool only available in workflow sessions

title: Implement CI merge server action step
description:
Create the CiMergeStepExecutor for the "merge when CI passes" workflow step.

## Implementation

### CiMergeStepExecutor
A ServerAction step that polls GitHub for CI check status and merges when ready:

1. **Get PR number** from WorkflowContext (set by the implement step that created the PR)
2. **Poll loop**: Check GitHub check suites/runs status via `IGitHubClientWrapper`
   - Interval: configurable, default 60 seconds
   - Timeout: configurable, default 30 minutes
3. **On all checks pass**: Merge the PR via GitHub API
   - Merge strategy: configurable (merge/squash/rebase), default squash
   - Delete branch after merge: configurable, default true
4. **On check failure**: Return StepResult.Failed with error details
   - The workflow's OnFailure transition can route to a fix step
5. **On timeout**: Return StepResult.Failed with timeout message

### ServerAction Dispatch
Update `ServerActionStepExecutor` to dispatch based on action type in step config:
- `ci_merge`: routes to CiMergeStepExecutor
- Future actions can be added here (notify, create_issue, etc.)

### Step Config Schema
```json
{
  "actionType": "ci_merge",
  "pollIntervalSeconds": 60,
  "timeoutMinutes": 30,
  "mergeStrategy": "squash",
  "deleteBranch": true
}
```

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/CiMergeStepExecutor.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/ServerActionStepExecutor.cs` (update dispatch)

## Dependencies
- Existing `IGitHubClientWrapper` for check status and merge API calls

## Tests
- All checks pass → merge succeeds
- Check failure → step fails
- Timeout → step fails
- Poll interval respected
- Merge strategy applied correctly

title: Default workflow template: verify-implement-review-merge
description:
Create the standard built-in workflow template and template service.

## Default Template Steps

### Step 1: Verify Plan (Agent/Plan mode)
- Prompt: Review the issue plan for completeness, clarity, and feasibility. Verify all necessary details are present for implementation. Call workflow_signal with success if ready, or fail if the plan needs work.
- OnSuccess: NextStep
- OnFailure: Exit(fail) - blocks workflow, plan needs manual revision

### Step 2: Implement (Agent/Build mode)
- Prompt: Implement the plan described in the issue. Follow TDD practices. Run tests. Create a PR when complete. Call workflow_signal with the PR number in data.
- OnSuccess: NextStep
- OnFailure: Exit(fail)

### Step 3: Architectural Review (Agent/Plan mode)
- Prompt: Review the PR diff for architectural issues, security concerns, and code quality. Call workflow_signal with success if approved, or fail with specific feedback.
- OnSuccess: NextStep
- OnFailure: GoToStep(fix) - routes to fix step

### Step 4: Merge When CI Passes (ServerAction/ci_merge)
- Config: pollIntervalSeconds=60, timeoutMinutes=30, mergeStrategy=squash
- OnSuccess: Exit(success)
- OnFailure: GoToStep(fix)

### Step 5: Fix CI/Review Issues (Agent/Build mode)
- Prompt: Fix the issues identified in the previous step. The errors are: {{steps.merge.output.error}}{{steps.review.output.error}}. Push fixes and call workflow_signal when done.
- OnSuccess: GoToStep(merge) - retry the merge
- OnFailure: Exit(fail)
- MaxRetries: 3

## WorkflowTemplateService
- `GetTemplates()`: Returns list of built-in templates
- `GetTemplate(templateId)`: Returns specific template
- `CreateWorkflowFromTemplate(templateId, projectId)`: Creates a WorkflowDefinition from template
- Templates stored in code (not JSONL) - shipped with application
- Each template is a method that returns a `WorkflowDefinition`

## API
- Add `GET /api/workflow-templates` endpoint to list available templates
- Add `POST /api/workflow-templates/{templateId}/create?projectId=` to instantiate

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowTemplateService.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Templates/DefaultWorkflowTemplates.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Controllers/WorkflowsController.cs` (add template endpoints)

## Tests
- Template creates valid WorkflowDefinition with all required fields
- Step transitions reference valid step IDs
- Template prompts contain expected {{variable}} references
- API endpoint returns template list

title: Add workflow SignalR hub for real-time updates
description:
Implement SignalR hub for broadcasting workflow execution events to the frontend.

## Implementation

### IWorkflowHubClient Interface
Create in Homespun.Shared:
```csharp
public interface IWorkflowHubClient
{
    Task StepStarted(string executionId, string stepId, int stepIndex);
    Task StepCompleted(string executionId, string stepId, StepExecutionStatus status, Dictionary<string, object>? output);
    Task StepFailed(string executionId, string stepId, string error);
    Task StepRetrying(string executionId, string stepId, int retryCount, int maxRetries);
    Task WorkflowCompleted(string executionId, WorkflowExecutionStatus status);
    Task WorkflowFailed(string executionId, string error);
    Task GatePending(string executionId, string stepId, string gateName);
}
```

### WorkflowHub
- Implement SignalR hub inheriting from `Hub<IWorkflowHubClient>`
- Client methods: `JoinExecution(executionId)`, `LeaveExecution(executionId)` for group-based subscriptions
- Map at `/hubs/workflow`

### Event Subscription
- Subscribe to WorkflowExecutionService events (add event delegates or use an event bus)
- Broadcast to execution-specific SignalR groups
- Also broadcast project-level summary events to `project-{projectId}` group

### Registration
- Add hub mapping in Program.cs
- Add hub constants to HubConstants.cs
- Register in DI

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/IWorkflowHubClient.cs` (new)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Hubs/WorkflowHub.cs` (update existing placeholder)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Program.cs` (add hub mapping)

## Pattern Reference
Follow `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Hubs/ClaudeCodeHub.cs`

## Tests
- Hub emits StepStarted on step execution begin
- Hub emits StepCompleted/Failed on step finish
- Hub emits WorkflowCompleted when all steps done
- Group-based subscription filters events correctly

title: Workflow list and detail pages with TanStack Router
description:

## Routes

title: Mermaid flowchart renderer for workflow steps
description:
Create a Mermaid-based flowchart component that visualizes workflow steps.

## WorkflowMermaidChart Component
Renders a workflow's steps as a Mermaid flowchart diagram.

### Mermaid Generation
Convert WorkflowDefinition steps into Mermaid flowchart syntax:
- Each step is a node (shape varies by type: Agent=rounded rect, ServerAction=hexagon, Gate=diamond)
- Sequential steps connected with arrows
- OnSuccess transitions shown as green arrows
- OnFailure transitions shown as red arrows
- GoToStep transitions shown as dashed arrows with labels
- Retry loops shown as self-referencing arrows with "retry (n/max)" label

### Example Output
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    verify["🔍 Verify Plan"] --> implement["🔨 Implement"]
    implement --> review["📋 Review"]
    review -->|success| merge{{"⚙️ CI Merge"}}
    review -->|fail| fix["🔧 Fix Issues"]
    merge -->|success| done(("✅ Done"))
    merge -->|fail| fix
    fix -->|success| merge
    fix -->|fail| failed(("❌ Failed"))
    style verify fill:#22c55e
    style implement fill:#3b82f6
```

### Execution Status Highlighting
When viewing an active execution, highlight steps by status:
- Pending: gray fill
- Running: blue fill with animation
- Completed: green fill
- Failed: red fill
- Skipped: gray dashed border
- WaitingForInput: yellow fill

### Click Handling
- Emit click events when a step node is clicked
- Parent component uses this to show the step settings card

## Dependencies
- `mermaid` npm package for rendering

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/workflows/components/workflow-mermaid-chart.tsx`

## Tests
- Snapshot tests for different workflow configurations
- Status highlighting applied correctly
- Click events emitted for step nodes

title: Step settings cards and workflow editor
description:
Create the step configuration UI and workflow editing experience.

## StepSettingsCard Component
Displayed when a step is clicked in the Mermaid chart or step list:

title: Fix QueueCoordinator nested series/non-leaf TODOs
description:
Address incomplete expansion in QueueCoordinator for nested hierarchies.

## Known TODOs

- Line 414: `// TODO: wire up continuation` for nested series within series
- Lines 438-439: Same as 308-309 pattern

## Changes

- For non-leaf children in parallel: expand into parallel sub-queue

title: Wire QueueCoordinator to dispatch workflows per issue type
description:
Modify QueueCoordinator to start workflow executions instead of direct agent starts when a workflow is configured for the issue type.

## Implementation

### Modify CreateRequest / Issue Processing
When QueueCoordinator dequeues an issue:
1. Look up the issue's type in the WorkflowMappings (stored on ProjectExecution)
2. If a workflow is mapped for this type:
   - Start a WorkflowExecution via WorkflowExecutionService
   - Pass the issue context (ID, title, description, branch) to the workflow
   - The workflow manages its own multi-step execution
3. If no workflow mapped:
   - Fall back to current behavior (direct AgentStartRequest)

### Workflow Completion Handling
A workflow execution spans multiple sessions (one per agent step). The TaskQueue's NotifyCompleted must wait for the entire workflow to finish, not just one session:
- Subscribe to WorkflowExecutionService completion events
- When workflow completes successfully: mark queue item as complete, advance queue
- When workflow fails: mark queue item as failed, optionally block the queue

### WorkflowMappings Storage
- Add `Dictionary<string, string>? WorkflowMappings` to ProjectExecution
- Passed in via the queue start API endpoint
- Keys are issue type names, values are workflow IDs

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/QueueCoordinator.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/TaskQueue.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/IQueueCoordinator.cs`

## Tests
- Issue with mapped workflow type → workflow execution started
- Issue without mapped type → direct agent start (fallback)
- Workflow completion → queue advances to next issue
- Workflow failure → queue item marked failed
- End-to-end: queue drains through multiple workflow executions

description:

## CLAUDE.md Updates

- **Session**: Individual agent session with context window, signals state to orchestrator
- **Workflow**: Series of steps on a single issue (Agent/ServerAction/Gate types), with transitions (NextStep/GoToStep/Exit/Retry)
- **Task Queue**: Activates on issue tree, creates parallel/series queues, dispatches workflows per issue type
- Step types and their behavior
- Default template: verify → implement → review → merge → fix (with retries)
- Per-issue-type workflow configuration

## docs/workflows.md
Create architecture documentation with:
- System overview Mermaid diagram showing: Issue Tree → QueueCoordinator → TaskQueue → WorkflowExecution → Sessions
- Workflow step flow diagram

## Inline Code Comments

- Document WorkflowStep transition semantics

- `docs/workflows.md` (new)

title: Fix QueueCoordinator nested series/non-leaf expansion TODOs
description:
Fix incomplete expansion logic in QueueCoordinator for nested issue hierarchies.

## Known TODOs

### Lines 308-309, 438-439: Non-leaf children enqueued as-is
In `ExpandSeriesIntoSingleQueue` and `ExpandSeries`, when a child has its own children, the code comments say "enqueue as-is for now" instead of properly expanding:
```csharp
// This child has children - we need to handle expansion when this is reached
// For now, enqueue it as-is (the coordinator can expand later)
await queue.EnqueueAsync(CreateRequest(execution, child), ct);
```
**Fix**: When a non-leaf child is encountered during series expansion, expand it recursively into sub-queues (or defer via series continuations) instead of enqueuing the parent issue directly.

### Line 414: Nested series continuation TODO
In `ExpandSeries`, nested series within series doesn't wire up continuations:
```csharp
// Nested series - recurse
await ExpandSeries(execution, firstChildChildren, ct);
// TODO: wire up continuation
```
**Fix**: Create a series continuation for remaining children and wire it to complete when the nested series finishes.

## Testing Scenarios
Test these hierarchy patterns:
1. Series → Series → Leaf (nested series)
2. Series → Parallel → Leaf (series with parallel children)
3. Parallel → Series → Leaf (parallel with series children)
4. Series → Mixed (some children are leaves, some have children)
5. Deep nesting: 3+ levels of series/parallel combinations

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/AgentOrchestration/Services/QueueCoordinator.cs`

## Tests
- Integration tests for each hierarchy pattern above
- Verify correct queue count and issue ordering
- Verify continuations fire correctly for nested series
- Verify no issues are lost or duplicated

title: Add button to detach issue from tree
Add PromptCategory to AgentPrompt model and update services
description:
## Task

Add a `PromptCategory` enum to the `AgentPrompt` model to distinguish between standard agent prompts and issue agent prompts, then update the prompt service to support filtering by category.

## Changes Required

### 1. Add PromptCategory enum
Create `PromptCategory.cs` in `Homespun.Shared/Models/Sessions/`:
```csharp
public enum PromptCategory
{
    Standard = 0,
    IssueAgent = 1
}
```

### 2. Update AgentPrompt model
In `Homespun.Shared/Models/Sessions/AgentPrompt.cs`:
- Add `PromptCategory Category { get; set; } = PromptCategory.Standard;` property

### 3. Update default-prompts.json
In `Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Resources/default-prompts.json`:
- Add `"category": "IssueAgent"` to the IssueModify prompt definition
- Keep IssueAgentSystem as a SessionType prompt (no category change needed)

### 4. Update DefaultPromptDefinition
In the default prompt definition POCO, add a `Category` field so it can be deserialized from JSON.

### 5. Update AgentPromptService
In `AgentPromptService.cs`:
- Update `EnsureDefaultPromptsAsync()` to set the `Category` from the default prompt definition
- Update `GetAllPrompts()` to filter by `PromptCategory.Standard` (existing behavior preserved)
- Update `GetProjectPrompts()` and `GetPromptsForProject()` to filter by `PromptCategory.Standard`
- Add `GetIssueAgentUserPrompts()` method that returns prompts with `Category = IssueAgent` (excluding SessionType prompts)
- Add `GetIssueAgentPromptsForProject(projectId)` that returns merged issue agent prompts (project + inherited global) with override detection, mirroring `GetPromptsForProject()` logic
- Keep existing `GetIssueAgentPrompts()` for backward compatibility (returns SessionType prompts)
- Convert the existing `IssueModify` prompt from `SessionType.IssueAgentModification` to `PromptCategory.IssueAgent` with `SessionType = null` and `Mode = Build`. This makes it a selectable prompt rather than a fixed system prompt.
- Keep `IssueAgentSystem` as `SessionType.IssueAgentSystem` (remains the fixed system prompt)

### 6. Update IAgentPromptService interface
Add the new method signatures to the interface.

### 7. Update API endpoints
In `AgentPromptsController.cs`:
- Add endpoint `GET /api/agent-prompts/issue-agent/available/{projectId}` that calls `GetIssueAgentPromptsForProject(projectId)` — mirrors the existing `GetAvailableForProject` endpoint pattern
- Ensure create/update endpoints handle the `Category` field

### 8. Update AgentPromptDto
In `Homespun.Shared` DTOs, add `Category` to the DTO so the frontend can distinguish prompt types.

## Tests

- Unit test: `GetIssueAgentUserPrompts()` returns only IssueAgent category prompts
- Unit test: `GetIssueAgentPromptsForProject()` returns merged prompts with override detection
- Unit test: `GetAllPrompts()` excludes IssueAgent category prompts
- Unit test: Default prompt initialization sets correct categories
- API test: New endpoint returns issue agent prompts for a project

## Notes
- The IssueModify default prompt should transition from being a fixed SessionType to a selectable PromptCategory.IssueAgent prompt. Handle migration: if the old SessionType version exists, update it.
- This is the foundational change — subsequent tasks depend on this.

title: Update issue agent session to accept prompt selection
description:
## Task

Update the issue agent session creation flow to accept an optional prompt ID, resolving the prompt and session mode from the selected prompt.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: 7jNyFK (PromptCategory model and service changes)

## Changes Required

### 1. Update CreateSessionRequest DTO
In `Homespun.Shared` (or wherever the issues agent request DTO lives):
- Add optional `string? PromptId` field to the create session request

### 2. Update IssuesAgentController.CreateSession()
In `Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/IssuesAgentController.cs` (or equivalent):
- Accept optional `PromptId` from the request
- If `PromptId` is provided:
  - Fetch the prompt via `agentPromptService.GetPrompt(promptId)`
  - Validate it exists and has `Category = IssueAgent`
  - Use the prompt's `Mode` for session mode (Plan or Build)
  - Render the prompt's `InitialMessage` template with the issue context
- If `PromptId` is NOT provided (backward compatibility):
  - Fall back to the existing behavior: fetch the first available IssueAgent category prompt
  - Default to Build mode
- Always use `IssueAgentSystem` SessionType prompt as the system prompt

### 3. Update session mode resolution
- Currently hardcoded to Build mode
- Change to: use the selected prompt's `Mode` property
- If no prompt selected, default to Build mode

## Tests

- Unit test: Session created with Plan-mode prompt uses Plan session mode
- Unit test: Session created with Build-mode prompt uses Build session mode
- Unit test: Session created without prompt ID falls back to default behavior
- Unit test: Invalid prompt ID returns appropriate error
- API test: POST to create session with prompt ID works correctly

## Notes
- The system prompt (IssueAgentSystem) remains unchanged — only the user-facing initial message prompt is selectable
- Ensure template rendering still works with the new prompt selection flow

title: Fix race conditions for agent status
description:
When I run an agent with a command to just build a feature, it successfully will run with the "working" status until the agent is complete, at which point it will change over to a "waiting" status. However, when I run it in plan mode, it will run in "working" until the plan is generated, then switch to "awaiting plan execution". Once a command has been issued to execute the plan, it often either switches to "waiting" mode or remains on "awaiting plan execution" mode rather than going to "working".

Review how the status is changed when a plan is executed to try and determine what is causing this failure.

title: Fix issue move buttons
description: On more complex issue hierarchies, the move up and move down buttons don't successfully move the issues up or down. This particularly happens in deeply nested issues. Add integration tests for this behaviour in complex situations like when issues have multiple parents. Add logging to show which details of the issue are being changed so I can further diagnose this issue.

title: Create likec4 architecture diagrams for target ACA deployment
description:

title: ACA infrastructure and authentication foundations
description:
Three independent tracks that can be worked on in parallel:

1. **Bicep modules for ACA environment** - Infrastructure-as-code for Azure Container Apps

3. **Azure Static Web Apps configuration** - SWA config, routing, API proxy, Bicep module

All three tracks are independent and can be developed concurrently. The existing local Docker Compose deployment must not be affected - all new functionality is conditional on configuration flags.

Verification: All three child tracks complete. Server starts with and without Authentication__Enabled. Bicep templates validate. SWA config is syntactically correct.

title: Add Bicep modules for Azure Container Apps environment

title: Add OIDC authentication middleware with Entra ID
description:
Parent issue for OIDC authentication implementation across server and frontend. Three sub-tasks in series:

1. JWT bearer auth on ASP.NET server (must come first - defines the auth middleware)
2. SignalR hub authentication (depends on server auth being in place)
3. MSAL login in React frontend (depends on server accepting tokens)

All authentication is conditional - disabled by default for local development. The `Authentication__Enabled` config flag controls server-side auth, and `VITE_AUTH_ENABLED` controls frontend auth.

Verification: Server starts and responds to API calls with auth both enabled and disabled. SignalR connections work with and without tokens. Frontend login flow works when enabled, app loads normally when disabled.

title: Configure Azure Static Web Apps for React frontend
description:
Set up Azure Static Web Apps (SWA) configuration for the React frontend deployment.

Tasks:

1. **Create `staticwebapp.config.json`** in `src/Homespun.Web/`:
   - SPA fallback routing (all routes → index.html)
   - API proxy rules: `/api/*` → ACA server backend URL (configured via environment variable)

   - Response headers (security headers: X-Frame-Options, CSP, etc.)

2. **Create Bicep module** `infra/aca/modules/static-web-app.bicep`:
   - Azure Static Web Apps resource
   - Custom domain configuration (optional parameter)
   - Link to GitHub repo for CI/CD deployment
   - Output the default hostname

3. **Create GitHub Actions workflow** `.github/workflows/deploy-swa.yml`:
   - Trigger on push to main (src/Homespun.Web/** paths)
   - Build React app with production environment variables
   - Deploy to Azure Static Web Apps using official action
   - Set `VITE_API_URL` and `VITE_AUTH_ENABLED` environment variables

4. **Update Vite config** if needed to support SWA deployment:
   - Ensure `base` path is correct for SWA
   - Verify build output is compatible with SWA expectations

Do NOT modify the existing development server setup or docker-compose configuration.

title: Add JWT bearer authentication to ASP.NET server
description:
Add conditional JWT bearer authentication to the ASP.NET server using Microsoft.Identity.Web.

Implementation:

1. **Add NuGet package:** `Microsoft.Identity.Web` to `Homespun.Server.csproj`

2. **Configuration in `appsettings.json`:**
   ```json
   "Authentication": {
     "Enabled": false
   },
   "AzureAd": {
     "Instance": "https://login.microsoftonline.com/",
     "TenantId": "",
     "ClientId": "",
     "Audience": ""
   }
   ```

3. **Conditional auth setup in `Program.cs`:**
   - Read `Authentication__Enabled` (or `Authentication:Enabled`) config
   - When enabled: `builder.Services.AddMicrosoftIdentityWebApiAuthentication(builder.Configuration)`
   - When disabled: add a no-op/pass-through authentication scheme that always succeeds
   - Add `app.UseAuthentication()` and `app.UseAuthorization()` to pipeline

4. **Add `[Authorize]` attribute** to all API controllers/endpoints
   - When auth is disabled, the no-op scheme ensures all requests pass through
   - When auth is enabled, valid JWT tokens are required

5. **Add tests:**
   - Unit test: verify auth middleware is registered when enabled
   - Unit test: verify no-op scheme when disabled
   - API integration test: verify endpoints return 401 when auth enabled and no token provided
   - API integration test: verify endpoints work when auth disabled

Key constraint: ALL existing tests must continue to pass unchanged (they run with auth disabled).

Files to modify:
- `src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj` (new package)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Program.cs` (auth middleware)
- `src/Homespun.Server/appsettings.json` (config)
- New test files in `tests/`

title: Add SignalR hub authentication for OIDC tokens
Add OIDC login flow to React frontend
description:
Add conditional OIDC login using MSAL (Microsoft Authentication Library) to the React frontend.

Implementation:

1. **Install packages:** `@azure/msal-browser` and `@azure/msal-react`

2. **MSAL configuration** in `src/Homespun.Web/src/lib/auth.ts`:
   - Read `VITE_AUTH_ENABLED`, `VITE_AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID`, `VITE_AZURE_AD_AUTHORITY`
   - Configure MSAL PublicClientApplication
   - Export auth helpers: `getAccessToken()`, `isAuthenticated()`, `login()`, `logout()`

3. **Conditional MsalProvider** wrapper:
   - When `VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=true`: wrap app in `<MsalProvider>`, show login page if not authenticated
   - When `VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=false` (default): render app directly, no auth UI

4. **Token injection in API client** (`src/Homespun.Web/src/api/client.ts`):
   - The file already has a `setAuthToken` stub
   - When auth enabled: call `getAccessToken()` before each request, set Bearer token
   - Integrate with the existing fetch client middleware

5. **SignalR token passing:**
   - Pass access token as query parameter when establishing SignalR connections
   - Update SignalR connection setup in hooks to include `accessTokenFactory`

6. **Login/Logout UI:**
   - Add user avatar/name display in the app header when authenticated
   - Add logout button
   - Login page with "Sign in with Microsoft" button

7. **Add `.env.example`** with all VITE_AUTH_* variables documented

8. **Add tests:**
   - Component test: app renders without auth when VITE_AUTH_ENABLED is false
   - Component test: login page shown when auth enabled and not authenticated
   - Mock MSAL for testing

Files to modify:
- `src/Homespun.Web/package.json` (new packages)
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/api/client.ts` (token injection)
- New files for auth config, login page, MSAL provider wrapper
- SignalR hook files (token passing)

title: Implement ACA agent execution service
description:
Create a new AcaAgentExecutionService that implements IAgentExecutionService to run agent workers as Azure Container Apps revisions instead of Docker containers.

Three sub-tasks in series:
1. AcaAgentExecutionService implementation (core container lifecycle)
2. Execution service factory (select Docker vs ACA based on config)
3. ACA container discovery and recovery (handle restarts, query active workers)

The existing DockerAgentExecutionService must remain unchanged. Mode selection is based on `AgentExecution__Mode` configuration (`Docker` or `ACA`).

Verification: All sub-tasks complete. Unit tests pass for both execution modes. DockerAgentExecutionService unchanged. Factory correctly selects implementation based on config.

title: Adapt storage services for Azure Files on ACA
description:
Verify and fix all storage-dependent services to work correctly on Azure Files (mounted as volumes in ACA).

Services to verify/fix:

1. **IssueWorkspaceService** - Verify JSONL file operations work on Azure Files
   - File locking semantics may differ
   - Ensure atomic write patterns are compatible

2. **GitCloneService** - Fix hardlink issue
   - Git hardlinks do NOT work across Azure Files mounts
   - Replace `git clone` with `git clone --reference` or `git clone --shared` where hardlinks are used
   - Test clone operations on a non-hardlink-supporting filesystem

3. **JsonDataStore** - Verify JSONL read/write on Azure Files
   - Check file append operations
   - Verify concurrent access patterns

4. **MessageCacheStore** - Verify message caching works
   - Check file I/O patterns are Azure Files compatible

5. **General file system checks:**
   - Verify all path construction uses `Path.Combine` (no hardcoded separators)
   - Check for any assumptions about filesystem performance (Azure Files has higher latency)
   - Verify file watching (FileSystemWatcher) behavior on Azure Files if used

Implementation approach:
- Add a configuration flag `Storage__Type` (`Local` | `AzureFiles`) if behavior needs to differ
- Prefer making code work on both without branching where possible
- Add integration tests that verify file operations work without hardlink support

Files to investigate:
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/` (IssueWorkspaceService)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Git/` (GitCloneService)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/` (JsonDataStore, MessageCacheStore)
- Any service using `System.IO` file operations

title: Create AcaAgentExecutionService with container lifecycle management
description:
Create a new AcaAgentExecutionService class implementing IAgentExecutionService that manages agent worker containers as Azure Container Apps revisions.

Implementation:

1. **Add NuGet package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.AppContainers` to `Homespun.Server.csproj`

2. **Create `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AcaAgentExecutionService.cs`:**
   - Implement `IAgentExecutionService` interface
   - Use `ArmClient` from Azure.ResourceManager to manage Container Apps
   - Methods to implement (mirror DockerAgentExecutionService):
     - `StartAgentAsync` - Create a new Container App revision for the worker
       - Use the same worker image from ACR
       - Set environment variables (session config, API keys, server URL)
       - Mount Azure Files volumes
       - Configure ingress for HTTP/SSE communication with server
     - `StopAgentAsync` - Deactivate the revision
     - `GetAgentStatusAsync` - Query revision status
     - `GetAgentLogsAsync` - Query Log Analytics for container logs

3. **Configuration model** `AcaExecutionOptions`:
   - `SubscriptionId`, `ResourceGroupName`, `ContainerAppsEnvironmentName`
   - `ContainerRegistryName`, `WorkerImageName`, `WorkerImageTag`
   - `AzureFilesShareName`
   - Bind from `AgentExecution:Aca` config section

4. **Use Managed Identity** for Azure resource access (DefaultAzureCredential)

5. **Add unit tests:**
   - Mock ArmClient interactions
   - Verify revision creation parameters
   - Verify environment variable passing
   - Verify volume mount configuration

Reference `DockerAgentExecutionService.cs` for the interface contract and expected behavior.

title: Add execution service factory for Docker/ACA mode selection
Create ACA container discovery and recovery services
description:
Add services to discover active ACA worker revisions on server startup and handle recovery scenarios.

Implementation:

1. **Create `AcaContainerDiscoveryService`:**
   - On server startup, query Azure Container Apps for active worker revisions
   - Match active revisions to known agent sessions
   - Resume monitoring for active sessions
   - Clean up orphaned revisions (workers without matching sessions)

2. **Create hosted service `AcaWorkerRecoveryService : BackgroundService`:**
   - Periodic health check of active worker revisions
   - Detect and handle revision failures (restart or report)
   - Clean up completed/failed revisions after session ends

3. **Register conditionally** - only when `AgentExecution:Mode` is `ACA`

4. **Add tests:**
   - Discovery correctly matches revisions to sessions
   - Orphaned revisions are cleaned up
   - Recovery detects failed revisions
   - Services not registered in Docker mode

Reference the existing Docker container management patterns for consistency.

Files to create:
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AcaContainerDiscoveryService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AcaWorkerRecoveryService.cs`
- Test files

title: Create ACA deployment script and CI/CD workflow
description:
Create deployment automation for Azure Container Apps.

1. **Create `scripts/deploy-aca.sh`:**
   - Accept parameters: resource group, environment name, ACR name
   - Build and push server image to ACR
   - Build and push worker image to ACR
   - Deploy Bicep templates from `infra/aca/`
   - Configure Container App with environment variables
   - Output deployed URLs

2. **Create `.github/workflows/deploy-aca.yml`:**
   - Trigger: manual (workflow_dispatch) with environment input
   - Steps:
     - Checkout code
     - Azure login (federated credentials)
     - Build and push Docker images to ACR
     - Deploy Bicep templates
     - Deploy SWA (or reference SWA workflow)
     - Run smoke tests against deployed endpoints
   - Environment secrets: Azure subscription, resource group, Entra ID config

3. **Add deployment documentation** in a comment block at the top of the script explaining prerequisites:
   - Azure CLI installed and logged in
   - Resource group created
   - Entra ID app registration configured

Do NOT modify existing deployment scripts or workflows for the VM deployment.

title: Update default issue agent prompts
description:
Update the default issue agent prompts as follows:

"Verify" prompt (build mode):

Verify that all of the work described in this task and in the child tasks are complete. If they are, mark this issue as complete. If not, add additional child issues to this one to complete the remaining work.

"Expand feature" prompt (plan mode):

Review the issue description and the descriptions of it's sub-issues (if it has any). Put together a plan to expand this issue into a full implementation plan in sub-issues (as required). Ask any clarifying questions you may have before finalising the plan. Alter this issue's description to make it a specification of the completed feature.

title: Associate issue agents with issue IDs when they are run with an issue selected
description:
When an issue agent is run with an issue selected, it does not associate the issue agent with the issue in any other way than by adding issue information to the prompt. This can be contrasted with running an normal agent on an issue, where there is a strong association between the issue and the agent: a ring is displayed in the task graph, the issue title is displayed in the session window and two agents cannot be started on the same issue. Add a similar association to issue agents started on a task - the worker container ID should be assigned a name that includes the issue ID and the branch name should include the issue ID as well.

Note that there is currently an API issue when I try to launch an issue agent. Nothing happens, and the client logs an "API error". The server clones the agent successfully but the agent doesn't start. These are the logs:

title: Update Fleece version to 1.7.0
description:
Update the version of Fleece that the Homespun application uses to version 1.7.0 from 1.6.2.

Release notes:
1.6.3: https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/releases/tag/v1.6.3
1.7.0: https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/releases/tag/v1.7.0

The most significant change is the change to the graph searching and layout algorithm. It is now configurable to allow for a more well-defined ordering. Adopt the default ordering and layout algorithm from the new version of the Fleece.Core API.

title: E2E and integration tests for workflow and task queue systems
description:
Add E2E and integration tests that test the workflow system and the workflow + task queue system together.

## Requirements

- Create a mock session service that returns hard-coded results when the workflow orchestrators start sessions
- Use this mock to test the full workflow execution pipeline without requiring a live Claude agent
- Test the workflow engine executing steps in sequence with success/failure transitions
- Test the task queue dispatching workflows for issues dequeued from the queue
- Test the integration between QueueCoordinator and WorkflowOrchestrator

## Test Scenarios

### Workflow Engine Tests (API Integration)
- Workflow executes agent steps using mock session service and completes successfully
- Workflow handles step failure and follows OnFailure transition (retry, goto, exit)
- Workflow pauses at gate steps and resumes on approval
- Workflow exits early when a step transition is Exit

### Task Queue + Workflow Integration Tests (API Integration)
- Task queue dequeues an issue and dispatches the configured workflow
- Task queue respects series/parallel execution mode when processing issue trees
- Task queue handles workflow completion and advances to next issue
- Task queue handles workflow failure and reports error state

### E2E Tests (Playwright)
- User can create a workflow, start execution, and observe step progression in the UI
- User can approve a gate step and see the workflow continue
- User can view workflow execution history and step outcomes

## Implementation Notes
- Use HomespunWebApplicationFactory for API integration tests
- Register mock ISessionService in the test DI container
- For E2E tests, configure the mock backend to use the mock session service
- Follow existing test patterns in tests/Homespun.Api.Tests and src/Homespun.Web/e2e

title: Live tests for worker tool system
description:
Add live tests to the Homespun.Worker project to test the tool system that communicates between sessions and the workflow orchestrator.

## Requirements

- Test the workflow_signal tool that agents use to report step outcomes (success/fail + data) back to the orchestrator
- Test tool registration and invocation within the worker's Claude Agent SDK integration
- Test the communication protocol between the worker (session) and the server (orchestrator)

## Test Scenarios

### Tool Registration Tests
- workflow_signal tool is correctly registered with the Claude Agent SDK session
- Tool schema matches expected parameters (outcome, data, step ID)

### Tool Invocation Tests
- workflow_signal tool sends success signal back to orchestrator via HTTP/SignalR
- workflow_signal tool sends failure signal with error data back to orchestrator
- Tool handles network errors gracefully (retry, error reporting)
- Tool validates required parameters before sending signal

### Integration Tests (Worker ↔ Server)
- Worker starts a session, receives tool call from Claude, and routes to workflow_signal handler
- Orchestrator receives signal and advances workflow to next step
- Multiple concurrent sessions can signal independently without interference

## Implementation Notes
- Tests should run against a live (local) server instance or use the existing test infrastructure in Homespun.Worker
- Follow existing test patterns in the worker project (src/Homespun.Worker)
- Use actual HTTP calls to the server's signal endpoint to verify end-to-end communication
- Consider using a test helper that starts a minimal server with the workflow signal endpoint

title: j and k keys move issues up and down without CTRL+SHIFT
description: The 'j' and 'k' keys move the issues up and down without CTRL+SHIFT being pressed. This causes issues to be changed in order accidentally. Review possible reasons for this and fix it.

title: Update issue agent functionality
Fix prompts for issue agents
description:
In the issue agent launcher, default to no prompt in the prompt selector or instructions input. When a prompt is selected in the selector, replace all placeholders in the prompt based on the selected issue and copy this hydrated prompt into the instructions input. When the run button is clicked the instructions input should be injected verbatim into the session (unless there is no prompt there, in which case the session should be started with no prompt and the user can continue to add their prompt in). If another prompt is selected, replace everything in the prompt window.

Currently the selector allows selection of a prompt, but if nothing is in the instructions input nothing is injected into the prompt. If there is input into the instructions window the prompt placeholders are replaced with nothing.

title: Modifying a global prompt in a project causes it to disappear
description: Modifying a prompt on the projects page should cause it to be set as a prompt that overrides the global prompt. There should then exist buttons to revert the prompt back to a local prompt. Add E2E tests that should show this behaviour. It does not happen when I do this in the application: the prompt disappears from the list entirely. Add logging to show the change as well.

title: Update to v2.0.0 of Fleece.Core
description: This change to the Fleece.Core library has a major change to the issue parents: it is no longer supported for an issue to have more than one parent, so all issues must form a tree (cyclic trees are prevented). Update the library to v2.0.0 and review any locations where this may cause a breaking change or a redundant algorithm (such as in graph rendering).

title: Remove the global prompt system
description:
I want to remove the global prompt management system and just have project prompts. This would entail removing the global prompts link on the left hand side, and just having the project prompts tab that allows the prompts to be managed per project. The prompts can be copied from project to project using the code view and copy pasting text if need be.

Thjs would also involve removing the global and project badges from the UI and any overriding behaviour that was set up. Also add a button to reset the prompts to default in the prompts page.

The prompts should also be read from and serialised to the .fleece folder in the main branch of the repository as `homespun-prompts.json`.

All locations where prompts are launched (the agent and issue agent launch modals, and the session window) should be updated to only use the prompts from the project.

title: Add create workflow dialog with template selection
description:
Add the ability to create new workflows from the workflow list page, including selecting from available templates.

**Hooks to add** in features/workflows/hooks/use-workflows.ts:
- useCreateWorkflow(projectId) — wraps postApiWorkflows with TanStack useMutation, invalidates workflowsQueryKey on success. Follow exact pattern of useDeleteWorkflow and useUpdateWorkflow.
- useWorkflowTemplates() — wraps getApiWorkflowTemplates with useQuery, returns { templates, isLoading, isError }. Query key: ['workflow-templates'].
- useCreateFromTemplate(projectId) — wraps postApiWorkflowTemplatesByTemplateIdCreate with useMutation, passing { path: { templateId }, query: { projectId } }. Invalidates workflowsQueryKey on success.

**Component to add**: features/workflows/components/create-workflow-dialog.tsx
- A shadcn Dialog with two modes: 'Blank' and 'From Template'
- 'Blank' mode: form with title (required Input), description (optional Textarea), calls useCreateWorkflow
- 'From Template' mode: fetches templates via useWorkflowTemplates(), shows list of WorkflowTemplateSummary cards (title, description, stepCount), clicking one calls useCreateFromTemplate
- On successful creation, navigate to the new workflow detail page using TanStack Router useNavigate to /projects/$projectId/workflows/$workflowId
- Show loading state during mutation

**Modifications to existing files**:
- workflow-list.tsx: Add a 'Create Workflow' Button (Plus icon) above the table. In the empty state, add a button that opens the dialog. Import and render CreateWorkflowDialog controlled by useState.
- routes/projects.$projectId.workflows.index.tsx: Add page header with 'Workflows' title and the create button
- features/workflows/index.ts: Export new hooks and component

**Tests (TDD)**: Write tests first for all 3 hooks (success/error) and the dialog component (blank mode form, template mode, navigation on success, dialog open/close). Update workflow-list tests for create button.

title: Add workflow settings and metadata editing
description:
The WorkflowDetail page shows workflow title/description as read-only and the editor save only sends { projectId, steps }. Add editable metadata and settings.

**Component to add**: features/workflows/components/workflow-settings-card.tsx
- A shadcn Card with editable fields:
  - Title: Input field
  - Description: Textarea field
  - Default Timeout: Input type='number' for settings.defaultTimeoutSeconds (default 3600)
  - Continue on Failure: Switch (install via npx shadcn@latest add switch if needed) for settings.continueOnFailure (default false)
  - Enabled: Switch for the enabled field
- Props: { workflow: WorkflowDefinition, projectId: string, onSave: (updates: Partial<UpdateWorkflowRequest>) => void, isSaving: boolean }
- Has its own 'Save Settings' button that calls onSave with changed fields

**Modifications to existing files**:
- workflow-detail.tsx: Import and render WorkflowSettingsCard above the Tabs component. Wire onSave to useUpdateWorkflow mutation.
- features/workflows/index.ts: Export new component

**Tests (TDD)**: Write tests first. workflow-settings-card.test.tsx: renders with workflow data, editing title/description updates inputs, toggling switches, changing timeout, save button calls onSave with correct payload, shows loading when isSaving. Update workflow-detail tests to verify settings card renders.

title: Add trigger type configuration UI
description:
Add trigger editing to the workflow detail page. The WorkflowTrigger model supports Manual/Event/Scheduled/Webhook types with rich configuration but no UI exists to configure them.

**Component to add**: features/workflows/components/workflow-trigger-card.tsx
- A shadcn Card with:
  - Trigger Type selector: button group (Manual/Event/Scheduled/Webhook) matching the pattern in StepSettingsCard
  - Event config (shown when type=event): multi-select checkboxes for eventTypes from WorkflowEventType enum (issueCreated, issueStatusChanged, issueAssigned, pullRequestOpened, pullRequestMerged, pullRequestReviewRequested, pullRequestChecksCompleted, agentSessionCompleted, agentSessionFailed, branchCreated)
  - Schedule config (shown when type=scheduled): Input for cronExpression with placeholder '0 */6 * * *', Input for timezone with placeholder 'UTC', Switch for skipIfRunning
  - Webhook config (shown when type=webhook): Input for secret (password type), Input for contentType (default 'application/json')
  - Trigger enabled toggle: Switch for trigger.enabled
- Props: { trigger: WorkflowTrigger | undefined, onChange: (trigger: WorkflowTrigger) => void }
- Default trigger if none provided: { type: 'manual', enabled: true }

**Modifications to existing files**:
- workflow-detail.tsx: Add a 'Trigger' tab in the Tabs component alongside Editor and Executions. Render WorkflowTriggerCard in that tab. Wire onChange to call useUpdateWorkflow with the trigger field.
- features/workflows/index.ts: Export new component

**Tests (TDD)**: Write tests first. workflow-trigger-card.test.tsx: renders with manual trigger default, switching to event shows event config, switching to scheduled shows cron input, switching to webhook shows secret input, toggling event types, editing cron expression, trigger enabled toggle, onChange called with correct payload for each type.

title: Add enable/disable toggle to workflow list
description:
The workflow list shows a 'Disabled' badge when workflow.enabled === false, but there is no way to toggle this from the list view. Add an enable/disable action to the dropdown menu.

**Hook to add** in features/workflows/hooks/use-workflows.ts:
- useToggleWorkflowEnabled(projectId) — calls putApiWorkflowsByWorkflowId with { enabled: !current }, invalidates workflowsQueryKey and workflowQueryKey. Uses useMutation. Follow same pattern as useDeleteWorkflow.

**Modifications to existing files**:
- workflow-list.tsx: Add a dropdown menu item between 'Run' and 'Edit' showing 'Disable' (for enabled workflows) or 'Enable' (for disabled ones) with a Power icon from lucide-react. On click, call useToggleWorkflowEnabled with { workflowId, enabled: !workflow.enabled }.
- features/workflows/hooks/use-workflows.ts: Add useToggleWorkflowEnabled hook
- features/workflows/index.ts: Export new hook

**Tests (TDD)**: Write tests first. use-workflows.test.ts: test useToggleWorkflowEnabled calls update API with correct enabled value and invalidates queries. workflow-list.test.tsx: test dropdown shows 'Disable' for enabled workflow, 'Enable' for disabled, clicking toggle calls mutation.

title: Show all issue properties in drop-down view
description:
When I double click on an issue in the task graph, a drop down view shows the issue information such as the title, ID, description etc.

Make the descriptions vertically scrollable, as it is currently cut off from view.

Add all of the other information for the issue to the view, such as the execution mode, parent issue IDs and sort order of each (e.g. `abc123:ab`).

title: Ensure mini prompts are using Haiku
description: The generation of branch IDs using the worker mini prompts has slowed down substantially. Add logging to show what model is used by the mini prompt when it is run and what the generated prompt is. It should be using Haiku by default.

title: Add button to prompts page to restore default prompts
description: Add a button to the global and project prompt pages to restore the default prompts. On the global page this should restore the prompt to the defaults that are saved in the codebase. On the projects page this should delete all prompts, as the defaults are just the un-overridden global prompts.

title: Update from Fleece.Core v1.7.0 to v1.7.1
description:
v1.7.1: https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/releases/tag/v1.7.1
The updated Fleece.Core API v1.7.1 has just exposed new methods for moving Fleece issues up or down the list of siblings, and for adding issues above or below an existing issue. Update the version to v1.7.1 and ensure that the methods to create issues, move them up or down the list of issues for a parent and for changing their parents use the methods directly from the Fleece library rather than having their own implementation here. Let me know if there are any missing methods you believe should be implemented in the Fleece.Core library. You can find the Fleece API documentation here (https://nick-boey.github.io/Fleece/api/)

This will also include an update that shows draft issues.

title: Example issue
Example issue 2
Fix plan execution using old plans
description:
Occasionally after a worker sends a plan back and awaits for approval, if the plan is rejected and changes are requested to the plan a new plan is generated successfully but the previous plan is displayed again. When execute is clicked, the old plan (that is displayed in the chat window) is the plan that is executed.

The plan is displayed correctly in the plans tab of the session information panel, so I believe that the plan file is being written correctly. Review thjs and ensure that the correct plan is being sent to the client and used when executed.

title: Remove MockAgentPromptService and MockSecretsService
description:
Replace MockAgentPromptService and MockSecretsService with their real implementations in MockServiceExtensions.cs. These have no prerequisites - just swap the DI registrations and delete the mock files.

## MockAgentPromptService
- The mock is a near-identical copy of real `AgentPromptService` (Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs)
- Only depends on `IDataStore` which is already real in mock mode
- Mock actually has simplified/regressed template rendering compared to real
- Remove the mock registration and let the real service be used
- Delete the mock file from Features/Testing/Services/

## MockSecretsService
- Real `SecretsService` (Features/Secrets/SecretsService.cs) uses `secrets.env` files in project root - pure file I/O
- Temp folder already has proper directory structure for this
- Remove mock registration, register real SecretsService
- Delete mock file

## Steps
1. Read `MockServiceExtensions.cs` to find the exact registration lines
2. Remove the two mock registrations
3. Ensure real services are registered (check if they're already in the non-mock DI setup)
4. Delete mock source files
5. Run `dotnet build` to verify compilation
6. Run `dotnet test` for backend tests
7. Run `cd src/Homespun.Web && npm run test:e2e` for E2E tests
8. Fix any test failures caused by the change

## Key Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/MockServiceExtensions.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockAgentPromptService.cs` (delete)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockSecretsService.cs` (delete)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs` (real)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Secrets/SecretsService.cs` (real)

title: Remove MockFleeceIssueTransitionService, MockIssuePrLinkingService, MockFleeceService
description:
Replace MockFleeceIssueTransitionService, MockIssuePrLinkingService, and MockFleeceService with real implementations in MockServiceExtensions.cs.

## MockFleeceIssueTransitionService
- Real `FleeceIssueTransitionService` (Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceIssueTransitionService.cs) depends on `IFleeceService` (real), `IDataStore` (real), and `IHubContext<NotificationHub>` (available)
- Real service adds status validation and SignalR broadcasts (improvements over mock)
- Remove mock registration, register real service

## MockIssuePrLinkingService
- Real `IssuePrLinkingService` (Features/GitHub/IssuePrLinkingService.cs) - same dependency pattern
- Remove mock registration, register real service

## MockFleeceService
- Only consumed by the two services above and possibly other services that are already real
- Once the above mocks are removed, check if MockFleeceService has any remaining consumers
- If not, remove it as well and use the real FleeceService
- Note: FleeceService runs CLI commands via ICommandRunner - this is fine since MockCommandRunner (still present at this stage) handles fleece commands

## Steps
1. Read `MockServiceExtensions.cs` for exact registrations
2. Read the real service implementations to verify dependencies are available
3. Remove mock registrations, add real service registrations
4. Delete mock source files
5. Run `dotnet build` to verify compilation
6. Run `dotnet test` and `npm run test:e2e`
7. Fix any test failures

## Key Files
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/MockServiceExtensions.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockFleeceIssueTransitionService.cs` (delete)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockIssuePrLinkingService.cs` (delete)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockFleeceService.cs` (delete if no consumers)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceIssueTransitionService.cs` (real)
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/GitHub/IssuePrLinkingService.cs` (real)

title: Initialize temp folders as git repos and remove MockCommandRunner
description:
Update the temp folder initialization to create real git repositories, then replace MockCommandRunner with the real CommandRunner.

## Prerequisites
This depends on issues 1 and 2 being complete (series execution ensures this).

## Git initialization
- Update `TempDataFolderService` or `FleeceIssueSeeder.CreateMinimalProjectStructure()` to:
  1. Run `git init` in each temp project folder
  2. Configure git user: `git config user.name 'Test'` and `git config user.email 'test@test.com'`
  3. Add all seeded files and create an initial commit
  4. This makes the temp folders real git repos that support git operations

## MockCommandRunner removal
- Real `CommandRunner` (Features/Commands/CommandRunner.cs) executes shell commands via `System.Diagnostics.Process`
- With git-initialized temp folders, git commands will work against real repos
- `gh` commands won't work but are only used by separately-mocked GitHub services (kept)
- `claude` commands won't work but are only used by separately-mocked session services (kept)
- Remove MockCommandRunner registration, register real CommandRunner
- Delete mock file

## Steps
1. Read `TempDataFolderService.cs` and `FleeceIssueSeeder.cs` to understand current setup
2. Add git init + initial commit after folder/file creation
3. Read `MockCommandRunner.cs` to understand what it stubs
4. Read real `CommandRunner.cs` to verify it will work
5. Swap registration in `MockServiceExtensions.cs`
6. Delete mock file

title: Fix status race condition
description:
# Fix: Session status race condition after plan approval with keepContext=false

## Context

When a plan is approved with `keepContext=false`, the UI reports `waitingForInput` instead of `working/running` during the build session execution. This is caused by a race condition between two concurrent `SendMessageAsync` invocations in `ClaudeSessionService`.

**Logs showing the issue:**
- Plan session `2d144684` enters `input-required` (plan pending)
- User approves plan → new Build session `46dc848b` created
- New session transitions `submitted → working` and actively produces output
- But UI shows "waitingForInput" instead of "running"

## Root Cause

In `ClaudeSessionService.SendMessageAsync` (lines 413-420), the turn ID is set **after** the status broadcast:

```csharp
session.Status = ClaudeSessionStatus.Running;                    // line 413
await _hubContext.BroadcastSessionStatusChanged(...);            // line 414 ← await yields thread
var turnId = Guid.NewGuid();                                     // line 419
_currentMessageTurnIds[sessionId] = turnId;                      // line 420
```

During the `await` at line 414, the first `SendMessageAsync` (old plan session) can resume on a thread pool thread. Its post-loop guard (line 518-524) checks:
- `session.Status == Running` → TRUE (just set by the second call at line 413)
- `currentTurn == turnId` → TRUE (`turnId_B` hasn't been written yet, dictionary still has `turnId_A`)

So it erroneously sets `session.Status = WaitingForInput`. The second `SendMessageAsync` then starts with status `WaitingForInput`. When the build session eventually completes, the post-loop guard checks `session.Status == Running` → FALSE → skips the transition. Status stays `WaitingForInput` for the entire build session.

## Fix

**File:** `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/ClaudeSessionService.cs`

Move the turn ID assignment (lines 419-420) to **before** the status broadcast (line 413). This ensures `turnId_B` is written before any `await` point, closing the race window.

```csharp
// BEFORE (buggy):
session.Status = ClaudeSessionStatus.Running;
await _hubContext.BroadcastSessionStatusChanged(sessionId, session.Status);  // ← race window
var turnId = Guid.NewGuid();
_currentMessageTurnIds[sessionId] = turnId;

// AFTER (fixed):
var turnId = Guid.NewGuid();
_currentMessageTurnIds[sessionId] = turnId;
session.Status = ClaudeSessionStatus.Running;
await _hubContext.BroadcastSessionStatusChanged(sessionId, session.Status);  // ← no race: turnId_B already set
```

This is safe because:
- `HandlePlanPendingFromWorkerAsync` and `HandleQuestionPendingFromWorkerAsync` use the `turnId` from `MessageProcessingContext` (captured locally), not a fresh read from `_currentMessageTurnIds`
- The post-loop guards read `_currentMessageTurnIds` only at the end of their invocation
- No other code depends on turnId being set after the broadcast

## Test Plan

**File:** `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/ClaudeCode/ClaudeSessionServiceTests.cs`

Add a test to the `ClaudeSessionServiceTurnIdTests` fixture that reproduces the race condition using a delayed async enumerable:

1. Add helper `CreateDelayedSdkMessageStream(SdkMessage[] immediate, Task<SdkMessage> delayed)` that yields immediate messages then awaits the delayed task before yielding a final result
2. Add test `ApprovePlan_ClearContext_WithSlowFirstStream_StatusIsRunningDuringBuildSession`:
   - Start a Plan session, send message that triggers `plan_pending` + blocks on a `TaskCompletionSource`
   - Call `ApprovePlanAsync(keepContext: false)` which starts the second `SendMessageAsync`
   - The second stream setup releases the first stream's TCS (simulating the worker producing a late result)
   - Assert session status is `WaitingForInput` (correct final state after build completes)
   - Key: without the fix, the first stream's post-loop guard would set `WaitingForInput` early, and the second stream's guard would fail to set it (because status is already `WaitingForInput`, not `Running`)

## Verification

```bash
# Run backend tests
dotnet test tests/Homespun.Tests

# Run all checks
cd src/Homespun.Web && npm run lint:fix && npm run typecheck && npm test
```

## Fleece Issue

After implementation, create a fleece issue to track this fix.

title: Fix display when there are no issues
description: When there are no issues present in the task graph, the web app displays an error page. Instead of this, show a large central placeholder in the same location that says "No issues are currently open" with a button that says "Create an issue"). This should create an issue. Clicking the create buttons in the toolbar doesn't do anything as the application doesn't show a task graph that can be added to.

title: Stop new branch ID generator from overwriting issue information when issue is first created
description: When a new issue is created and I am navigated to the edit screen, the branch ID is generated in the background. If it successfully generated but I have entered some information into the description, when the branch ID is put into the issue details the information in the description is cleared. Fix this such that the auto branch ID generation only updates the branch ID. Note that the branch ID auto generation button in the edit window does not do this.

title: Update to Fleece.Core v1.7.2 to include multi-parent rendering
description:
v1.7.2: https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/releases/tag/v1.7.2

v1.7.2 of the Fleece.Core API (https://nick-boey.github.io/Fleece/api/) adds a feature to the graph and graph layout services that shows issues with multiple parents in every location that the parent can be found. Child issues of this parent, however, are only shown in the first instance that it is found.

Updating to this version of the API should automatically result in issues with multiple parents (i.e. a DAG) shown multiple times. Add issues with multiple parents to the mock Fleece file that is used to create for mocking the application and add tests to confirm that they are shown multiple times.

Add the (1/3), (2/3) badge indicator that shows that the issue being shown has multiple parents (if there is only one parent, show nothing). When this badge is clicked, the first issue (1/3) should be selected as it is the one that will contain the child issues of this multi-parent issue.

Add a visual indicator for multi-parent issues nodes in the graph display. This should be a short diagonal line going down to the right from the issue node for the first multi-parent issue, one going up and to the left for the last multi-parent issue rendered and one going in both directions for all others, indicating that they form a collection). This should be approximately the length to reach the top/bottom of the row, and should fade to opacity = 0 at the ends. Do this for both the SVG and Konva renderers.

title: Add graph view options
Consolidate run panel into single modal
description:
Consolidate the two separate agent run modals (AgentLauncherDialog and IssuesAgentDialog) into a single tabbed RunAgentDialog component. This also incorporates the sizing fix (NF8rVk) and navigation fix (5Qt2EF).

## New Component: RunAgentDialog

Create `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/agents/components/run-agent-dialog.tsx` with:

### Architecture
```
RunAgentDialog (Dialog shell + responsive sizing + Tabs)
├── Tabs (line variant, horizontal)
│   ├── TabsTrigger "Task Agent"
│   └── TabsTrigger "Issues Agent"
├── TabsContent "task" → TaskAgentTabContent (internal component)
└── TabsContent "issues" → IssuesAgentTabContent (internal component)
```

### Props
- `open`, `onOpenChange` - dialog state
- `projectId` - required
- `issueId?` - pre-selects task tab with this issue
- `selectedIssueId?` - passed to issues agent tab
- `defaultTab?` - 'task' | 'issues' (defaults based on issueId presence)
- `onAgentStart?`, `onSessionCreated?`, `onError?` - callbacks

### Sizing (NF8rVk)
DialogContent: `w-[80vw] sm:w-[60vw] max-h-[80vh] overflow-hidden flex flex-col`
Tab content area: `flex-1 overflow-y-auto`

### Prompt-to-Input Pattern (both tabs)
1. Prompt dropdown at top - selecting a prompt copies its initialMessage (with placeholders replaced client-side) into an editable textarea
2. User can edit the textarea freely
3. On Start: textarea content sent as `userInstructions` to the API
4. If "None" prompt selected, textarea starts empty
5. Client-side placeholder replacement: `{{title}}`, `{{id}}`, `{{description}}`, `{{branch}}`, `{{type}}`, `{{context}}`

### Navigation Logic (5Qt2EF)
- Task agent tab: Close dialog on 202 Accepted, no navigation (unchanged)
- Issues agent tab with prompt/instructions: close modal, stay on page
- Issues agent tab without prompt: navigate to session page

### State Management
- Each tab manages own state independently (prompt, model, instructions, base branch)
- Only shared state is activeTab at dialog level
- Keep existing localStorage keys for backward compatibility

### Hooks to Reuse
- Task tab: `useRunAgent()`, `useAgentPrompts()`, `useProject()`
- Issues tab: `useCreateIssuesAgentSession()`, `useIssueAgentAvailablePrompts()`

### Mount Point Changes
- `routes/projects.$projectId.issues.index.tsx`: Replace both dialogs with single RunAgentDialog
- `routes/projects.$projectId.issues.$issueId.edit.tsx`: Replace AgentLauncherDialog with RunAgentDialog

### Cleanup
- Delete `agent-launcher-dialog.tsx` and its tests
- Delete `issues-agent-dialog.tsx` and its tests
- Update index/export files

### TDD Tests (run-agent-dialog.test.tsx)
- Renders nothing when closed
- Renders dialog with tabs when open
- Defaults to task tab when issueId provided
- Defaults to issues tab when no issueId
- Task tab: shows prompt dropdown, model dropdown, start button
- Task tab: calls run agent endpoint on start with userInstructions
- Task tab: shows conflict state / advanced settings
- Issues tab: shows prompt dropdown, model, instructions textarea
- Issues tab: stays on page when prompt is selected
- Issues tab: navigates to session when no prompt
- Dialog sizing uses responsive width classes
- Content scrolls when overflowing
- Tab switching preserves state
- Prompt selection populates textarea with rendered template text

### Verification
1. `npm run lint:fix` - no errors
2. `npm run typecheck` - no errors
3. `npm test` - all tests pass
4. `npm run test:e2e` - all e2e tests pass

title: Review architecture diagrams
Add curved edges
description:
When edges have 90-degree bends (at junctions between horizontal and vertical segments), show the elbows as smooth curves instead of sharp corners. The curve radius should be approximately half the height of a row (~20px, i.e. ROW_HEIGHT / 2).

Current state: In use-edge-paths.ts, edge paths are computed as arrays of straight line segment points. 90-degree corners are approximated with a single intermediate point (e.g. lines 101-109 for lane-0 connectors, lines 162-175 for parallel connectors, lines 212-229 for series connectors). These render as angular joints despite lineCap='round' and lineJoin='round'.

Fix approach:
1. In use-edge-paths.ts, replace each 90-degree corner with a series of points approximating a quarter-circle arc
2. The arc radius should be min(ROW_HEIGHT / 2, available_space) to avoid overshooting
3. Use ~8-12 intermediate points per arc for smooth rendering
4. Affected edge types:
   a. Lane 0 connectors (last connector arc at lines 94-109)
   b. Parallel connectors (first child arc at lines 162-175)
   c. Series connectors (L-shaped junction at lines 206-229)

Files to modify:
- src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/use-edge-paths.ts — Add arc point generation helper function and apply to all corner points

Tests:
- Verify visually that all edge corners are smooth curves
- Run existing task-graph tests to ensure no regressions
- Test with various graph depths and lane configurations

title: Make graph view fill available screen space
description:
The Konva graph view does not fill the available screen space. It renders much smaller than the window.

## Context
