- Container sets `style={{ height: viewportSize.height }}` which can override flex layout
- Parent route wraps in `<div className="min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto p-4">` with `className="h-full"` on the component
- The ResizeObserver should pick up the correct height but may not be measuring correctly

## Implementation
1. In `task-graph-konva-view.tsx`, ensure the container div properly fills its flex parent
2. Remove or fix the explicit height style that overrides flex layout — use CSS flex/grid to fill space instead
3. Verify ResizeObserver picks up the correct dimensions when the container is in a flex layout
4. Check that the Stage dimensions match the container dimensions
5. Test at various viewport sizes to ensure responsive behavior

## Files
- `task-graph-konva-view.tsx`
- Possibly `projects.$projectId.issues.index.tsx` (parent route layout)

title: Fix scrolling and panning on mobile devices
description:
Scrolling doesn't work on mobile devices — the page scrolls instead of panning the graph. May also affect desktop scroll behavior.

## Context
- `onWheel` handler only captures mouse wheel events, not touch events
- The Konva Stage `draggable` prop partially works for touch but the outer div may intercept touch events
- Page scrolling within the graph area needs to be prevented on touch devices

## Implementation
1. In `task-graph-konva-view.tsx` and `use-camera.ts`, add touch event handlers (touchstart/touchmove/touchend) for panning
2. Prevent default page scrolling when touch events occur within the graph area
3. Implement pinch-to-zoom for mobile if zoom is supported
4. Ensure the Konva Stage `draggable` prop works correctly with touch events, or implement custom touch drag
5. Verify desktop scroll/wheel behavior still works correctly
6. Test on mobile viewport sizes

## Files
- `task-graph-konva-view.tsx`
- `use-camera.ts`

title: Add diagonal edges for multi-parent issues
description:
When an issue has multiple parents, only the primary parent connection is rendered. Additional parent relationships have no visual representation. Add diagonal lines to indicate secondary parent connections.

Visual spec:
- Render a diagonal line from the node toward the approximate direction of each additional parent
- Lines should be dotted/dashed (use Konva Line dash property)
- Lines should have fading opacity at the far end (start at ~0.4, fade to ~0.1)
- Similar visual weight to the hidden parent indicator dots (KonvaHiddenParentIndicator)

Implementation approach:
1. In task-graph-layout.ts, ensure all parentIssues are exposed on the render line (currently only parentIssues[0] is used for edge computation)
2. In use-edge-paths.ts, for each render line with parentIssues.length > 1, compute diagonal edge paths for secondary parents
3. In konva-nodes.tsx or a new component, render these diagonal edges with dash=[4,4] and opacity gradient
4. Direction of diagonal: if secondary parent's row is above, angle upward; compute approximate angle based on row/lane difference

Files to modify:
- src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/services/task-graph-layout.ts — Ensure multi-parent info is available on render lines
- src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/use-edge-paths.ts — Add diagonal edge computation for secondary parents
- src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/konva-nodes.tsx — Render diagonal edges with dash and opacity

Tests:
- Create test issues with multiple parents and verify diagonal edges appear
- Verify primary parent edge is unchanged
- Visual verification that dotted diagonal lines with fading opacity render correctly

title: Shift rows down when issue details expanded
description:
When an issue is double-clicked and its details panel expands, all rows below overlap instead of shifting down.

## Context
- Double-clicking an issue shows details beneath it
- Currently expansion uses absolute positioning with `marginTop: ROW_HEIGHT` which causes overlap
- The SVG renderer handles this correctly by recalculating positions
- Both Konva nodes/edges and HTML overlays need offset recalculation

## Implementation
1. Compute per-row Y offsets that account for expanded rows — each expanded row adds extra height
2. Apply the adjusted Y offsets to Konva node positions in `konva-nodes.tsx`
3. Apply the adjusted Y offsets to edge path computations in `use-edge-paths.ts`
4. Apply the adjusted Y offsets to HTML overlay positions in the view component
5. Ensure expanding/collapsing smoothly repositions all elements
6. Test with multiple expanded rows simultaneously

## Files
- `task-graph-konva-view.tsx` — main layout logic and HTML overlay positioning
- `use-edge-paths.ts` — edge path computation needs expanded-row offsets
- `konva-nodes.tsx` — node Y positions need adjustment

title: Shift rows down when issue details are expanded
description:
When an issue is double-clicked, the detail panel expands beneath it but overlaps all issues below. All content below an expanded issue should shift down to make space, similar to the SVG renderer behavior.

Root cause: HTML overlay rows are positioned with absolute positioning (top: rowIndex * ROW_HEIGHT). When a detail panel expands, it uses marginTop: ROW_HEIGHT but doesn't affect the positioning of subsequent rows. The Konva Stage nodes and edges also use fixed row-based positioning.

Fix approach:
1. Compute cumulative Y offsets for each row, accounting for expanded detail panels
2. When an issue is expanded, all rows below it need their Y position increased by the height of the detail panel
3. Apply these offsets to:
   a. HTML overlay div positions (the absolute top: value for each row)
   b. Konva node positions (cy calculations in KonvaIssueNode)
   c. Edge path Y coordinates (all computations in use-edge-paths.ts)
4. The content size calculation must also account for total expansion height
5. Measure the actual height of the InlineIssueDetailRow after render (may need a ref + ResizeObserver or a fixed estimated height)

Files to modify:
- src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/task-graph-konva-view.tsx — Compute row Y offsets, pass to children, update content size

title: Fix konvajs-content div not filling full screen
description:
## Problem
The container div with class `konvajs-content` is constrained to 800x600 pixels despite the ResizeObserver updating `viewportSize`. The root cause is that the container div at `task-graph-konva-view.tsx:689-699` uses `style={{ height: viewportSize.height }}` which initializes to `DEFAULT_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT = 600`. The container is inside a `<div className="min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-auto p-4">` (in the route file at line 415) with `className="h-full"` on the KonvaView.

## Fix
1. Remove the explicit `style={{ height: viewportSize.height }}` from the container div (line 699)
2. Use CSS (`h-full` or `flex-1`) to make the container fill its parent naturally
3. Let the ResizeObserver pick up the natural height from the CSS layout
4. Ensure the `DEFAULT_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT` fallback only applies when `container.clientHeight` is 0

## Files
- `task-graph-konva-view.tsx` (lines 75, 109-112, 225-242, 699)

title: Fix issue details panel height and gap
description:
## Problem
When issue details are expanded, the panel doesn't fully display because the height is variable/animated with `max-height: 500px`. Also there's a 40px gap between the issue row and the details panel caused by `marginTop: ROW_HEIGHT` on the containing div (line 845).

## Fix
1. Set the details panel to a fixed height of 700px (instead of relying on max-height animation)
2. Remove the `marginTop: ROW_HEIGHT` from the expanded details container div (line 845) to eliminate the gap
3. Ensure the Y-position offset for rows below the expanded panel accounts for exactly 700px
4. Update any related edge path calculations to account for the fixed 700px panel height

## Files
- `task-graph-konva-view.tsx` (line 845)
- `inline-issue-detail-row.tsx`
- `index.css` (expand animation)

title: Fix multi-parent diagonal line rendering on all instances
description:
## Problem
A dashed diagonal line was added between issues that have multiple parents, but it only appears on the first instance of the issue. There was already a diagonal line with a faded end (using stepped opacity segments) which is the correct visual approach. The dashed line should be removed and replaced with the faded-end diagonal line on ALL instances.

## Current Implementation
The `KonvaMultiParentIndicator` component in `konva-nodes.tsx` (added in commit 708227db) renders diagonal lines with stepped opacity segments (3 segments with decreasing opacity from 0.5 to ~0.17). The component uses `multiParentIndex` and `multiParentTotal` from the render line to determine which diagonals to show:
- First instance (`multiParentIndex === 0`): down-right diagonal only
- Last instance (`multiParentIndex === total - 1`): up-left diagonal only  
- Middle instances: both diagonals

The `computeLayout()` function in `task-graph-layout.ts` computes `multiParentIndex`/`multiParentTotal` via post-processing that counts duplicate issue IDs in the render line array.

## Fix
1. Verify the `multiParentIndex`/`multiParentTotal` computation assigns values to ALL instances (not just the first)
2. Remove any dashed diagonal line variant if present — only the faded-segment style (`KonvaMultiParentIndicator`) should be used
3. Ensure the faded diagonal renders on every instance of a multi-parent issue, matching the visual style of the three-dotted vertical hidden parent indicator
4. Test with 2-parent and 3-parent scenarios

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/konva-nodes.tsx` (`KonvaMultiParentIndicator`)
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-svg.tsx` (`MultiParentIndicator`)
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/services/task-graph-layout.ts` (multiParentIndex/multiParentTotal post-processing)

## Tests
- Create test with 2-parent issue: verify both instances get diagonal indicators
- Create test with 3-parent issue: verify first gets down-right, middle gets both, last gets up-left
- Run existing tests: `npm test -- konva-nodes` and `npm test -- task-graph-layout`

title: Fix new issue creation positioning and add virtual node
description:
## Problem
When new issues are added with the 'o' key, the input appears two rows below the selected issue instead of one row below. Similarly for 'Shift+O' (above). There is no visual preview node on the Konva canvas showing where the issue will be placed in the graph.

## Current Implementation
- 'o' key triggers `handleCreateBelow()` which sets `insertAtIndex = selectedIndex + 1` and creates a `PendingNewIssue` with `isAbove: false`
- 'Shift+O' triggers `handleCreateAbove()` with `isAbove: true`
- The inline editor is rendered via conditional `shouldInsertAbove`/`shouldInsertBelow` checks in the HTML overlay loop (`task-graph-konva-view.tsx:746-888`)
- The editor is inserted with `marginTop: ROW_HEIGHT` for below-insertion (line 859)
- Tab sets `pendingChildId` (new issue becomes parent), Shift+Tab sets `pendingParentId` (new issue becomes child)

## Fix
1. Fix positioning so the inline editor appears exactly 1 row below (for 'o') or 1 row above (for 'Shift+O') the selected issue, with no extra gap
2. Other issues below the insertion point should shift down to make space (similar to how the detail panel expansion works)
3. Add a virtual Konva issue node (circle) at the canvas position where the new issue will be created:
   - Default: same lane as the reference issue
   - When Tab is pressed (pendingChildId set): virtual node moves to the lane where a parent would be positioned
   - When Shift+Tab is pressed (pendingParentId set): virtual node moves to the lane where a child would be positioned
4. The virtual node should use a Task-colored circle with reduced opacity to indicate it's a preview
5. Create tests proving that the graph layout with the virtual node matches the layout after the issue is actually created — the graph should not jump or rearrange

## Implementation Notes
- Refer to the existing `computeInheritedParentInfo()` for determining the parent/child relationship
- The virtual node lane computation should use the same lane logic as `computeLayout()`
- Edge paths should also render for the virtual node (connecting it to its parent/sibling)
- The existing issue `zwE7WW` covers edge display during creation — this issue covers node positioning and virtual node rendering

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/task-graph-konva-view.tsx` (lines 298-368, 746-796, 857-888)
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/konva-nodes.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/use-edge-paths.ts`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/services/task-graph-layout.ts`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/types.ts` (PendingNewIssue interface)

## Tests
- Test that 'o' positions editor exactly 1 row below selected issue
- Test that 'Shift+O' positions editor exactly 1 row above selected issue
- Test virtual node renders at correct lane for default, Tab, and Shift+Tab states
- Test graph layout stability: virtual node layout matches post-creation layout
- Run existing tests: `npm test -- task-graph-konva-view` and `npm test -- use-edge-paths`

title: Fix sibling edges when parent is not visible
description:
## Problem
When a parent issue has two children in series execution mode, but the parent issue is not visible (hidden by depth filter), the solid vertical line connecting the two siblings is missing. The second child correctly shows the dotted hidden-parent indicator, but there should also be a solid vertical line between the first and second child indicating they are siblings.

## Expected Behavior
- Edges should be computed as if all layers are visible
- Then edges to issues that are not visible should be converted to the dotted line form (hidden parent indicator)
- The solid vertical sibling connection between series siblings should be preserved even when the parent is hidden

## Current Implementation
The `drawTopLine`/`drawBottomLine` logic in `task-graph-layout.ts` (lines 531-610) already handles hidden parent siblings:
- Lines 568-581: Check for series siblings with same hidden parent to set `drawTopLine`
- Lines 592-609: Check for next sibling with same hidden parent to set `drawBottomLine`
- The `hiddenParentByNode` map tracks which nodes have hidden parents

The edge rendering in `use-edge-paths.ts` renders top/bottom lines based on these flags:
- Lines 232-246: `drawTopLine` renders vertical from row top to node top
- Lines 248-263: `drawBottomLine` renders vertical from node bottom to row bottom

## Investigation Steps
1. Verify that `drawTopLine` and `drawBottomLine` are correctly set for both children when parent is hidden (add test case with 2 children, parent hidden)
2. Check if the issue is that the children are not adjacent in the render line array (there might be other issues between them)
3. Verify `hiddenParentByNode` correctly maps both children to the same hidden parent
4. If the flags are correct, check if `use-edge-paths.ts` renders the vertical connection correctly

## Fix Approach
The core principle: compute edges assuming all layers visible, then convert edges to hidden issues into the indicator form. This means:
1. Both series children should have `drawTopLine`/`drawBottomLine` set to create the solid vertical connection between them
2. Only the hidden-parent indicator should use the dotted form (already handled by `KonvaHiddenParentIndicator`)
3. The solid sibling line should remain solid regardless of parent visibility

## Files
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/services/task-graph-layout.ts` (lines 520-610, `computeHiddenParentInfo`)
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/use-edge-paths.ts`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/task-graph-konva/konva-nodes.tsx`

## Tests
- Test case: parent with 2 children in series mode, parent filtered out — verify both children get drawTopLine/drawBottomLine
- Test case: parent with 3 children in series mode, parent filtered out — verify continuous vertical line through all children
- Verify hidden parent indicator still shows on appropriate child
- Run existing tests: `npm test -- task-graph-layout` and `npm test -- use-edge-paths`

title: Fix TOC anchor links for HashRouter compatibility
description:
## Problem

HashRouter uses the URL hash for routing (e.g. `/#/docs/usage`), so TOC anchor links (`href="#heading-id"`) replace the route hash and break navigation instead of scrolling to the heading.

## Solution

In `docs-site/src/components/TableOfContents.tsx`:
- Add an `onClick` handler to each TOC link that calls `e.preventDefault()` then `document.getElementById(id)?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' })`
- Keep the `href` attribute for accessibility

## Tests

In `docs-site/src/components/TableOfContents.test.tsx`:
- Add test: clicking a TOC link calls `scrollIntoView` on the target element
- Add test: clicking a TOC link calls `preventDefault`

## Verification
```bash
cd docs-site && npm test
```

title: Fix issues with global and local prompts
Create Aspire AppHost and ServiceDefaults projects
description:
Create the two foundational Aspire projects and wire them into the solution.

## Steps

1. **Add Aspire packages** to the solution:
   - Create `src/Homespun.AppHost/Homespun.AppHost.csproj` — the orchestration project
   - Create `src/Homespun.ServiceDefaults/Homespun.ServiceDefaults.csproj` — shared service configuration
   - Add both projects to `Homespun.sln`

2. **AppHost Program.cs** — Define the distributed application topology:
   ```csharp
   var builder = DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

   var server = builder.AddProject<Projects.Homespun_Server>("server");

   var worker = builder.AddNpmApp("worker", "../Homespun.Worker", "dev")
       .WithHttpEndpoint(port: 3001, env: "PORT");

   var web = builder.AddNpmApp("web", "../Homespun.Web", "dev")
       .WithHttpEndpoint(port: 5173)
       .WithReference(server);

   server.WithReference(worker);

   builder.Build().Run();
   ```
   Adjust ports and environment variable names to match current configuration.

3. **ServiceDefaults** — Add shared configuration:
   - OpenTelemetry (traces, metrics, logs) configured to export to Aspire dashboard
   - Health check defaults (the server already has `/health`)
   - HTTP client resilience defaults

4. **Wire ServiceDefaults into Homespun.Server**:
   - Add project reference to ServiceDefaults
   - Call `builder.AddServiceDefaults()` in Program.cs
   - Call `app.MapDefaultEndpoints()` for health checks

5. **Verify**:
   - `dotnet run --project src/Homespun.AppHost` starts server, worker, and web
   - Aspire dashboard is accessible at the default URL
   - All three services are visible in the dashboard
   - Existing `dotnet run --project src/Homespun.Server` still works standalone
   - All existing tests pass (`dotnet test`, `npm test`)

title: Add OpenTelemetry observability via Aspire ServiceDefaults
description:
Enhance Homespun.Server with OpenTelemetry instrumentation provided by Aspire ServiceDefaults, so that traces, metrics, and structured logs appear in the Aspire dashboard during development.

## Prerequisites
- Aspire AppHost and ServiceDefaults projects exist (parent sibling issue)

## Steps

1. **Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to Homespun.Server**:
   - ASP.NET Core instrumentation (HTTP requests)
   - SignalR instrumentation (hub invocations)
   - HttpClient instrumentation (outgoing calls to GitHub API, worker sidecar)
   - Custom activity sources for key operations: agent orchestration, git clone, fleece sync

2. **Add structured logging integration**:
   - The server already uses JSON console logging for Promtail — ensure OpenTelemetry log exporter coexists
   - When running under Aspire, logs should appear in the dashboard
   - When running standalone or in Docker, existing Promtail/Loki pipeline is unaffected

3. **Add health check enhancements**:
   - The server has `/health` — enrich with component health checks:
     - Worker sidecar connectivity
     - Data directory writability
     - GitHub API reachability (optional, non-critical)

4. **Verify**:
   - Run via AppHost, make API calls, and confirm traces appear in Aspire dashboard
   - Confirm structured logs from SignalR hubs and feature services appear
   - Confirm health checks show in dashboard
   - Existing PLG stack (Loki/Promtail/Grafana) still works when running via docker-compose
   - All tests pass

title: Configure Aspire service discovery for inter-service communication
description:
Replace hardcoded service URLs with Aspire service discovery so services find each other automatically when running under the AppHost.

## Prerequisites
- Aspire AppHost project exists (parent sibling issue)

## Steps

1. **Server → Worker communication**:
   - Currently configured via MiniPrompt__SidecarUrl environment variable
   - Use Aspire's WithReference to inject the worker URL automatically
   - Fall back to env var when not running under Aspire (standalone/docker-compose)

2. **Web → Server communication**:
   - Currently configured via VITE_API_URL environment variable
   - Use Aspire's WithReference to inject the server URL for the React dev server proxy
   - Fall back to env var when not running under Aspire

3. **Environment variable passthrough**:
   - Ensure GITHUB_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, and other secrets are passed through from AppHost configuration
   - Use Aspire's WithEnvironment or parameter resources for secrets

4. **Verify**:
   - Run via AppHost — web can call server API, server can call worker
   - Run via docker-compose — all services communicate as before
   - Run server standalone — falls back to env vars gracefully
   - All tests pass

title: Add azd integration for Aspire-based ACA deployment
description:
Add Azure Developer CLI (azd) support so that the Aspire AppHost can deploy to Azure Container Apps with a single command.

## Prerequisites
- Aspire AppHost, ServiceDefaults, and service discovery are complete (parent sibling issues)

## Steps

1. **Initialize azd**:
   - Run azd init to generate azure.yaml and infra/ directory
   - Configure azure.yaml to reference the Aspire AppHost project
   - The AppHost topology (server, worker, web) drives the ACA resource generation

2. **Configure deployment parameters**:
   - Azure Container Registry for custom images (server, worker use custom Dockerfiles)
   - Environment variables and secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) as ACA secrets
   - Azure Files volume mount for persistent data (matching existing HOMESPUN_DATA_PATH pattern)
   - Container resource limits (memory: 4GB, CPU: 2.0 — matching existing Docker config)

3. **Handle custom container builds**:
   - Server and worker use multi-stage Dockerfiles with a shared base image
   - Configure azd to build from existing Dockerfiles rather than auto-generating
   - Ensure Dockerfile.base is built first as a dependency

4. **Relationship to existing ACA issues**:
   - This provides an alternative deployment path to the manual Bicep approach in issue xJ1xoN
   - The OIDC authentication work (vGFK6O) is orthogonal and can be layered on top
   - If both approaches are completed, document when to use each

5. **Verify**:
   - azd up deploys all services to ACA
   - Services can communicate within the ACA environment
   - Persistent storage is mounted correctly
   - azd down tears down resources cleanly
   - Document the deployment process in a deploy-aspire.md or similar

title: Backend - Refactor prompt system from ID-based to name+projectId composite key
description:
## Backend: Remove prompt ID, use name+projectId composite key

### Changes Required

#### 1. Model Changes (Homespun.Shared)
- Remove `Id` property from `AgentPrompt` model in `Homespun.Shared/Models/Sessions/AgentPrompt.cs`
- Ensure `Name` + `ProjectId` (nullable, null=global) serve as composite key
- Update any shared contracts/hub interfaces referencing prompt ID

#### 2. Data Store Changes (Homespun.Server)
- Update `JsonDataStore` prompt operations to use name+projectId as lookup key instead of ID
- Remove ID generation logic (currently a 6-char GUID slice in AgentPrompt constructor)
- Add duplicate name validation within same scope (global or per-project)
- Update all CRUD operations in the data store to use name-based lookups

#### 3. Service Layer Changes
- Update `AgentPromptService` methods to accept name+projectId instead of ID
- Add validation: reject duplicate names within same scope
- Names are immutable - no rename operation needed

#### 4. Controller/API Changes
- Update `AgentPromptsController` routes from `{id}` to `by-name/{name}?projectId=xxx`
- Update all endpoint signatures and route parameters
- Ensure proper URL encoding handling for prompt names

#### 5. Agent Execution Changes
- Update `AgentStartBackgroundService` which currently calls `agentPromptService.GetPrompt()` with a prompt ID to use name+projectId instead
- Update `AgentStartRequest` or equivalent DTOs to carry prompt name+projectId instead of prompt ID
- Verify `RenderTemplate` and prompt resolution flow still works with name-based lookup

#### 6. Backend Tests
- Write tests FIRST (TDD) for:
  - Name+projectId composite key lookups
  - Duplicate name rejection within same scope
  - API route changes (integration tests using `HomespunWebApplicationFactory`)
  - Agent execution with name-based prompts
- Update existing prompt tests to remove ID references

### Pre-completion Checks
```bash
dotnet test
```

title: Frontend - Update all components to name-based prompt identification
description:
## Frontend: Update all components from prompt ID to name-based identification

### Prerequisites
Backend sub-issue must be complete first (API routes have changed).

### Changes Required

#### 1. Regenerate API Client
```bash
cd src/Homespun.Web
npm run generate:api:fetch
```
This will update the generated types and client to reflect the new name-based API routes.

#### 2. Update Components
Search for all references to `prompt.id` or prompt ID usage in:
- `src/features/` - All feature components dealing with prompts
- `src/components/` - Any shared components
- `src/hooks/` - Custom hooks
- `src/stores/` - Zustand stores

Replace ID-based identification with name-based:
- Use `prompt.name` as the key/identifier
- Pass `projectId` alongside name where scope matters
- Update any React keys from `key={prompt.id}` to `key={prompt.name}`
- Update TanStack Query keys that include prompt IDs

#### 3. Update API Calls
- All prompt CRUD operations use new `by-name/{name}?projectId=xxx` routes
- Frontend always sends exact scope (projectId or null for global)
- No automatic resolution/fallback logic in frontend

#### 4. Update Frontend Tests
- Write tests FIRST (TDD) for updated component behavior
- Update existing tests to remove prompt ID references
- Verify prompt selection, editing, deletion all work with name-based keys

### Pre-completion Checks
```bash
cd src/Homespun.Web
npm run lint:fix
npm run format:check
npm run generate:api:fetch
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run test:e2e
```

title: Fix sizing of Konva renderer on desktop screens
description:
When rendered on desktop screens, the Konva renderer is now the full width of the screen, but the individual row width is still 800px.

The height is also limited to 600px on desktop, it should be the entire height to the bottom of the screen.

title: Fix issue with container build
description:
The docker container build is failing in the CD pipeline. Refer to logs here: https://github.com/nick-boey/Homespun/actions/runs/23722141654/job/69098876639

Diagnose the issue and put together a plan to fix it.

title: Use Fleece dependency tracking
description:
When an issue is moved to become a child of another issue, it starts with the ordering of '0'. When I then click on the buttons to move it up or down the list, additional characters "V" get added and removed from the end of the parent sort order reference. This is not correct - it should be using the Fleece.Core library which already exposes the DependencyService (https://nick-boey.github.io/Fleece/api/Fleece.Core.Services.DependencyService.html). When this is used to add and remove children (i.e. with the Fleece CLI, which is a wrapper around this library), when added as a child the issue should have the sort order 'nnn', and then when moved up and down there is logic to adjust this Lexical sort order. Please review the implementation of this feature. Ensure that the buttons in the UI are wired correctly to the DependencyService running on the server, and that the old code that created this functionality in the Fleece Server is deleted.

Version 1.7.2 of the Fleece.Core library should be used.

title: React Konva renderer is not full size
description: The React Konva task graph view remains the same 800x600 size instead of filling the screen. This has been the subject of recent PRs. However, when I switch between the SVG view type and the Konva viewer, the horizontal size of the Konva viewer becomes the right size. This may be an issue with resizing that may require a different fix than what has been tried with CSS and Tailwind thus far.

title: Placeholder replacements in agent run modal not working
description:
In the new unified agent run modal, when a prompt is selected from the list the placeholders are not being replaced with the values for the selected issue. Review and update this to ensure that the prompt that is put into the instructions input has all placeholders replaced. When the agent is started, the value in the instructions input is successfully being injected into the session, indicating that the issue is with the prompt placeholder replacement.

Starting with no prompt and then adding a prompt in the session window (where the chat is located) does successfully work - this should be used for the placeholder replacement logic.

title: Backend - Add name-based API routes for prompts
description:
## Overview
Refactor the AgentPromptsController to use name-based routes instead of ID-based routes for prompt CRUD operations.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add new name-based routes to AgentPromptsController
File: `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Controllers/AgentPromptsController.cs`

- Add `[HttpGet("by-name/{name}")]` endpoint that accepts optional `projectId` query parameter
  - If projectId provided: look up project prompt by name, fall back to global
  - If no projectId: look up global prompt by name
- Add `[HttpPut("by-name/{name}")]` for updating prompts by name+projectId
- Add `[HttpDelete("by-name/{name}")]` for deleting prompts by name+projectId
- Keep existing ID routes temporarily for backward compatibility, mark as `[Obsolete]`

### 2. Add name-based lookup to AgentPromptService
File: `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AgentPromptService.cs`

- Add `GetPromptByName(string name, string? projectId)` method
- Add name uniqueness validation in `CreatePromptAsync` - reject duplicate names within same scope (same projectId or both global)

### 3. Update IAgentPromptService interface
File: `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/IAgentPromptService.cs`

### 4. Update MockAgentPromptService
File: `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockAgentPromptService.cs`

### 5. Regenerate API client
```bash
cd src/Homespun.Web && npm run generate:api:fetch
```

## Testing
- Write unit tests for name-based lookup (exact match, scope precedence)
- Write unit tests for duplicate name rejection
- Write API integration tests for new routes
- Ensure existing ID-based tests still pass

title: Frontend - Migrate components from prompt ID to name-based lookups
description:
## Overview
Update all frontend components to use name-based prompt identification instead of ID-based lookups. This depends on the backend name-based routes being available.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Update API hooks to use name-based endpoints
Files:
- `src/features/prompts/hooks/use-remove-override.ts` - switch from `{id}/override` to `by-name/{name}`
- `src/features/prompts/hooks/use-create-override.ts` - use name+projectId instead of globalPromptId

### 2. Update agent launcher components
Files:
- `src/features/agents/components/agent-launcher.tsx` - change `selectedPromptId`/`effectivePromptId` to use prompt name
- `src/features/agents/components/agent-launcher-dialog.tsx` - same changes, update `promptId` in run params to prompt name
- `src/features/agents/hooks/use-run-agent.ts` - change `promptId` param to `promptName`

### 3. Update issues agent components
Files:
- `src/features/issues-agent/components/issues-agent-dialog.tsx` - migrate from promptId to prompt name
- `src/features/issues-agent/hooks/use-create-issues-agent-session.ts` - change `promptId` to `promptName`

### 4. Update prompt management components
Files:
- `src/features/prompts/components/prompts-list.tsx` - use name for delete/override operations
- `src/features/prompts/components/prompt-card.tsx` - pass name instead of id to callbacks
- `src/features/prompts/components/issue-agent-prompts-section.tsx` - same changes
- `src/features/prompts/components/prompts-code-editor.tsx` - update globalPromptIds to globalPromptNames
- `src/features/prompts/utils/prompt-diff.ts` - update diff logic to use names

### 5. Update session components
Files:
- `src/features/sessions/components/chat-input.tsx` - use name for prompt selection

### 6. Update workflow components
Files:
- `src/features/workflows/components/step-settings-card.tsx` - change promptId to promptName
- `src/features/workflows/components/workflow-editor.tsx` - same

### 7. Regenerate API client after backend changes
```bash
npm run generate:api:fetch
```

## Testing
- Update all existing component tests to use name-based identification
- Test that prompt selection, deletion, and override operations work with names
- Verify localStorage keys use prompt names instead of IDs

title: Fix issue agent launching
description:
Agents are being successfully launched but issue agents don't launch. Clones are successfuly created but even after adding more logging there is no indication that agent worker containers are being started. Please review and try to diagnose the issue - the wiring appears to be missing.

One interesting thing is that when I run the mock application with scripts/mock.sh I am able to start a mock agent.

Add logging to the issue agent start process, E2E tests and API integration tests to determine what is going wrong. If you find a fix in the process, implement the fix as well.

title: Make the agent run modal wider
description: The agent run modal window remains a small width and height. Please increase the size of it so that it is 80% of the screen width and height.

title: Add logging to issue agents startup
description: Issue agents are not starting when triggered by the client. The clone is being created but there is no indication the the worker container is being started. Add logging through the whole process so that I can diagnose the issue. There is logging for starting regular agents - this should be similar.

title: Fix blocking of pulls when there are non Fleece changes
description:
If there are changes to files other than those in the .fleece folder, when the pull button is clicked for a project it fails to update the default branch. This should only happen if there is a merge conflict. If there is no merge conflicts, the changes should just be pulled. If there is a merge conflict, a dialog should ask the user whether they want to rollback the changes not in the .fleece folder or abort the pull.

Currently there is no warning that the pull has failed.

title: Make workflow data part of fleece folder
description: Workflow data for projects is currently stored in the .workflows folder of the root registry folder. Instead, put these in .fleece/workflows. There is no need to include backwards compatibility with the original location.

title: Full site is not visible
description: On many pages of the site the bottom of the application is not visible. For example on the edit screen, the buttons are not visible even when scrolling down due to apparent clipping issues. This error appears to have been introduced in recent fixes attempting to fix the Konva viewer size.

It should confirm that the responses to the prompts are sent back and displayed in the session display. Update the `./scripts/mock.sh` so that it runs both the backend and frontend in the background and captures logging into a .gitignored file so that it can be used for agent context.

title: Fix Konva graph scrolling

title: Update Konva graph
Create UI in subframe
Replace e2e/ issue IDs with alphanumeric IDs
description:
In `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/MockDataSeederService.cs`, the `SeedIssuesAsync()` method (lines 544-628) creates 7 mock issues with slash-based IDs that break the UI when editing:

- `e2e/parent1` → `e2Prt1`
- `e2e/child1` → `e2Chd1`
- `e2e/child2` → `e2Chd2`
- `e2e/orphan` → `e2Orph`
- `e2e/series-parent` → `e2SPrt`
- `e2e/series-child1` → `e2SCh1`
- `e2e/series-child2` → `e2SCh2`

### Steps
1. Replace all 7 issue `Id` values with the new alphanumeric IDs above
2. Update all `ParentIssue` references in `ParentIssueRef` objects to use the new parent IDs:
   - `e2e/parent1` → `e2Prt1` (in child1 and child2)
   - `e2e/series-parent` → `e2SPrt` (in series-child1 and series-child2)
3. No E2E tests reference these IDs directly, so no test changes are needed
4. Run `dotnet build` to verify compilation

### Verification
- The mock server starts without errors
- Issues are visible in the task graph
- Clicking on any of these issues and editing them does not produce an error

title: Remove pre-seeded demo session and update E2E tests
description:
The `SeedDemoSessions()` method in `MockDataSeederService.cs` (lines 670-963) creates a hardcoded session `demo-session-001` for entity `task/abc123`. This session doesn't use the mock agent execution service, so prompts sent to it do nothing. Remove it and update affected E2E tests.

### Steps

#### 1. Remove `SeedDemoSessions()` method
In `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/MockDataSeederService.cs`:
- Delete the entire `SeedDemoSessions()` method (lines 670-963)
- In `SeedSessionsAsync()`, remove the fallback call to `SeedDemoSessions()` on line 109. When no JSONL files are found, just log a message and return (no sessions seeded)
- Remove the `_toolResultParser` field and constructor parameter since it's only used by `SeedDemoSessions()`
- Update `MockServiceExtensions.cs` if `IToolResultParser` registration was only for this purpose

#### 2. Update `mobile-chat-layout.spec.ts`
In `src/Homespun.Web/e2e/mobile-chat-layout.spec.ts`:
- Add a `beforeAll` or shared setup that creates a session via API:
  1. POST to `/api/issues/ISSUE-003/run` (or similar endpoint) to start a mock agent session
  2. Capture the session ID from the response
  3. Use that session ID in all `page.goto("/sessions/${sessionId}")` calls
- The session needs messages to test chat layout, so after creating it, send a prompt (e.g. `"tool"`) to generate tool use messages that the tests can verify against

#### 3. Update `mention-search.spec.ts`
In `src/Homespun.Web/e2e/mention-search.spec.ts` (around line 150-156):
- Similar to above: create a session via API before navigating to it
- This test only needs a session to exist with a chat input, so a simple session creation suffices

#### 4. Verify
- Run `npm run test:e2e` from `src/Homespun.Web` and ensure all tests pass
- Verify the sessions list page shows no pre-existing sessions on startup

title: Add echo fallback to mock agent responses
description:
In `MockAgentExecutionService`, when no keywords match, change the response to echo back the prompt.

### Steps

#### 1. Update `StartSessionAsync` in `MockAgentExecutionService.cs`
In `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Testing/Services/MockAgentExecutionService.cs` (lines 101-121):
- Change the no-keywords branch text from `"[Mock] Session started successfully."` to `$"You said '{request.Prompt}'"`

#### 2. Update `SendMessageAsync` 
The `SendMessageAsync` method (lines 148-190) always uses the channel-based flow via `EmitResponseSequence`. When no keywords are found, `EmitResponseSequence` (line 253-255) calls `EmitResultMessage` and completes. But it doesn't emit a text response.
- In `EmitResponseSequence`, before the final `EmitResultMessage` call (line 254), add a text message with `$"You said '{prompt}'"`
- This requires passing the original prompt string into `EmitResponseSequence` (currently it only receives keywords)

#### 3. Update unit tests
- Add/update tests to verify the echo behavior for both `StartSessionAsync` and `SendMessageAsync`
- Test that keyword prompts still produce keyword-specific responses (regression check)

### Verification
- Send a prompt like `"hello world"` to a mock session → response should be `"You said 'hello world'"`
- Send a prompt like `"think"` → should still produce thinking blocks (not echo)
- Run `dotnet test` to verify all backend tests pass

title: Update mock server data
description:
Update the mock server data to fix three issues:

## 1. Fix issue IDs with slashes
The mock issues seeded in `MockDataSeederService.SeedIssuesAsync()` use IDs with slashes (e.g. `e2e/parent1`, `e2e/series-child1`). These cause "Issue not found" errors when the frontend tries to edit them because the slash gets URL-encoded. Replace all 7 `e2e/*` IDs with 6-character alphanumeric IDs (e.g. `e2Prt1`, `e2Chd1`, etc.) and update all internal parent references.

## 2. Remove pre-seeded demo session
The `SeedDemoSessions()` method creates a hardcoded session (`demo-session-001`) for entity `task/abc123`. This session doesn't use the mock agent execution service, so prompts sent to it do nothing. Remove this seeded session. Update the two E2E test files that reference it (`mobile-chat-layout.spec.ts` and `mention-search.spec.ts`) to create their own sessions via API calls before navigating to them.

## 3. Add echo fallback to mock agent responses
In `MockAgentExecutionService`, when no keywords (think/tool/question/plan) match the prompt, the response is a generic "[Mock] Session started successfully." message. Change this to echo back the user's prompt: `You said '<prompt>'`. This applies to both `StartSessionAsync` (initial prompt) and `SendMessageAsync` (follow-up messages).

## Verification
- All existing E2E tests pass
- Mock issues with new IDs are editable in the UI
- Sending a non-keyword prompt to a mock session returns the echo response
- The sessions list page shows no pre-existing sessions on startup (when using fallback demo data path)
- Run the mock server with .\scripts\mock.sh and navigate the site with Playwright MCP to confirm that it still works.

title: Fix modal size bug
Fix run modal size bug
description:
When I open an agent run modal, it stays at the same fixed width and expands to a very large height on the screen if the prompt is long enough. Change this such that the width is 80% of the screen width and the height is 80% of the screen height. The instructions text box should have the same height regardless.

Run the mock application with ./scripts/mock.sh and test it using the Playwright MCP tools to ensure that this works correctly even with very large prompts.

title: Start agent button should always be visible in agent run modal
description:
At small screen sizes when the agent modal width is too small, the "start agent" button no longer fits on the modal and overflows off the right side. Instead of this, place the start button on another row below the selectors for selecting the prompt, mode and model for mobile screen sizes.

The width of the button should be the full width of the modal.

Verify this with ./scripts/mock.sh and the Playwright MCP tools.

title: Update Fleece.Core version to v2.0.0

The Fleece.Core library was recently updated to version 2.0.0. This version has consolidated the large number of services into a single service, so only one dependency will need to be injected for the majority of the Fleece issues interactions.

title: Add documentation for installation and usage
description:
# Add Documentation for Installation and Usage

Create comprehensive documentation for Homespun covering installation, multi-user configuration, usage guides, and troubleshooting. Also create a static React documentation site hosted on GitHub Pages that matches the web client's visual style (TailwindCSS v4, shadcn/ui, Zinc base color, dark theme).

title: Allow multiple selectable issues agent prompts
description:
## Overview

Allow multiple selectable predefined prompts for issue modification agents, mirroring how the normal agent prompting system works. Each prompt should be assignable to either Plan or Build mode.

Currently, issue agents use two fixed SessionType prompts (IssueAgentModification for the initial message template, IssueAgentSystem for the system prompt). This change makes the initial message prompts selectable from a list, similar to the agent launcher dialog's prompt dropdown.

## Current State

- Issue agent prompts are stored as two fixed `SessionType` entries: `IssueAgentModification` and `IssueAgentSystem`
- `IssuesAgentController.CreateSession()` fetches the single IssueAgentModification prompt and renders it
- Issue agents always run in Build mode
- The global prompts page shows issue agent prompts as a read-only/edit-only section
- The project prompts page does not show issue agent prompts at all

## Target State

- Multiple issue agent prompts can be created (each with Name, InitialMessage, Mode)
- The IssueAgentSystem prompt remains a single fixed system prompt
- Issue agent dialog includes a prompt selection dropdown (like the agent launcher dialog)
- Selected prompt's Mode determines whether the session runs in Plan or Build mode
- Global prompts page allows full CRUD for issue agent prompts
- Project prompts page supports overrides for issue agent prompts (same inheritance model as normal prompts)

## Implementation Approach

1. **Backend prompt model changes**: Repurpose or add a mechanism to distinguish multiple issue agent prompts from normal prompts. Options:
   - Add a new `PromptCategory` enum (Standard, IssueAgent) to `AgentPrompt`
   - Or use `SessionType.IssueAgentModification` for all selectable issue agent prompts (allowing multiples)
   - Keep `IssueAgentSystem` as the single system prompt

2. **Backend service changes**: Update `AgentPromptService` to support listing/filtering multiple issue agent prompts, analogous to `GetPromptsForProject()` but for issue agent prompts

3. **Backend API changes**: Update `IssuesAgentController.CreateSession()` to accept an optional `PromptId` and resolve the prompt + mode from it

4. **Frontend dialog changes**: Add prompt selector dropdown to `issues-agent-dialog.tsx` using the same pattern as `agent-launcher-dialog.tsx`

5. **Frontend prompts page changes**: Update global and project prompts pages to allow creating/editing/deleting issue agent prompts with full CRUD support

## Verification

- Multiple issue agent prompts can be created from the global prompts page
- Project-level overrides work for issue agent prompts
- Issue agent dialog shows prompt dropdown with available prompts
- Selecting a Plan-mode prompt creates a Plan session; Build-mode creates Build session
- Default prompts page loads correctly with the default IssueModify prompt
- Existing issue agent functionality continues to work when no prompt is explicitly selected

title: End-to-end ACA deployment verification and documentation
description:
Final verification that the complete ACA deployment works end-to-end and that local Docker deployment is unaffected.

Two sub-tasks:
1. Create deployment scripts and CI/CD workflow for ACA
2. Verify local Docker deployment passes all tests unchanged

Verification: ACA deployment script runs successfully. Local Docker deployment passes full test suite. Both deployment modes are documented.

title: Verify local Docker deployment is unaffected
description:
Final verification that all changes made in this initiative have not broken the existing local Docker Compose deployment.

Verification steps:

1. **Run full backend test suite:** `dotnet test` - all tests must pass
2. **Run frontend tests:** `cd src/Homespun.Web && npm test` - all tests must pass
3. **Run E2E tests:** `cd src/Homespun.Web && npm run test:e2e` - all tests must pass
4. **Run linting and type checks:**
   - `npm run lint:fix` - no errors
   - `npm run typecheck` - no errors
5. **Verify Docker Compose starts correctly:**
   - `docker-compose up` starts without errors
   - Server responds to health check
   - Frontend loads in browser
   - SignalR connections establish
   - Agent sessions can be started (DockerAgentExecutionService used)
6. **Verify no auth is required in local mode:**
   - All API endpoints accessible without tokens
   - SignalR hubs connect without tokens
   - No login page shown in frontend

This is a verification-only task. If any issues are found, create follow-up bug issues.

title: Add UI for modifying workflows
description:
Verify that all workflow modification UI features are complete. When all children are done:

1. Run npm run typecheck and npm run lint:fix with no errors
2. Run npm test to confirm all component and hook tests pass
3. Verify workflows can be created from blank and from templates
4. Verify workflow settings (title, description, timeout, continueOnFailure, enabled) can be edited
5. Verify trigger configuration works for all trigger types (Manual, Event, Scheduled, Webhook)
6. Verify enable/disable toggle works from the workflow list dropdown menu
7. Run npm run generate:api:fetch to ensure API client is up to date

title: Improve mock
description:
Parent issue for improving the mock server. Verifies that all sub-tasks are complete: unnecessary in-memory mocks removed and replaced with real implementations using temp folder infrastructure, sample-app project removed, seed data improved with realistic issues, and mock agent wired for workflows.

## Verification
- `dotnet test` - all backend tests pass
- `cd src/Homespun.Web && npm test` - frontend tests pass
- `cd src/Homespun.Web && npm run test:e2e` - E2E tests pass
- `npm run lint:fix && npm run format:check && npm run typecheck` - no errors
- Manual: start with `./scripts/mock.sh` + `npm run dev`, verify UI works end-to-end
- Confirm only 7 mock services remain: MockGitHubEnvironmentService, MockGitHubClientWrapper, MockGitHubService, MockPRStatusResolver, MockContainerQueryService, MockGitCloneService, MockFleeceIssuesSyncService

title: Documentation site improvements
description:
The content links on the right hand side of the page to navigate to headings don't work as the anchors don't exist on the rendered site. Update this.

Also, break up the content into smaller individual pages rather than just a single page containing the entire usage guide. Don't include any Fleece CLI tips, just note that there is a CLI and link to the Fleece GitHub page.

title: Add .NET Aspire as optional development and deployment orchestration
description:
Add .NET Aspire as an optional orchestration layer for local development and Azure Container Apps deployment. Aspire provides:

- **AppHost project** for orchestrating the ASP.NET server, Node.js worker, and React frontend during development
- **Service Defaults** for shared OpenTelemetry, health checks, and resilience configuration
- **Aspire Dashboard** for observability during development (traces, logs, metrics)
- **Azure Developer CLI (azd) integration** for streamlined ACA deployment

This is additive — the existing docker-compose.yml workflow must remain fully functional. Aspire is offered as an alternative for developers who prefer it and as a deployment path for Azure Container Apps.

Verification:
- All child issues complete
- `dotnet run --project src/Homespun.AppHost` starts all services and the Aspire dashboard is accessible
- Existing `docker-compose up` workflow is unaffected
- `azd up` deploys to ACA (if ACA deployment child is complete)
- All existing tests pass

title: Verify - End-to-end verification of name-based prompt system
description:
## Verification: Name-based prompt system is fully working

### Status: NOT COMPLETE

The name-based prompt system refactoring was NOT fully implemented. The frontend task (4yn0uT) was closed/abandoned, and the backend still uses ID-based routes.

### Remaining Work

1. **API routes still use ID-based pattern** - `/api/agent-prompts/{id}` routes remain. No `by-name/{name}` routes exist.
2. **Frontend still uses promptId extensively** - agent-launcher, issues-agent-dialog, and other components reference `promptId` and `prompt.id`
3. **Backend services use ID-based lookups** - `GetPrompt(id)`, `RemoveOverrideAsync(promptId)`, etc.

### What IS working
- All existing tests pass (dotnet: 2269, frontend: 2322)
- Lint and typecheck pass
- The prompt override system (isOverride, create/remove override) works with the current ID-based system

### Original Success Criteria (NOT MET)
- ~~No prompt ID references remain (except projectId)~~ - MANY remain
- ~~API uses name-based routes exclusively~~ - Still uses ID routes
- ~~Duplicate names rejected within same scope~~ - Not verified (no name-based routes)

title: Improve e2e testing
description: The children of this issue consist of tasks that were intended to change the mock server (run with ./scripts/mock.sh) to use local files instead of in-memory issues, and to add a mock agent that provides simple answers back to the server. This means that almost everything (except GitHub) could be tested in the e2e tests. Confirm that this has been implemented and is the case by inspecting the files created by the mock.sh and whether they are edited when the e2e tests are run.

title: UI fixes
Workflow Foundation (MVP)
description:
Foundation layer for workflow system. Creates the basic storage, execution, and MCP tool infrastructure.

## Deliverables
1. Workflow JSONL storage service (`.workflows/` directory)
2. Basic linear workflow execution engine
3. WorkflowComplete MCP tool in worker
4. Workflow context store for accumulated state
5. Basic REST API endpoints for workflow CRUD
6. Unit tests for storage and execution

title: Node Executors
description:
Implement all node type executors with their specific behaviors.

## Deliverables
1. AgentNodeExecutor - Run Claude agents with prompt templating
2. GateNodeExecutor - Human approval workflow
3. ActionNodeExecutor - Automated actions (PR, merge, notify)
4. TransformNodeExecutor - Data transformation
5. WorkflowPassData and WorkflowGetContext MCP tools

title: Verify Clones tab feature is complete
description:
## Verification Checklist

After all child tasks are complete, verify the following:

### Backend
- [ ] `EnrichedCloneInfo` model exists with all required fields
- [ ] `CloneEnrichmentService` correctly enriches clones with issue/PR data
- [ ] `GET /api/clones/enriched` endpoint returns enriched data
- [ ] `DELETE /api/clones/bulk` endpoint deletes multiple clones
- [ ] Service is registered in `Program.cs`
- [ ] All backend tests pass

### Frontend
- [ ] Clones tab appears in project page navigation
- [ ] Route `/projects/:projectId/clones` works
- [ ] Feature Clones display separately from Issues Agent Clones
- [ ] Linked issue shows title and status badge
- [ ] Linked PR shows number, title, and status badge
- [ ] Delete button appears only on deletable clones
- [ ] "Delete All Stale" button shows correct count
- [ ] Confirmation dialog appears before deletion
- [ ] Clones are deleted after confirmation
- [ ] Loading skeleton displays during fetch
- [ ] Error state displays on fetch failure
- [ ] Empty state displays when no clones
- [ ] All frontend tests pass

### E2E Tests
- [ ] All Playwright tests pass
- [ ] Navigation to Clones tab works
- [ ] Clone data displays correctly
- [ ] Delete functionality works

### Pre-PR Checks
- [ ] `dotnet test` passes
- [ ] `npm run lint:fix` passes
- [ ] `npm run format:check` passes
- [ ] `npm run generate:api:fetch` runs successfully
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes
- [ ] `npm run test:e2e` passes

### Manual Testing
- [ ] Create a project with clones
- [ ] Navigate to Clones tab
- [ ] Verify clones display with correct linked data
- [ ] Create a merged PR and verify its clone shows delete button
- [ ] Complete an issue and verify its clone shows delete button
- [ ] Test single clone deletion
- [ ] Test "Delete All Stale" functionality

title: Initialize with default prompts
description: When the application is deployed, if there are no prompts found it the default prompts should be added in - i.e. the prompts that are created when the "Restore defaults" button is pressed on the global "Prompts" page.
